docs/perf/codex-reviews/ab632c1.md:661:-**Why this milestone exists separately.** Every milestone M1–M10 has run iOS measurements, but each in service of its own feature. M10.5 is the dedicated *FFI surface* hardening pass — finding and fixing every shape of FFI bug that a non-social-domain consumer (M11 podcast app) would otherwise discover the hard way. This is also the milestone where we delete every shortcut and "TODO: revisit" comment in the FFI layer.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/ab632c1.md:679:-- **Zero open `TODO`/`FIXME`/`XXX`/`unimplemented!()`** in `crates/nmp-core/src/ffi.rs`, `crates/nmp-core/src/actor.rs`, `crates/nmp-core/src/relay.rs`, `crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/**`, and the iOS bridge sources (`ios/NmpStress/NmpStress/KernelBridge.swift`, `KernelModel.swift`). Hard zero — no deferral escape, no "tracking issue" carve-out. Every pre-existing one is resolved in M10.5. If something genuinely cannot be done in M10.5 because it belongs to a later milestone (e.g. NIP-65 outbox work), then it is not a TODO/FIXME in the scoped files — it lives as a milestone task in `docs/plan.md`, not as a code marker.

docs/decisions/0007-diagnostics-and-non-nostr-domain-data.md:189:Non-Nostr data must not be encoded as fake Nostr events. It enters one of four actor-owned lanes.
docs/decisions/0007-diagnostics-and-non-nostr-domain-data.md:198:The actor decides which lane a capability result belongs to. Native reports raw facts only; Rust derives policy.
docs/decisions/0007-diagnostics-and-non-nostr-domain-data.md:242:- A synthetic non-Nostr module test writes domain records, action-ledger rows, capability reports, and side effects, then verifies each crosses the correct bridge lane.

File size: 300 LOC soft, 500 hard.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:17:M2 goal (per docs/plan.md §M2): replace hardcoded relay set with a subscription compiler stage. Logical interests compile to per-relay plans. NIP-65 outbox routing default for reads + writes. Four diagnostic lanes (NIP-65 / hint / provenance / user-configured) never collapse.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:21:=== M2 design merge: 031fc07 ===
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:34:031fc07 design(m2): subscription compilation + outbox routing spec
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:44:  - subscription-compilation/compiler.md  4-stage pipeline + plan-id contract
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:55:                                          four-lane records (NIP-65 / Hint /
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:73:                                          (4) four-lane diagnostic distinctness
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:78:§7; extends ADR-0007 diagnostic records without collapsing the four lanes.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:95:+> **Companion docs:** `docs/plan.md` §M2; `docs/aim.md` §4.4–§4.5; `docs/design/ndk-applesauce-lessons.md` §7; ADR-0007 (diagnostic lanes); `docs/design/kernel-substrate.md` §3 (`ViewModule`) and §4 (`ActionModule`); `docs/product-spec/subsystems.md` §7.2–§7.3.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:101:+- [Compiler — pipeline + plan-id contract + function migration table](subscription-compilation/compiler.md) (§3)
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:103:+- [Diagnostics — four-lane records + reverse-coverage view](subscription-compilation/diagnostics.md) (§5, §8)
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:114:+| 3 | Compilation pipeline: authors → mailboxes → per-relay plans + plan-id | compiler.md |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:116:+| 5 | Four-lane diagnostic records (NIP-65 / hint / provenance / user-configured) | diagnostics.md |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:127:+1. **Plan-id stability under perturbation.** The intro/compiler picks "logical-interest set + author-mailbox snapshot ⇒ plan-id." That ties plan-id to mailbox membership, so a single new kind:10002 arrival reshuffles plan-ids for every interest including that author. An alternative scopes plan-id to the *logical-interest set only* and tracks per-relay assignment as a separate stable identifier. Pick one in an ADR; the test contracts in §9 assume the former.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:228:+2. **Read fallback.** For a `RoutingDirection::Outbox` interest whose author has no known mailboxes, the compiler routes the interest to the indexer set **as read-only fallback**. Per `docs/product-spec/subsystems.md` §7.3 line 99: "fall back to indexer set for reads only; do not publish to indexers." The `RoutingSource::Indexer` tag on the resulting `AuthorRouting` flows through to the diagnostic surface so the four-lane view (§5) can render "author X is being served by indexer Y because we have no mailbox for them."
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:292:+- **Recompilation with no change ⇒ same plan-id.** If `ingest_relay_list` (`crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/ingest.rs:218-221`) deduplicates and decides not to replace a stale mailbox, no plan-id churn.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:293:+- **Adding an interest changes plan-id even if no new wire REQ results.** Two interests can merge into the same SubShape; the plan-id changes because the *interest set* changed. The platform diagnostic correctly reports "logical-interest count went up; wire-sub count did not."
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:294:+- **A new kind:10002 for a covered author changes plan-id.** Even if the author's new write relays overlap entirely with the old set (e.g. relay added then removed), the snapshot's `created_at` advanced, so the hash changes. ADR-0007 diagnostics will reflect this; the wire-emitter's diff will be a no-op if the actual relay assignment is unchanged.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:295:+- **Indexer set change changes plan-id.** Operator config edits surface immediately.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:297:+The `plan_id` is stored on `CompiledPlan` and rendered into `LogicalInterestStatus` (extending the record at `crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/mod.rs:147-154` with `plan_id: String, plan_generation: u64`). Tests in §9 assert plan-id stability across no-op recompilations.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:305:+| `startup_requests` (requests.rs:50-106) | Becomes `register_startup_interests()`: pushes 4 `LogicalInterest`s into the registry (seed timeline; account kind:0; account kind:10002; seed contacts kind:3). The compiler runs once and produces wire REQs. The TEST_PUBKEY-specific bootstrap (line 71-82) becomes an `InterestScope::Global` interest with hardcoded indexer routing flagged `RoutingSource::UserConfigured` (it is operator-debug seed data). |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:346:+## 5.0 The four lanes
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:348:+Per `docs/design/ndk-applesauce-lessons.md` §4 (lines 39–46) and `docs/aim.md` §6 doctrine 10 ("provenance preserved"), the four relay-fact lanes are:
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:355:+Each lane is its own record stream. They never merge into a single "relays" field — that collapse is exactly the bug `docs/design/ndk-applesauce-lessons.md` §4 line 46 forbids. They may be displayed side-by-side in a diagnostic view; the actor stores them apart.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:357:+This is structurally enforced: there is no `Vec<RelayUrl>` field on any compiler output type. Every relay-bearing field carries a `lane: RelayFactLane` discriminator.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:360:+// crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/diagnostics/lanes.rs (proposed)
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:371:+## 5.1 Per-lane record schemas
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:373:+Each lane has one record type. All four are exposed to the platform via the existing ADR-0007 `ViewBatch` lane (low-cadence, coalesced to 1–4 Hz per ADR-0007 "How status crosses the bridge"). They feed into the diagnostics screen, not into normal product UI.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:396:+### Lane 2 — `HintRelayFact`
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:399:+pub struct HintRelayFact {
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:407:+    EventTag    { event_id: EventId, tag: TagKey, position: u8 },
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:413:+Emitted by the pointer loader (post-M2; for M2 the field exists but is rarely populated — only `e`/`a`-tag third-slot hints from thread-view hydration fill it). Per-event hints are de-duplicated; an event whose `e` tag contains a hint URL produces one `HintRelayFact` per (relay_url, source) pair.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:428:+Emitted by `handle_event` (`crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/ingest.rs:134-164`) for every EVENT arrival. This is the highest-cardinality lane and the only one where coalescing matters at the ADR-0007 boundary: the platform diagnostic view consumes a summarised projection (`ProvenanceSummary` per author or per event), not the raw fact stream.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:452:+The compiler may *use* facts from multiple lanes to compute a routing decision; it may **not** present them as one. Concretely:
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:454:+- **Routing decision** (`AuthorRouting.source` from [compiler.md](compiler.md) §3.1): records *which lane* the relay set was derived from. Single-valued; one of `Nip65 | Hint | Indexer | UserConfigured`. The author may have facts in three lanes; the compiler picks one, says so, and the other lane records remain visible.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:456:+- **The platform diagnostic view** receives all four lanes as separate `ViewBatch` records. The UI may render them in one screen with four side-by-side columns, but the data path is four lanes.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:462:+The lanes inform each other through these well-defined hooks:
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:465:+- `Hint → planner suggestion.` `HintRelayFact` with `recently_succeeded: true` may surface in the diagnostic view as "you might want to add this to your indexer set." Again, not automatic.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:466:+- `User-configured` is the **only** lane the compiler treats as authoritative-by-policy (the user said so). Open question 5 in the parent index resolves the augment-vs-override precedence between NIP-65 and user-configured for the active account.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:468:+The lesson the four-lane discipline preserves: routing is **derivable but contested** evidence. Collapsing the lanes loses information; preserving them lets the diagnostic answer "why did we route this REQ to that relay?" months after the decision.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:476:+This is one specific `ViewModule` that consumes the four-lane fact streams plus the compiler's `RelayPlan`s and produces a per-relay summary.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:489:+    pub by_lane: ByLaneCounts,
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:512:+1. `Nip65RelayFact` records — increments/decrements `by_lane.nip65` per (relay_url, pubkey) membership.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:522:+Emission cadence follows ADR-0007's diagnostic-view rule: material-transition immediately, otherwise 1–4 Hz. The provenance counter ticks every second; the `by_lane` counts only emit on `CompiledPlan` recompiles or new mailbox arrivals.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:562:+/// A logical interest is what a kernel-side consumer (view, action, monitor,
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:563:+/// sync job, pointer loader) wants alive on the wire. The compiler turns N
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:564:+/// logical interests into M ≤ N per-relay plans.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:567:+    /// Stable identity assigned by the registry on first insertion. Survives
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:568:+    /// recompilation. Two interests with identical content collide on hash but
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:569:+    /// keep distinct ids if they were registered by distinct claims.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:572:+    /// Scope decides how mailbox resolution and indexer fallback behave.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:573:+    /// Account-scoped interests resolve via the active account's mailbox view
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:574:+    /// for ambiguous filters (e.g. interests with no `authors` and no `#p`).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:577:+    /// What the consumer wants. This is a normalised filter set, not a Nostr
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:578:+    /// wire filter. Tags use sorted vec representation so equality is stable.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:581:+    /// Routing hints the consumer wants honoured. The compiler may ignore
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:582:+    /// hints if they conflict with policy (e.g. private-publish privacy).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:585:+    /// Lifecycle: tailing means "stay open after EOSE"; one_shot closes on
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:586:+    /// EOSE. Window is the planner's intent, not necessarily the relay
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:587:+    /// `since`/`until` it ends up emitting.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:592:+    /// Bound to the active account in SessionState. Re-routes on account switch.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:594:+    /// Bound to a specific account (multi-account UIs, M8). Re-routes on that
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:595:+    /// account's mailbox refresh; ignored on account switch.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:597:+    /// No account context. Used for global pointer loaders, NIP-19-driven
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:598:+    /// fetches, and indexer-direct probes.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:629:+    /// Translate a view spec into the logical interests required to keep it
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:630:+    /// live. Called by the planner when the view opens; re-called on
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:631:+    /// recompilation triggers that the view module opts into (account-switch,
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:632:+    /// mailbox refresh).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:661:+- A **diagnostic record**. ADR-0007 lanes are facts derived from the planner's state, not inputs.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:840:+These are the two pure functions [compiler.md](compiler.md) Stage 1 calls per author. They return `AuthorRouting` with the `RoutingSource` tag set per the four-lane discipline ([diagnostics.md](diagnostics.md) §5.2). Test fixtures live in `crates/nmp-nip65/src/tests/routing.rs`; the same fixtures plug into the audit gate (§9).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:914:+    /// Compute the relay set for publishing a signed event. Pure (no side
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:915:+    /// effects); the caller (action ledger) feeds the result into the
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:916:+    /// per-relay publish state machine.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:928:+    pub required_success_count: u8,            // ledger acceptance threshold
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:934:+    pub reason: PublishRouteReason,            // which lane motivated this relay
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:939:+    AuthorWriteRelay  { lane: RelayFactLane },     // Nip65 or UserConfigured
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:940:+    RecipientInbox    { recipient: Pubkey, lane: RelayFactLane },
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:953:+    /// Public events (kind:1, kind:0, kind:3, kind:10002, kind:7, ...).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:954:+    /// Falls back to indexer set if author has no write relays.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:956:+    /// Private/gift-wrapped events (kind:1059 wrapping NIP-44). Fails closed
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:957:+    /// if any recipient has no inbox relays.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:959:+    /// Notifications (kind:1 with `#p` tags, reactions, zaps, replies that
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:960:+    /// the author wants to surface to the tagged pubkey). Combines author
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:961:+    /// write + each `#p` inbox.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:967:+    NoAuthorRelays { author: Pubkey, lane_facts: ByLaneCounts },
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:1009:+        assignments = [each author_outbox.relays → AuthorWriteRelay { lane }]
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:1016:+        assignments = union(each recipient's inbox.relays → RecipientInbox { recipient, lane })
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:1018:+        required_success_count = recipients.len() as u8  // at least one per recipient
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:1029:+- **Step 2's "no indexer fallback for writes"** is the structural enforcement of the doctrine `docs/product-spec/subsystems.md` §7.3 line 99: "fall back to indexer set for reads only; do not publish to indexers." A failed Step 2 surfaces in the action ledger as `Failed { reason: NoAuthorRelays }`, which the UI renders as a toast per ADR-0007's `SideEffect` lane.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:1035:+The override exists for tests, migration tools, and operator power-user flows. Per `docs/aim.md` §6 doctrine 5 ("manual relay selection is the opt-out, not the default") and `docs/product-spec/subsystems.md` §7.3 line 90 ("explicit overrides are named, one-shot, and debug-flagged in logs"), the override must be:
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:1039:+3. **Audited** — emits a `Diagnostic::PublishOverrideUsed { reason, action_id }` on the `SideEffect` lane and writes a debug-level log line on every dispatch.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:1103:+This is the SideEffect-lane payload per ADR-0007. The platform diagnostic UI renders an entry on every override; the count over a session is a soft metric (Cargo dependents using overrides routinely should re-examine their flow).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:1131:+> Read first: [compiler.md](compiler.md) §3.4 for plan-id semantics.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:1222:+Compiler effect: re-runs Stages 1–4 for every interest that touches `pubkey` either as a member of `shape.authors` or as a member of `shape.tags[#p]`. Other interests stay assigned to their current plan-id slot; the merged plan-id changes (because the mailbox snapshot's `created_at` for `pubkey` advanced) but its `per_relay` content may be identical.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:1260:+Both bump a `generation: u64` so the plan-id picks up the change (per [compiler.md](compiler.md) §3.4). M2 binds the generation counters but does not yet implement a settings UI to mutate them; v1 ships the seams.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:1326:+> **Codegen dependency.** The test below introspects an `AppActionMeta` reflection
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:1331:+> generate `AppActionMeta` or to back-stop with a `proc-macro`-driven enum walker.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:1341:+    let variants = AppActionMeta::all_variants();
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:1410:+    // Assert: plan-id is deterministic — running compile twice yields the same id.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:1450:+    // Assert: plan-id changed.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:1472:+### Assertion 4 — Four-lane diagnostic distinctness
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:1474:+> "The diagnostics screen shows the four relay-fact lanes (NIP-65 / hint / provenance / user-configured) separately."
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:1478:+fn four_lanes_stay_distinct_in_diagnostic_payload() {
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:1482:+    // Set up evidence in all four lanes for the same relay url.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:1494:+    assert_eq!(coverage.by_lane.nip65, 1);
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:1495:+    assert_eq!(coverage.by_lane.hint,  1);
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:1496:+    assert_eq!(coverage.by_lane.user_configured, 1);
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:1500:+    // Structural: no compiler output collapses lanes.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:1504:+    // role_tags is a SET, not a single value — lanes are preserved.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:1559:+The audit gate's job is exactly the four assertions: API shape, fan-out structure, recompilation on late NIP-65, and four-lane diagnostic distinctness. Those are the four exit-gate bullets the milestone document lists; this test is the verification surface for all four.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:1567:Review for: (1) doctrine compliance (in particular D0 — does nmp-nip65 propose any kernel-side type leaks? D4 — single source for relay-set facts?); (2) any TODO/FIXME/unimplemented; (3) file-size compliance (any new file > 300 LOC?); (4) internal consistency across the 8 sub-docs; (5) compatibility with the upcoming M3 LMDB schema (e.g. watermark table); (6) the planned test (m2_subscription_compilation_audit) exit criteria; (7) any hidden shortcuts. Be terse; name file:line + fix for any concern.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:1646:docs/design/subscription-compilation/diagnostics.md:75:Emitted by the pointer loader (post-M2; for M2 the field exists but is rarely populated — only `e`/`a`-tag third-slot hints from thread-view hydration fill it). docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:1750:docs/plan.md:9:> **The doctrine is final** (`docs/product-spec.md` §1.5): D0 kernel never grows app nouns · D1 best-effort rendering with placeholders · D2 reactivity contract (composite reverse index, ≤60Hz/view, working-set bound) · D3 errors never cross FFI · D4 one writer per fact · D5 capabilities report, never decide. Every PR is reviewed against this rubric; a change that makes any doctrine harder to enforce is rewritten or rejected.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:1754:docs/plan.md:65:4. **The doctrine rubric is final.** Every PR is reviewed against the cardinal doctrines (`product-spec.md` §1.5, D0–D5). A change that makes any doctrine harder to enforce is rewritten or rejected.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:1755:docs/plan.md:66:5. **The kernel never grows app nouns.** ADR-0009 doctrine D0 is enforced by review and by the M11 podcast-app proof.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:1764:docs/plan.md:137:- Distinct-source visibility: the diagnostics screen shows the four relay-fact lanes (NIP-65 / hint / provenance / user-configured) separately.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:1778:docs/plan.md:363:- **Zero open `TODO`/`FIXME`/`XXX`/`unimplemented!()`** in `crates/nmp-core/src/ffi.rs`, `crates/nmp-core/src/actor.rs`, `crates/nmp-core/src/relay.rs`, `crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/**`, and the iOS bridge sources (`ios/NmpStress/NmpStress/KernelBridge.swift`, `KernelModel.swift`). Hard zero — no deferral escape, no "tracking issue" carve-out. Every pre-existing one is resolved in M10.5. If something genuinely cannot be done in M10.5 because it belongs to a later milestone (e.g. NIP-65 outbox work), then it is not a TODO/FIXME in the scoped files — it lives as a milestone task in `docs/plan.md`, not as a code marker.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:1779:docs/plan.md:380:- Doctrine review (D0–D5) signed off on the FFI surface in writing in `docs/perf/m10.5/doctrine-review.md`.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:1940:docs/decisions/0009-app-extension-kernel-boundary.md:88:- **Two fixture apps prove the boundary in v1.** A tiny non-Nostr-shaped fixture (e.g., a TODO/notes module with an app-local identity) lands in 1a.1; the Twitter clone is the first Nostr-shaped extension module. Together they prove the kernel works in both directions.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:1990:docs/product-spec/subsystems.md:90:- "Publish leaked to wrong relays" → ruled out by the safe API. The developer cannot supply a relay list to `SendNote`. Explicit overrides are named, one-shot, and debug-flagged in logs.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:2036:docs/product-spec/api-surface.md:22:    /// `config` carries data directory, default relays, storage backend choice,
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:2037:docs/product-spec/api-surface.md:48:`AppConfig` is a `uniffi::Record` containing only platform-resolved primitives (paths, lists of relay URLs, feature-flag booleans). No `Arc<dyn ...>` types in the config — capabilities are registered separately via setters so each can be bridged on its own schedule.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:2063:docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md:31:### D0. Kernel + extension modules — no app nouns in `nmp-core`

docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:2478:   132	- **Step 2's "no indexer fallback for writes"** is the structural enforcement of the doctrine `docs/product-spec/subsystems.md` §7.3 line 99: "fall back to indexer set for reads only; do not publish to indexers." docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:2484:   138	The override exists for tests, migration tools, and operator power-user flows. Per `docs/aim.md` §6 doctrine 5 ("manual relay selection is the opt-out, not the default") and `docs/product-spec/subsystems.md` §7.3 line 90 ("explicit overrides are named, one-shot, and debug-flagged in logs"), the override must be:
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:2488:   142	3. **Audited** — emits a `Diagnostic::PublishOverrideUsed { reason, action_id }` on the `SideEffect` lane and writes a debug-level log line on every dispatch.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:2552:   206	This is the SideEffect-lane payload per ADR-0007. The platform diagnostic UI renders an entry on every override; the count over a session is a soft metric (Cargo dependents using overrides routinely should re-examine their flow).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:2663:    89	2. **Read fallback.** For a `RoutingDirection::Outbox` interest whose author has no known mailboxes, the compiler routes the interest to the indexer set **as read-only fallback**. Per `docs/product-spec/subsystems.md` §7.3 line 99: "fall back to indexer set for reads only; do not publish to indexers." The `RoutingSource::Indexer` tag on the resulting `AuthorRouting` flows through to the diagnostic surface so the four-lane view (§5) can render "author X is being served by indexer Y because we have no mailbox for them."
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:2727:   153	- **Recompilation with no change ⇒ same plan-id.** If `ingest_relay_list` (`crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/ingest.rs:218-221`) deduplicates and decides not to replace a stale mailbox, no plan-id churn.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:2728:   154	- **Adding an interest changes plan-id even if no new wire REQ results.** Two interests can merge into the same SubShape; the plan-id changes because the *interest set* changed. The platform diagnostic correctly reports "logical-interest count went up; wire-sub count did not."
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:2729:   155	- **A new kind:10002 for a covered author changes plan-id.** Even if the author's new write relays overlap entirely with the old set (e.g. relay added then removed), the snapshot's `created_at` advanced, so the hash changes. ADR-0007 diagnostics will reflect this; the wire-emitter's diff will be a no-op if the actual relay assignment is unchanged.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:2730:   156	- **Indexer set change changes plan-id.** Operator config edits surface immediately.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:2732:   158	The `plan_id` is stored on `CompiledPlan` and rendered into `LogicalInterestStatus` (extending the record at `crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/mod.rs:147-154` with `plan_id: String, plan_generation: u64`). Tests in §9 assert plan-id stability across no-op recompilations.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:2740:   166	| `startup_requests` (requests.rs:50-106) | Becomes `register_startup_interests()`: pushes 4 `LogicalInterest`s into the registry (seed timeline; account kind:0; account kind:10002; seed contacts kind:3). The compiler runs once and produces wire REQs. The TEST_PUBKEY-specific bootstrap (line 71-82) becomes an `InterestScope::Global` interest with hardcoded indexer routing flagged `RoutingSource::UserConfigured` (it is operator-debug seed data). |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:2779:     8	## 5.0 The four lanes
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:2781:    10	Per `docs/design/ndk-applesauce-lessons.md` §4 (lines 39–46) and `docs/aim.md` §6 doctrine 10 ("provenance preserved"), the four relay-fact lanes are:
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:2788:    17	Each lane is its own record stream. They never merge into a single "relays" field — that collapse is exactly the bug `docs/design/ndk-applesauce-lessons.md` §4 line 46 forbids. They may be displayed side-by-side in a diagnostic view; the actor stores them apart.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:2790:    19	This is structurally enforced: there is no `Vec<RelayUrl>` field on any compiler output type. Every relay-bearing field carries a `lane: RelayFactLane` discriminator.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:2793:    22	// crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/diagnostics/lanes.rs (proposed)
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:2804:    33	## 5.1 Per-lane record schemas
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:2806:    35	Each lane has one record type. All four are exposed to the platform via the existing ADR-0007 `ViewBatch` lane (low-cadence, coalesced to 1–4 Hz per ADR-0007 "How status crosses the bridge"). They feed into the diagnostics screen, not into normal product UI.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:2829:    58	### Lane 2 — `HintRelayFact`
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:2832:    61	pub struct HintRelayFact {
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:2840:    69	    EventTag    { event_id: EventId, tag: TagKey, position: u8 },
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:2846:    75	Emitted by the pointer loader (post-M2; for M2 the field exists but is rarely populated — only `e`/`a`-tag third-slot hints from thread-view hydration fill it). Per-event hints are de-duplicated; an event whose `e` tag contains a hint URL produces one `HintRelayFact` per (relay_url, source) pair.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:2861:    90	Emitted by `handle_event` (`crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/ingest.rs:134-164`) for every EVENT arrival. This is the highest-cardinality lane and the only one where coalescing matters at the ADR-0007 boundary: the platform diagnostic view consumes a summarised projection (`ProvenanceSummary` per author or per event), not the raw fact stream.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:2885:   114	The compiler may *use* facts from multiple lanes to compute a routing decision; it may **not** present them as one. Concretely:
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:2887:   116	- **Routing decision** (`AuthorRouting.source` from [compiler.md](compiler.md) §3.1): records *which lane* the relay set was derived from. Single-valued; one of `Nip65 | Hint | Indexer | UserConfigured`. The author may have facts in three lanes; the compiler picks one, says so, and the other lane records remain visible.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:2889:   118	- **The platform diagnostic view** receives all four lanes as separate `ViewBatch` records. The UI may render them in one screen with four side-by-side columns, but the data path is four lanes.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:2895:   124	The lanes inform each other through these well-defined hooks:
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:2898:   127	- `Hint → planner suggestion.` `HintRelayFact` with `recently_succeeded: true` may surface in the diagnostic view as "you might want to add this to your indexer set." Again, not automatic.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:2899:   128	- `User-configured` is the **only** lane the compiler treats as authoritative-by-policy (the user said so). Open question 5 in the parent index resolves the augment-vs-override precedence between NIP-65 and user-configured for the active account.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:2901:   130	The lesson the four-lane discipline preserves: routing is **derivable but contested** evidence. Collapsing the lanes loses information; preserving them lets the diagnostic answer "why did we route this REQ to that relay?" months after the decision.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:2909:   138	This is one specific `ViewModule` that consumes the four-lane fact streams plus the compiler's `RelayPlan`s and produces a per-relay summary.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:2922:   151	    pub by_lane: ByLaneCounts,
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:2945:   174	1. `Nip65RelayFact` records — increments/decrements `by_lane.nip65` per (relay_url, pubkey) membership.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:2955:   184	Emission cadence follows ADR-0007's diagnostic-view rule: material-transition immediately, otherwise 1–4 Hz. The provenance counter ticks every second; the `by_lane` counts only emit on `CompiledPlan` recompiles or new mailbox arrivals.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:2969:     4	> Read first: [compiler.md](compiler.md) §3.4 for plan-id semantics.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:3060:    95	Compiler effect: re-runs Stages 1–4 for every interest that touches `pubkey` either as a member of `shape.authors` or as a member of `shape.tags[#p]`. Other interests stay assigned to their current plan-id slot; the merged plan-id changes (because the mailbox snapshot's `created_at` for `pubkey` advanced) but its `per_relay` content may be identical.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:3098:   133	Both bump a `generation: u64` so the plan-id picks up the change (per [compiler.md](compiler.md) §3.4). M2 binds the generation counters but does not yet implement a settings UI to mutate them; v1 ships the seams.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:3316:   190	    // Set up evidence in all four lanes for the same relay url.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:3393:   267	The audit gate's job is exactly the four assertions: API shape, fan-out structure, recompilation on late NIP-65, and four-lane diagnostic distinctness. docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:3410:     5	> **Companion docs:** `docs/plan.md` §M2; `docs/aim.md` §4.4–§4.5; `docs/design/ndk-applesauce-lessons.md` §7; ADR-0007 (diagnostic lanes); `docs/design/kernel-substrate.md` §3 (`ViewModule`) and §4 (`ActionModule`); `docs/product-spec/subsystems.md` §7.2–§7.3.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:3418:    13	- [Diagnostics — four-lane records + reverse-coverage view](subscription-compilation/diagnostics.md) (§5, §8)
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:3429:    24	| 3 | Compilation pipeline: authors → mailboxes → per-relay plans + plan-id | compiler.md |

docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:3442:    37	1. **Plan-id stability under perturbation.** The intro/compiler picks "logical-interest set + author-mailbox snapshot ⇒ plan-id." That ties plan-id to mailbox membership, so a single new kind:10002 arrival reshuffles plan-ids for every interest including that author. An alternative scopes plan-id to the *logical-interest set only* and tracks per-relay assignment as a separate stable identifier. Pick one in an ADR; the test contracts in §9 assume the former.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:3480:    30	/// A logical interest is what a kernel-side consumer (view, action, monitor,
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:3481:    31	/// sync job, pointer loader) wants alive on the wire. The compiler turns N
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:3482:    32	/// logical interests into M ≤ N per-relay plans.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:3485:    35	    /// Stable identity assigned by the registry on first insertion. Survives
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:3486:    36	    /// recompilation. Two interests with identical content collide on hash but
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:3487:    37	    /// keep distinct ids if they were registered by distinct claims.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:3490:    40	    /// Scope decides how mailbox resolution and indexer fallback behave.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:3491:    41	    /// Account-scoped interests resolve via the active account's mailbox view
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:3492:    42	    /// for ambiguous filters (e.g. interests with no `authors` and no `#p`).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:3495:    45	    /// What the consumer wants. This is a normalised filter set, not a Nostr
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:3496:    46	    /// wire filter. Tags use sorted vec representation so equality is stable.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:3499:    49	    /// Routing hints the consumer wants honoured. The compiler may ignore
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:3500:    50	    /// hints if they conflict with policy (e.g. private-publish privacy).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:3503:    53	    /// Lifecycle: tailing means "stay open after EOSE"; one_shot closes on
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:3504:    54	    /// EOSE. Window is the planner's intent, not necessarily the relay
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:3505:    55	    /// `since`/`until` it ends up emitting.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:3510:    60	    /// Bound to the active account in SessionState. Re-routes on account switch.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:3512:    62	    /// Bound to a specific account (multi-account UIs, M8). Re-routes on that
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:3513:    63	    /// account's mailbox refresh; ignored on account switch.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:3515:    65	    /// No account context. Used for global pointer loaders, NIP-19-driven
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:3516:    66	    /// fetches, and indexer-direct probes.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:3547:    97	    /// Translate a view spec into the logical interests required to keep it
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:3548:    98	    /// live. Called by the planner when the view opens; re-called on
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:3549:    99	    /// recompilation triggers that the view module opts into (account-switch,
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:3550:   100	    /// mailbox refresh).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:3579:   129	- A **diagnostic record**. ADR-0007 lanes are facts derived from the planner's state, not inputs.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:3672:    90	- "Publish leaked to wrong relays" → ruled out by the safe API. The developer cannot supply a relay list to `SendNote`. Explicit overrides are named, one-shot, and debug-flagged in logs.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:3886:docs/design/subscription-compilation/diagnostics.md:10:Per `docs/design/ndk-applesauce-lessons.md` §4 (lines 39–46) and `docs/aim.md` §6 doctrine 10 ("provenance preserved"), the four relay-fact lanes are:
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:3887:docs/design/subscription-compilation/diagnostics.md:17:Each lane is its own record stream. They never merge into a single "relays" field — that collapse is exactly the bug `docs/design/ndk-applesauce-lessons.md` §4 line 46 forbids. They may be displayed side-by-side in a diagnostic view; the actor stores them apart.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:3893:docs/design/subscription-compilation/compiler.md:89:2. **Read fallback.** For a `RoutingDirection::Outbox` interest whose author has no known mailboxes, the compiler routes the interest to the indexer set **as read-only fallback**. Per `docs/product-spec/subsystems.md` §7.3 line 99: "fall back to indexer set for reads only; do not publish to indexers." The `RoutingSource::Indexer` tag on the resulting `AuthorRouting` flows through to the diagnostic surface so the four-lane view (§5) can render "author X is being served by indexer Y because we have no mailbox for them."
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:3894:docs/design/subscription-compilation/compiler.md:166:| `startup_requests` (requests.rs:50-106) | Becomes `register_startup_interests()`: pushes 4 `LogicalInterest`s into the registry (seed timeline; account kind:0; account kind:10002; seed contacts kind:3). The compiler runs once and produces wire REQs. The TEST_PUBKEY-specific bootstrap (line 71-82) becomes an `InterestScope::Global` interest with hardcoded indexer routing flagged `RoutingSource::UserConfigured` (it is operator-debug seed data). |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:3904:docs/design/subscription-compilation/outbox.md:132:- **Step 2's "no indexer fallback for writes"** is the structural enforcement of the doctrine `docs/product-spec/subsystems.md` §7.3 line 99: "fall back to indexer set for reads only; do not publish to indexers." A failed Step 2 surfaces in the action ledger as `Failed { reason: NoAuthorRelays }`, which the UI renders as a toast per ADR-0007's `SideEffect` lane.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:3906:docs/design/subscription-compilation/outbox.md:138:The override exists for tests, migration tools, and operator power-user flows. Per `docs/aim.md` §6 doctrine 5 ("manual relay selection is the opt-out, not the default") and `docs/product-spec/subsystems.md` §7.3 line 90 ("explicit overrides are named, one-shot, and debug-flagged in logs"), the override must be:
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:4112:   137	- Distinct-source visibility: the diagnostics screen shows the four relay-fact lanes (NIP-65 / hint / provenance / user-configured) separately.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:4380:codex
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:4387:- [nip65.md:192](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/docs/design/subscription-compilation/nip65.md:192) plus [nip65.md:197](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/docs/design/subscription-compilation/nip65.md:197): `nmp-nip65` depends on `nmp-core`, while `nmp-core::kernel::planner` consumes `nmp-nip65`. That is a Rust crate cycle and blurs D0. Fix by moving mailbox traits/types into `nmp-core` or a tiny no-core crate, with `nmp-nip65` only registering module behavior.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:4393:- [diagnostics.md:61](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/docs/design/subscription-compilation/diagnostics.md:61) and [diagnostics.md:172](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/docs/design/subscription-compilation/diagnostics.md:172): `HintRelayFact` has no author/subject key, and the coverage reducer does not consume hint or user-configured fact streams, yet [tests.md:202](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/docs/design/subscription-compilation/tests.md:202) asserts those counts. Add subject identity to hint facts and make the view consume all four lanes.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:4395:- [diagnostics.md:15](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/docs/design/subscription-compilation/diagnostics.md:15), [diagnostics.md:116](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/docs/design/subscription-compilation/diagnostics.md:116), [diagnostics.md:157](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/docs/design/subscription-compilation/diagnostics.md:157): indexer fallback is sometimes a fifth source and sometimes user-configured. Fix by modeling it as `UserConfigured { category: Indexer }`, with any indexer count as a subcategory, not a new lane.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:4397:- [compiler.md:166](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/docs/design/subscription-compilation/compiler.md:166) and [compiler.md:184](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/docs/design/subscription-compilation/compiler.md:184): startup timeline/profile/contact behavior remains framed as `nmp-core` migration work. D0 fix: move social bootstrap modules to `nmp-nip01` / `nmp-nip02` / demo app code; core should only execute registered module interests.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:4399:- [outbox.md:119](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/docs/design/subscription-compilation/outbox.md:119): private publish uses a union of recipient inbox relays plus `required_success_count = recipients.len() as u8`; this neither proves one success per recipient nor avoids overflow. Fix with per-recipient delivery requirements and `usize`.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:4401:Other checks: no literal `TODO`, `FIXME`, or `unimplemented!` in the added docs. File sizes are compliant: all new files are under 300 LOC, largest is `tests.md` at 273. The planned audit test still has weak spots: [tests.md:34](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/docs/design/subscription-compilation/tests.md:34) depends on non-existent `AppActionMeta`, and [tests.md:213](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/docs/design/subscription-compilation/tests.md:213) does not actually assert lane distinctness.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:15:You are reviewing merge 8d633e8 (research(applesauce+ndk): deep-dive notes for M2 outbox + framework-magic) on master in nostr-multi-platform. This adds research notes from studying the Applesauce and NDK codebases to inform M2 outbox routing and the framework-magic contract. Doctrine D0-D5:
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:16:- D0: kernel never grows app nouns (nmp-core stays substrate-only; per ADR-0009)
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:112:+- `claims: WeakMap<NostrEvent, number>` (line 176, **a counter, not a boolean** — see gotchas §G2).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:189:+- `selectOptimalRelays` (`helpers/relay-selection.ts:14-93`) is a greedy set-cover: each iteration picks the relay covering the most uncovered users, optionally biased by `score(relay, coverage, popularity)`. `maxRelaysPerUser` caps per-user concentration. The TODO at line 89 acknowledges that the final per-user relay list may exceed `maxRelaysPerUser` because the count is enforced during selection, not after.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:226:+## G2. Event claims: counter vs boolean = leak vs over-eviction
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:228:+**Commit `75ef7d5f`** ("Convert event claims to counter to avoid memory leaks"). Pre-fix: claims were a boolean (`Set<NostrEvent>` semantics). If two subscriptions claimed the same event and one unsubscribed, the boolean cleared and the LRU pruner could evict the event while the second subscription was still bound to it. Fix: refcount via `WeakMap<NostrEvent, number>`. Decrement to zero deletes the entry; non-zero keeps the event pinned.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:584:+The TODO at line 89 acknowledges a known imprecision: `maxRelaysPerUser` is enforced during the greedy pass but not as a post-filter, so a user can end up with more than `maxRelaysPerUser` final relays if their first picks happened to cover the pool.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:1200:+Source explicitly notes the gap: `// TODO: The state of this tracker needs to be added to cache adapters` (`tracker.ts:48-49`).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:1230:+`calculateRelaySetFromEvent` doesn't fully account for relays where specific hashtags are popular (`relay/sets/calculate.ts:18-19` TODO). For tag-heavy posts (e.g., #bitdevs), publishing is suboptimal.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:1234:+`getTopRelaysForAuthors` has TODO to incorporate quality metrics. Currently ranks by author-count only.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:1480:+Outbox tracker state is *not* persisted to cache adapters — there is a `TODO` in `tracker.ts:48-49` noting rehydration is unimplemented. On every fresh app boot, the tracker is empty and the first wave of subscriptions pays full discovery cost.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:2196:docs/decisions/0009-app-extension-kernel-boundary.md:88:- **Two fixture apps prove the boundary in v1.** A tiny non-Nostr-shaped fixture (e.g., a TODO/notes module with an app-local identity) lands in 1a.1; the Twitter clone is the first Nostr-shaped extension module. Together they prove the kernel works in both directions.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:2208:docs/design/kernel-substrate.md:39:    /// Secondary indexes the kernel maintains for this module's primary store.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:2230:docs/design/kernel-substrate.md:483:1. Phase 1a.1 (kernel substrate prototype) ships with one fixture module (`fixture-todo-core`) demonstrating each of the five trait families. Codegen produces a working `nmp-app-fixture` crate. Desktop iced app renders a TODO list, no business logic in Swift / iced.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:2232:docs/design/subscription-compilation.md:5:> **Companion docs:** [`docs/plan/m2-subscription-compilation.md`](../plan/m2-subscription-compilation.md); `docs/aim.md` §4.4–§4.5; `docs/design/ndk-applesauce-lessons.md` §7; ADR-0007 (diagnostic lanes); `docs/design/kernel-substrate.md` §3 (`ViewModule`) and §4 (`ActionModule`); `docs/product-spec/subsystems.md` §7.2–§7.3.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:2236:docs/design/subscription-compilation.md:37:1. **Plan-id stability under perturbation.** The intro/compiler picks "logical-interest set + author-mailbox snapshot ⇒ plan-id." That ties plan-id to mailbox membership, so a single new kind:10002 arrival reshuffles plan-ids for every interest including that author. An alternative scopes plan-id to the *logical-interest set only* and tracks per-relay assignment as a separate stable identifier. Pick one in an ADR; the test contracts in §9 assume the former.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:2239:docs/design/framework-magic.md:5:> **Source directives:** `docs/plan/scope-adjustments-2026-05-18.md` "Framework magic contract" section; `docs/aim.md` §6 doctrines 1–12; `docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md` §1.5 (cardinal doctrines D0–D5) + §3.3 (bug-class extinction); `docs/product-spec/subsystems.md` §7.1–§7.8.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:2255:docs/plan/scope-adjustments-2026-05-18.md:9:| **M9 — NIP-17 DMs + NSE** | **Deferred to post-v1.** DMs add NSE, gift-wrap, NIP-44, App Groups, and a whole capability lane that we don't need for v1 demos. Re-add as a post-v1 milestone. |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:2280:docs/plan.md:9:> **The doctrine is final** (`docs/product-spec.md` §1.5): D0 kernel never grows app nouns · D1 best-effort rendering with placeholders · D2 reactivity contract (composite reverse index, ≤60Hz/view, working-set bound) · D3 errors never cross FFI · D4 one writer per fact · D5 capabilities report, never decide. Every PR is reviewed against this rubric; a change that makes any doctrine harder to enforce is rewritten or rejected.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:2331:docs/design/subscription-compilation/diagnostics.md:22:// crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/diagnostics/lanes.rs (proposed)
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:2333:docs/design/subscription-compilation/diagnostics.md:90:Emitted by `handle_event` (`crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/ingest.rs:134-164`) for every EVENT arrival. This is the highest-cardinality lane and the only one where coalescing matters at the ADR-0007 boundary: the platform diagnostic view consumes a summarised projection (`ProvenanceSummary` per author or per event), not the raw fact stream.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:2365:docs/design/subscription-compilation/compiler.md:153:- **Recompilation with no change ⇒ same plan-id.** If `ingest_relay_list` (`crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/ingest.rs:218-221`) deduplicates and decides not to replace a stale mailbox, no plan-id churn.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:2366:docs/design/subscription-compilation/compiler.md:158:The `plan_id` is stored on `CompiledPlan` and rendered into `LogicalInterestStatus` (extending the record at `crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/mod.rs:147-154` with `plan_id: String, plan_generation: u64`). Tests in §9 assert plan-id stability across no-op recompilations.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:2408:docs/design/subscription-compilation/intro.md:30:/// A logical interest is what a kernel-side consumer (view, action, monitor,
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:2409:docs/design/subscription-compilation/intro.md:41:    /// Account-scoped interests resolve via the active account's mailbox view
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:2437:docs/design/subscription-compilation/outbox.md:112:        assignments = [each author_outbox.relays → AuthorWriteRelay { lane }]
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:2442:docs/design/subscription-compilation/outbox.md:138:The override exists for tests, migration tools, and operator power-user flows. Per `docs/aim.md` §6 doctrine 5 ("manual relay selection is the opt-out, not the default") and `docs/product-spec/subsystems.md` §7.3 line 90 ("explicit overrides are named, one-shot, and debug-flagged in logs"), the override must be:
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:2451:docs/design/framework-magic/intro.md:21:The 13 contract bullets map onto the cardinal doctrines (D0–D5 in `product-spec/overview-and-dx.md` §1.5) and onto the older `aim.md` §6 doctrines 1–12. The mapping is intentionally many-to-many — a single behavior may discharge multiple doctrines, and a single doctrine may require several behaviors to be fully discharged.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:2452:docs/design/framework-magic/intro.md:25:| **D0** kernel + extension modules (no app nouns in `nmp-core`) | All 13 — the contract is the API the app sees in place of the missing nouns |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:2460:docs/design/framework-magic/intro.md:56:1. **It forces honesty.** If a chapter cannot fill the "App writes" field with `"nothing"` or a single safe call, the framework has leaked the operation to the app, and the doctrine D0 boundary is violated. The author of that chapter is required to file an ADR rather than ship the bullet as-is.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:2486:docs/design/framework-magic/outbox.md:50:5. **Override audit:** dispatch a `PublishWithOverride` on a public action; assert the side-effect lane emits `Diagnostic::PublishOverrideUsed { ... }` and the debug log line per `outbox.md` §7.4 (3).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:2538:    15	2. **Type-erased registry.** Modules register handlers keyed by string namespace; payloads cross FFI as `Vec<u8>` (or `serde_json::Value`). The kernel doesn't know module types statically; it dispatches via the registry. Plug-and-play module loading; loses type safety at the FFI boundary.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:2631:   108	    Module(&'static str, Vec<u8>),    // module namespace + serialized action
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:2669:   146	- **Cleaner FFI surface.** No `Vec<u8>` blobs across FFI. No JSON parsing on the platform side. No string-keyed switches.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:2680:   157	The type-erased path (`Vec<u8>` actions, string-keyed registry, runtime dispatch) was seriously considered and rejected for v1:
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:2682:   159	- **Loses safety where it matters most.** The FFI boundary is exactly where strong typing has the highest payoff. Swift / Kotlin / TypeScript consumers benefit from idiomatic typed enums. Vec<u8> + opaque dispatch reproduces JavaScript-shaped fragility in three statically-typed languages.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:2700:   177	| Type-erased registry (`Vec<u8>` actions) | See "Why not type-erased" above. |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:2730:   127	    /// Domain key the platform shadow indexes this view under (ADR-0005).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:2733:   130	    /// What kinds of events / projections wake this view (ADR-0001).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:2736:   133	    /// Initial open: build State and Payload from current store contents.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:2739:   136	    /// Event arrived matching dependencies.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:2743:   140	    /// Event removed (kind:5 delete, expiration, etc).

docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:2828:    11	- **Fixture proving the kernel boundary** in `crates/fixture-todo-core` (~304 LOC) plus generated `apps/fixture/nmp-app-fixture`: a non-Nostr TODO module implementing all five substrate trait families, with codegen producing the per-app crate. Proves the kernel works for arbitrary domains, not just Nostr.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:2874:     9	| **M9 — NIP-17 DMs + NSE** | **Deferred to post-v1.** DMs add NSE, gift-wrap, NIP-44, App Groups, and a whole capability lane that we don't need for v1 demos. Re-add as a post-v1 milestone. |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:3017:     9	See [`m9-messaging.md`](m9-messaging.md) for the full milestone spec (scope, subsystem deliverables, exit gate). **Deferred reason:** DMs add NSE, gift-wrap, NIP-44, App Groups, and a whole capability lane that are not load-bearing for v1 doctrine proofs. The outbox planner's structural ban on routing private events to non-inbox relays is implemented in [M2](m2-subscription-compilation.md) regardless — so the routing contract is already enforced at v1; DMs slot in cleanly when this milestone runs post-v1.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:2485:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:1914:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:6696:+docs/design/lmdb-schema.md:75:**Error semantics.** All methods return `Result<T, StoreError>`. Per doctrine D3, store errors do **not** cross FFI — the actor maps every variant to either (a) a typed `Effect` (e.g. `StoreCorrupt`, surfaces via diagnostics + toast), (b) a `tracing::warn!` log + degraded state, or (c) a panic at startup if the LMDB environment refuses to open. The trait itself uses `Result` since it is internal to the actor process.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:2505:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:1934:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:6872:+   181	**Best-effort field contract (per doctrine D1).** Every display-bearing field in every view payload is **non-optional** and has a defined placeholder when the underlying data is missing:
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:2506:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:1935:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:6933:+   242	Per doctrine D2, live views tail with REQ immediately and use NIP-77 as the preferred historical backfill mechanism when support can be proven. The sync engine is a planner policy over cache coverage, relay capabilities, and progress state.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:2507:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:1936:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:7096:+    74	Per doctrine D3, NIP-65 routing is the long-term default policy for reads and writes. v1 ships only the relay-target resolver seam and explicit/simple resolver; this subsystem is the post-v1 policy layer that consumes that seam.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:2508:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:1937:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:7153:+     9	> **The doctrine is final** (`docs/product-spec.md` §1.5): D0 kernel never grows app nouns · D1 best-effort rendering with placeholders · D2 reactivity contract (composite reverse index, ≤60Hz/view, working-set bound) · D3 errors never cross FFI · D4 one writer per fact · D5 capabilities report, never decide. Every PR is reviewed against this rubric; a change that makes any doctrine harder to enforce is rewritten or rejected.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:2509:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:1938:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:7164:+    20	- **Live Nostr-connected iOS app** in `ios/NmpStress` (~1,375 LOC Swift): SwiftUI shell wired to the Rust kernel via raw C FFI. Connects to `wss://relay.primal.net` (content) + `wss://purplepag.es` (indexer). Renders seed-driven timeline from union of pablof7z + fiatjaf + jb55 follow lists. Profile resolution with placeholders → in-place refinement on kind:0 arrival per doctrine D1. Thread view. Diagnostics screen showing relay status, logical interests, wire subscriptions (ADR-0007).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:2510:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:1939:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:7174:+    30	- NIP-65 outbox routing (hardcoded content + indexer relays today).

docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:2516:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:1945:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:7346:+    23	| Per-relay provenance (which relays delivered each event; first seen / last seen) | Out of scope; the crate models events, not their wire history | `subsystems.md` §7.1 "Duplicate id → merge relay provenance set"; ADR-0007 diagnostics; outbox routing scoring in M2+ |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:2517:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:1946:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:7398:+    75	**Error semantics.** All methods return `Result<T, StoreError>`. Per doctrine D3, store errors do **not** cross FFI — the actor maps every variant to either (a) a typed `Effect` (e.g. `StoreCorrupt`, surfaces via diagnostics + toast), (b) a `tracing::warn!` log + degraded state, or (c) a panic at startup if the LMDB environment refuses to open. The trait itself uses `Result` since it is internal to the actor process.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:2518:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:1947:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:7600:+A few likely consistency issues are emerging: key-layout drift between the master doc and `keys.md`, trait methods named in summaries but absent from the actual trait, and a possible D2/working-set leak around the “open view cover” being outside the trait contract. I’m checking those against the exact sub-doc lines now.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:2663:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2093:docs/design/framework-magic/sessions.md:41:C12 is the most direct demonstration of cardinal doctrine **D4** ("single writer per fact; caches derive"). The "fact" is `SessionState.active`. The "caches" are every active-account-scoped view, every signer binding, every relay-routing decision. The framework's job is to make sure every cache derives mechanically; the app's job is to write the fact once.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:2664:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2094:docs/design/framework-magic/outbox.md:6:Both bullets in this chapter discharge cardinal doctrine **D3** ("outbox routing is automatic; manual relay selection is the opt-out") and `aim.md` §6 doctrine 5. C7 additionally discharges `aim.md` §6 doctrine 10 ("private events cannot be accidentally republished to public relays") and `product-spec/overview-and-dx.md` §3.3 bug #4.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:2665:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2095:docs/design/framework-magic/outbox.md:54:## The two bullets together discharge D3
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:2666:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2096:docs/design/framework-magic/outbox.md:56:C6 covers the read side; C7 covers the write side. Together they discharge cardinal doctrine D3 in full — every relay-touching operation routes through framework policy, and the only API surfaces that name a relay URL are:
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:2667:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2097:docs/design/framework-magic/outbox.md:62:The app's domain code, view modules, and action modules **never** name a relay. That is the doctrine-D3 boundary the contract holds in place.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:2668:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2098:docs/plan/m8-multi-account.md:7:**Scope.** Per spec doctrine D4 (single writer per fact) extended to account scope:
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:2669:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2099:docs/design/framework-magic/sync.md:34:**App writes:** nothing. The app opens a view; the framework decides whether to serve from cache (with confidence backed by coverage), backfill via NIP-77, or fall back to bounded REQ. The view payload streams in as the gap closes; no spinner gates the cached render (per C13 and D1).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:2670:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2100:docs/plan/m2-subscription-compilation.md:1:# M2 — Subscription compilation + outbox routing
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:2671:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2101:docs/plan/m2-subscription-compilation.md:11:- `nmp-nip65` protocol module: Mailboxes view module (parsed kind:10002); outbox routing as a planner subsystem; recompilation triggers (kind:10002 arrival, view open, relay reconnect).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:2672:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2102:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:17:NMP doctrine D0-D5 (D0 kernel never grows app nouns; D1 best-effort placeholders; D2 reactivity ≤60Hz/view + working-set bound; D3 errors never cross FFI; D4 one writer per fact; D5 capabilities report don't decide). File size: 300 LOC soft, 500 hard.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:2673:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2103:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:87:+- **M2 (subscription compilation + outbox routing + NIP-65):** design landed; 8 codex-flagged issues being addressed (T13).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:2674:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2104:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:99:+- **Cardinal doctrine (D0–D5).** Every PR is reviewed against this rubric. A change that makes any doctrine harder to enforce is rewritten or rejected.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:2678:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2108:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:103:+  - **D3** Errors never cross FFI — become `toast: Option<String>` state fields
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:2679:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2109:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:104:+  - **D4** One writer per fact
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:2680:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2110:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:105:+  - **D5** Capabilities report, never decide policy

docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:2684:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2114:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:210:+- [Capabilities & rendering — best-effort placeholders, in-place refinement](framework-magic/capabilities.md) (C13)
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:2685:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2115:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:223:+| C5 | Kind:3 auto-tracking: active account's follow-list change recompiles dependent subscriptions transparently | kind3.md | `c5_kind3_change_recompiles_follow_dependent_subs` | **[PENDING M2]** | scope-adj §"Folded into M2"; D3; M2 design §4 (Trigger::Nip65Arrived analog for kind:3) |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:2686:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2116:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:224:+| C6 | Outbox read routing: `authors`-filter subscriptions fan out to those authors' write relays (NIP-65), de-duplicated | outbox.md | `c6_authors_subscription_routes_to_per_author_write_relays` | **[PENDING M2]** | D3; spec §7.3 row "Subscription with `authors`"; M2 design §7 |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:2687:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2117:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:225:+| C7 | Outbox write routing: publishes go to author write + `#p`-recipient inbox; private (gift-wrap) events fail closed when recipient inbox is unknown | outbox.md | `c7_publish_routes_outbox_and_private_fails_closed` | **[PENDING M2 seam → M6 publish]** | D3; spec §7.3 rows "Publish*"; §3.3 bugs #3, #4 |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:2688:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2118:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:228:+| C10 | Sync watermarks: planner consults `(filter, relay)` coverage before issuing historical REQ; full coverage makes cache-miss authoritative; NIP-77 negentropy is the default backfill where supported | sync.md | `c10_watermark_gates_backfill_and_authoritative_miss` | **[PENDING M4]** | D2; spec §7.1 watermarks, §7.8 sync engine |

docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:2690:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2120:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:231:+| C13 | Best-effort rendering: every view payload field is non-`Option`; missing data uses defined placeholders (shortened npub, identicon, "just now"); the same payload updates in place when authoritative data arrives | capabilities.md | `c13_view_payload_uses_placeholders_then_refines_in_place` | **[DONE]** in placeholder shape; **[PENDING M2/M3]** for in-place refinement on enrich | D1; spec §7.6 "Best-effort field contract"; aim §4.12 |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:2691:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2121:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:293:+> Read first: `docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md` §1.5 doctrine D1; `docs/product-spec/subsystems.md` §7.6 (the per-field placeholder table + the `TimelineItem` concrete example); `docs/aim.md` §4.12; `docs/design/view-catalog/profile-timeline-thread-reactions.md`.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:2692:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2122:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:311:+**Failure mode prevented:** the entire class of "Nostr-client cold-start UI" bugs `subsystems.md` §1.5 D1 enumerates as ruled out by construction:
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:2693:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2123:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:318:+The bug-extinction surface in `overview-and-dx.md` §3.3 does not have a single numbered bug for this because the failures are UX defects rather than data-corruption bugs, but the doctrine clause D1 is the explicit promise the contract holds.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:2694:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2124:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:344:+C13 is the canonical instance of cardinal doctrine **D1**. The doctrine clause's wording — *"There is no `if has_profile { render } else { spinner }` pattern available in the API"* — is testable through the payload shape itself, which is what sub-path 1's "no `loading` field" assertion checks. The framework cannot guarantee the app does not implement its own spinner over the payload, but it can guarantee the API does not give the app a way to ask the question that would justify one.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:2695:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2125:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:346:+C13 also intersects D4 (single writer per fact; caches derive). The "fact" is the projection (Alice's display name); the "caches" are every timeline cell, profile chip, thread author marker rendering that name. The in-place refinement is the derivation.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:2696:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2126:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:387:+The 13 contract bullets map onto the cardinal doctrines (D0–D5 in `product-spec/overview-and-dx.md` §1.5) and onto the older `aim.md` §6 doctrines 1–12. The mapping is intentionally many-to-many — a single behavior may discharge multiple doctrines, and a single doctrine may require several behaviors to be fully discharged.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:2697:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2127:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:391:+| **D0** kernel + extension modules (no app nouns in `nmp-core`) | All 13 — the contract is the API the app sees in place of the missing nouns |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:2698:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2128:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:392:+| **D1** best-effort rendering — render now, refine in place | C13 (placeholders), C1–C4 (refinement triggers), C5 (kind:3 → re-render of follow-derived views) |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:2699:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2129:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:393:+| **D2** negentropy first, REQ second | C10 (watermarks + NIP-77 backfill) |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:2700:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2130:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:394:+| **D3** outbox routing is automatic | C5 (kind:3 → recompile), C6 (read fan-out), C7 (write fan-out + private fail-closed) |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:2701:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2131:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:395:+| **D4** single writer per fact; caches derive | C9 (provenance merge), C12 (account switch as state), C13 (refinement is a re-render, not a re-fetch) |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:2702:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2132:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:396:+| **D5** snapshots bounded by what's open | C8 (view-scoped subscriptions; buffered ≤60Hz per view) |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:2703:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2133:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:422:+1. **It forces honesty.** If a chapter cannot fill the "App writes" field with `"nothing"` or a single safe call, the framework has leaked the operation to the app, and the doctrine D0 boundary is violated. The author of that chapter is required to file an ADR rather than ship the bullet as-is.

C7 additionally discharges `aim.md` §6 doctrine 10 ("private events cannot be accidentally republished to public relays") and `product-spec/overview-and-dx.md` §3.3 bug #4.

Together they discharge cardinal doctrine D3 in full — every relay-touching operation routes through framework policy, and the only API surfaces that name a relay URL are:
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:2707:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2137:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:602:+The app's domain code, view modules, and action modules **never** name a relay. That is the doctrine-D3 boundary the contract holds in place.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:2708:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2138:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:754:+C12 is the most direct demonstration of cardinal doctrine **D4** ("single writer per fact; caches derive"). The "fact" is `SessionState.active`. The "caches" are every active-account-scoped view, every signer binding, every relay-routing decision. The framework's job is to make sure every cache derives mechanically; the app's job is to write the fact once.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:2709:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2139:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:898:+The reason this is C8 and not bundled with C6/C7 is that C6/C7 govern *which relay* a REQ targets; C8 governs *how many REQs and at what cadence* regardless of the relay. Different doctrines (D3 vs D5+aim §6 doctrine 6) and different milestone responsibility.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:2710:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2140:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:952:+**App writes:** nothing. The app opens a view; the framework decides whether to serve from cache (with confidence backed by coverage), backfill via NIP-77, or fall back to bounded REQ. The view payload streams in as the gap closes; no spinner gates the cached render (per C13 and D1).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:2711:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2141:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:1257: | 2026-05-18 01:55 | 1 | Wave 1 completed in ~30 min. Landed: T7 build-verifier f1e374b (all gates green, sim screenshot captured); T6 debt-auditor d660735 (0 TODO/FIXME, 0 unimplemented in FFI surface, 4 must-fix safety-comment items); T2 m2-designer 031fc07 (subscription compilation spec, 8 files); T3 m3-designer 9fead0e (LMDB schema spec, 6 files); T5 m11-designer 0dfb975 (M11 podcast design, 13 files); T4 m105-designer's ffi-hardening files (5) absorbed into orchestrator commit fcf8b43. Three codex post-merge reviews ran: review-1 on the wave-1 cumulative diff flagged 5 issues (debt-inventory exit-ready language, NIP-XX placeholder, ADR-deferral escape, file sizes); review-2 on fcf8b43 flagged 6 issues (D5 mismatch, pre-merge CI gate, freed-pointer UB, S10 conditional, file sizes again, symbol count); review-3 on 0dfb975 flagged 7 issues (Swift file sizes 898/642, MCP-in-CI, AudioPlayback policy, EmbeddingId cycle, rig.rs weakened, OPML pixel-parity, view count). All flagged issues are being addressed via dedicated fix-it tasks T8 (codex-fixer-1) and T11 (codex-fixer-2). T1 (m1-hardener) unblocked + dispatched. T9 (ffi-safety-commenter) dispatched for the 4 must-fix items. Post-merge codex review protocol established and entered memory. |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:2712:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2142:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:1258: | 2026-05-18 02:10 | 2 | Heartbeat #2. Landed: T9 ffi-safety-commenter 03d7a23 (SAFETY comments accurate, semantics unchanged, cargo+tests pass; codex flagged pre-existing clippy lints in actor.rs+reactivity-bench → T12); m1-hardener live.rs prototype stashed (was polluting main tree again). Two outstanding codex reviews caught up: codex on 031fc07 found 8 substantive M2-design issues (no-publish-to-indexer, D4 single-writer, D0 module ownership, plan-id churn, lane modeling) → T13; codex on 9fead0e found 10 substantive M3-design issues (atomicity ADR blocker, D2 not enforced through trait, GC pinned-overlay unbounded, kind:5 a-tag deletes missing, tombstone semantics, key encoding mismatches, scan_by_kind_dtag missing from trait) → T14. Worktree agent-a41054fd04781ce06 cleaned. T10 (plan.md split) dispatched. 4 agents in flight: m1-hardener, codex-fixer-1, codex-fixer-2, plan-splitter. |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:2713:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2143:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:1723:+Applesauce's outbox is not a relay-resolver service. It is a **reactive composition of three RxJS operators** over the EventStore. There is no central "outbox service" — there is just `OutboxModel = contacts() ⇒ includeMailboxes() ⇒ selectOptimalRelays()`. Every consumer that wants outbox routing subscribes to the model directly.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:2749:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2179:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:3646:README.md:31:  - **D5** Capabilities report, never decide policy
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:2750:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2180:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:3647:README.md:88:| `docs/product-spec.md` + `docs/product-spec/` | What we ship at v1. The cardinal doctrine D0–D5 lives in §1.5. |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:2751:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2181:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:3648:README.md:101:2. `docs/product-spec.md` §1.5 — the doctrine D0–D5.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:2752:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2182:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:3649:docs/design/framework-magic/subs.md:34:The reason this is C8 and not bundled with C6/C7 is that C6/C7 govern *which relay* a REQ targets; C8 governs *how many REQs and at what cadence* regardless of the relay. Different doctrines (D3 vs D5+aim §6 doctrine 6) and different milestone responsibility.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:2753:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2183:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:3650:docs/design/framework-magic/sessions.md:41:C12 is the most direct demonstration of cardinal doctrine **D4** ("single writer per fact; caches derive"). The "fact" is `SessionState.active`. The "caches" are every active-account-scoped view, every signer binding, every relay-routing decision. The framework's job is to make sure every cache derives mechanically; the app's job is to write the fact once.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:2754:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2184:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:3651:docs/design/framework-magic/sync.md:34:**App writes:** nothing. The app opens a view; the framework decides whether to serve from cache (with confidence backed by coverage), backfill via NIP-77, or fall back to bounded REQ. The view payload streams in as the gap closes; no spinner gates the cached render (per C13 and D1).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:2755:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f68a479.md:2185:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:3652:docs/design/framework-magic/intro.md:21:The 13 contract bullets map onto the cardinal doctrines (D0–D5 in `product-spec/overview-and-dx.md` §1.5) and onto the older `aim.md` §6 doctrines 1–12. The mapping is intentionally many-to-many — a single behavior may discharge multiple doctrines, and a single doctrine may require several behaviors to be fully discharged.
