docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:5213:docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:4798:docs/design/podcast-app-rebuild.md:19:**Multiplatform-ready.** Although M11 ships only the iPhone target, every extension crate is pure Rust and compiles to wasm32 + android targets (capabilities aside). Android/Desktop/Web shells are explicitly post-M11; the design must not foreclose them.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:5214:docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:4899:docs/plan/parallelization.md:12:- **[M15](m15-cross-platform.md) (Android + Desktop + Web)** is three parallel tracks once [M14](m14-uniffi.md) (UniFFI) lands.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:5309:docs/perf/orchestration-log.md:12:| 2026-05-18 02:55 | 4 | Heartbeat #4 (autonomous). MAJOR: T17 framework-magic-designer landed edc17b0 (13-bullet contract + 14 named tests in framework_magic_contract.rs across 10 sub-files + index). T15 applesauce-explorer + T16 ndk-explorer both completed but couldn't write/commit themselves; orchestrator transcribed 13 files (5 applesauce + 8 ndk) and pushed as 8d633e8. KEY FINDING from NDK research that propagated into framework-magic contract + scope memo: "auto-update of open subs when follow list changes — no app code involved" is empirically FALSE in core NDK; Svelte gets it via runes, React requires explicit deps, raw core has NO follow-list watcher. NMP must build this as framework code (~200 LOC per platform) AND the framework-magic contract guarantees app dispatches zero code. T8 codex-fixer-1 also wrapped up its iterative cycle (fb139ab + 80217cc final). User landed mid-tick directive: maintain top-level README.md with TL;DR + decisions + architecture map (committed 810d0f8); heartbeat cron rewritten (cc1fba11) to fold README refresh into every tick. ~~4 lingering m11-design followup commits~~ (59f8d1b/8302cc1/fd8f173/2477372) from codex-fixer-2 cleaned by its own internal codex loop. T15/T16/T17 marked completed. Pending T13 (M2 codex fixes), T14 (M3 codex fixes) still held. Plan-splitter, m1-hardener, highlighter-explorer, codex-fixer-1, clippy-cleaner still in flight. |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:5310:docs/perf/pending-user-decisions.md:13:**Decision (autonomous):** The canonical D0–D5 is what's in `docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md` §1.5 (kernel-boundary, best-effort, negentropy-first, outbox-automatic, single-writer-per-fact, snapshots-bounded). I corrected the README to match (commit pending in next push).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:5311:docs/perf/pending-user-decisions.md:24:**Current default while you decide:** option (a). README + framework-magic docs will be aligned to product-spec D0–D5; supporting rules cited by their source (RMP-bible invariant N, ADR-NNNN, etc.).

docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:5352:docs/perf/codex-reviews/ab632c1.md:2496:- **Kind:3 auto-tracking** for the active account's follow list. When a kind:3 lands for the active account, the planner automatically refreshes the author-set of every subscription that depends on "the current user's follows". The app never re-issues the REQ — the framework recompiles transparently.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:5404:docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:190:README.md:15:- **M4 (NIP-77 negentropy sync), M5 (NIP-42 auth), M6 (sessions + signers + write), M7 (interaction loop), M8 (multi-session):** designs pending after M2/M3 hardens.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:5405:docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:191:README.md:18:- **M11 (rebuild of `/Users/pablofernandez/src/podcast` on NMP):** design landed; pixel-parity UI copy + Rust-backed business logic + `rig.rs` LLM + RAG + podcast-feeds; awaits M10.5 empirical pass before impl starts.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:5406:docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:192:README.md:19:- **M11.5 (rebuild of `/Users/pablofernandez/Work/hl/app` Highlighter on NMP + `nmp-nip29` crate):** designed in flight (T18).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:5407:docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:193:README.md:21:- **Framework-magic contract** (kind:3 auto-tracking, `bunker://` URL onboarding, new-nsec creation, outbox-by-default-on-publish, etc.): designed ([docs/design/framework-magic.md](docs/design/framework-magic.md)) with 13 behaviors and 14 named tests in `crates/nmp-testing/tests/framework_magic_contract.rs`.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:5408:docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:194:README.md:25:- **Cardinal doctrine (D0–D5).** Canonical wording from [`docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md` §1.5](docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md). Every PR is reviewed against this rubric. A change that makes any doctrine harder to enforce is rewritten or rejected. Conflicts between doctrines resolve in the order listed.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:5409:docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:195:README.md:26:  - **D0** Kernel + extension modules — no app nouns in `nmp-core` (proven by the M11 podcast rebuild and the existing fixture-todo-core).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:5410:docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:196:README.md:27:  - **D1** Best-effort rendering — render now, refine in place. Placeholders are part of the type contract; the API has no "spinner gating cached content" pattern.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:5411:docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:197:README.md:28:  - **D2** Negentropy first, REQ second. NIP-77 reconciliation with durable watermarks is the default backfill; live REQ is the tailing path. (See M4.)
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:5412:docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:198:README.md:29:  - **D3** Outbox routing is automatic; manual relay selection is the opt-out. Reads route per author write relays; publishes route to author write relays + tagged recipients' inbox relays. (See M2.)
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:5413:docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:199:README.md:30:  - **D4** Single writer per fact; caches derive. Five cache layers exist; one writer per fact; cache invalidation is not a concept in the public API.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:5414:docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:200:README.md:31:  - **D5** Snapshots bounded by what's open. `AppState` carries the projection through currently-open views, not the underlying event store.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:5415:docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:201:README.md:36:- **DMs (was M9) and Wallet (was M12) deferred to post-v1.** Documented in `docs/plan/scope-adjustments-2026-05-18.md`. M11.5 Highlighter takes their slot.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:5416:docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:202:README.md:38:- **Kind:3 auto-tracking is framework-magic** — when the active account's follow list changes, every open subscription that depends on "current user's follows" auto-recompiles on the wire. Apps dispatch zero code. The Applesauce/NDK research (`docs/research/`) confirmed core NDK does NOT provide this automatically — NMP must build it as framework code.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:5417:docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:203:README.md:40:- **Empirical iOS proof before podcast rebuild (M10.5 gate).** No "we'll wire it up later" — the FFI surface ships rock-solid (stress harness + simulator-driven Sonnet-agent UI fleet + Instruments leaks audit + iPhone-12 baseline) before any line of M11 code is written.

Plan revised (55dd5f2) inserting M10.5 (FFI hardening) and concretizing M11 (`../podcast` rebuild). 15-min cron heartbeat armed. First wave of 6 background agents dispatched: build-verifier (T7), debt-auditor (T6), m2-designer (T2), m3-designer (T3), m105-designer (T4), m11-designer (T5). T1 blocked on T7. |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:5427:docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:216:docs/perf/orchestration-log.md:12:| 2026-05-18 02:55 | 4 | Heartbeat #4 (autonomous). MAJOR: T17 framework-magic-designer landed edc17b0 (13-bullet contract + 14 named tests in framework_magic_contract.rs across 10 sub-files + index). T15 applesauce-explorer + T16 ndk-explorer both completed but couldn't write/commit themselves; orchestrator transcribed 13 files (5 applesauce + 8 ndk) and pushed as 8d633e8. KEY FINDING from NDK research that propagated into framework-magic contract + scope memo: "auto-update of open subs when follow list changes — no app code involved" is empirically FALSE in core NDK; Svelte gets it via runes, React requires explicit deps, raw core has NO follow-list watcher. NMP must build this as framework code (~200 LOC per platform) AND the framework-magic contract guarantees app dispatches zero code. T8 codex-fixer-1 also wrapped up its iterative cycle (fb139ab + 80217cc final). User landed mid-tick directive: maintain top-level README.md with TL;DR + decisions + architecture map (committed 810d0f8); heartbeat cron rewritten (cc1fba11) to fold README refresh into every tick. ~~4 lingering m11-design followup commits~~ (59f8d1b/8302cc1/fd8f173/2477372) from codex-fixer-2 cleaned by its own internal codex loop. T15/T16/T17 marked completed. Pending T13 (M2 codex fixes), T14 (M3 codex fixes) still held. Plan-splitter, m1-hardener, highlighter-explorer, codex-fixer-1, clippy-cleaner still in flight. |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:5428:docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:231:docs/perf/pending-user-decisions.md:13:**Decision (autonomous):** The canonical D0–D5 is what's in `docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md` §1.5 (kernel-boundary, best-effort, negentropy-first, outbox-automatic, single-writer-per-fact, snapshots-bounded). I corrected the README to match (commit pending in next push).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:5429:docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:237:docs/perf/pending-user-decisions.md:24:**Current default while you decide:** option (a). README + framework-magic docs will be aligned to product-spec D0–D5; supporting rules cited by their source (RMP-bible invariant N, ADR-NNNN, etc.).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:5430:docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:269:docs/aim.md:129:Per-pubkey relay lists are fetched lazily via a gossip layer, cached in a swappable backend (in-memory or SQLite), and refreshed when a fresher kind-10002 arrives. When a user's outbox changes, dependent subscriptions automatically re-resolve their relay sets.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:5431:docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:309:docs/perf/codex-reviews/f1e374b.md:545:* | | 51120cb perf(orchestration): HB4 (autonomous) — framework-magic landed + research + README
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:5432:docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:404:docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:648:+- **No outbox cache TTL or staleness check.** Once `MailboxesModel` has emitted, only `update$`/`insert$` for that specific kind:10002 changes it. There's no periodic refresh.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:5433:docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:414:docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:1176:+- Core NDK: filters are immutable post-`subscribe()`. There is no follow-list watcher. The closest mechanism (`outboxTracker.on("user:relay-list-updated", …) → refreshRelayConnections`) only adds *relays*, not *authors*.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:5434:docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:591:docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:2302:docs/aim.md:129:Per-pubkey relay lists are fetched lazily via a gossip layer, cached in a swappable backend (in-memory or SQLite), and refreshed when a fresher kind-10002 arrives. When a user's outbox changes, dependent subscriptions automatically re-resolve their relay sets.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:5454:docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:3555:docs/design/framework-magic.md:73:The orchestrator's heartbeat triage rule includes a "framework-magic regression" gate: any milestone landing that *removes* a bullet without an ADR fails review.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:5455:docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:3641:docs/research/applesauce/outbox.md:148:- **No outbox cache TTL or staleness check.** Once `MailboxesModel` has emitted, only `update$`/`insert$` for that specific kind:10002 changes it. There's no periodic refresh.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:5456:docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:3696:docs/design/podcast/podcast-feeds.md:66:Conditional GET (ETag / If-Modified-Since) for `RefreshFeed`: the kernel persists a `FeedFetchCache { feed_url, etag?, last_modified?, fetched_at_ms }` row per podcast. On refresh: send the headers; on 304 → emit `RefreshFeedOutput::NotModified`.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:5457:docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:3925:docs/design/framework-magic/intro.md:70:The heartbeat triage cron (`docs/perf/orchestration-log.md`) treats a milestone landing without a framework-magic delta as a structural defect — the milestone must either touch the contract or explicitly affirm it did not.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:5475:docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:4546:    19	- **Kind:3 auto-tracking** for the active account's follow list. When a kind:3 lands for the active account, the planner automatically refreshes the author-set of every subscription that depends on "the current user's follows". The app never re-issues the REQ — the framework recompiles transparently.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:5477:docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:4919:docs/perf/orchestration-log.md:7:| 2026-05-18 01:24 | 0 | Session start. Pulled to e9cbafa. Plan revised (55dd5f2) inserting M10.5 (FFI hardening) and concretizing M11 (`../podcast` rebuild). 15-min cron heartbeat armed. First wave of 6 background agents dispatched: build-verifier (T7), debt-auditor (T6), m2-designer (T2), m3-designer (T3), m105-designer (T4), m11-designer (T5). T1 blocked on T7. |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:5478:docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:4924:docs/perf/orchestration-log.md:12:| 2026-05-18 02:55 | 4 | Heartbeat #4 (autonomous). MAJOR: T17 framework-magic-designer landed edc17b0 (13-bullet contract + 14 named tests in framework_magic_contract.rs across 10 sub-files + index). T15 applesauce-explorer + T16 ndk-explorer both completed but couldn't write/commit themselves; orchestrator transcribed 13 files (5 applesauce + 8 ndk) and pushed as 8d633e8. KEY FINDING from NDK research that propagated into framework-magic contract + scope memo: "auto-update of open subs when follow list changes — no app code involved" is empirically FALSE in core NDK; Svelte gets it via runes, React requires explicit deps, raw core has NO follow-list watcher. NMP must build this as framework code (~200 LOC per platform) AND the framework-magic contract guarantees app dispatches zero code. T8 codex-fixer-1 also wrapped up its iterative cycle (fb139ab + 80217cc final). User landed mid-tick directive: maintain top-level README.md with TL;DR + decisions + architecture map (committed 810d0f8); heartbeat cron rewritten (cc1fba11) to fold README refresh into every tick. ~~4 lingering m11-design followup commits~~ (59f8d1b/8302cc1/fd8f173/2477372) from codex-fixer-2 cleaned by its own internal codex loop. T15/T16/T17 marked completed. Pending T13 (M2 codex fixes), T14 (M3 codex fixes) still held. Plan-splitter, m1-hardener, highlighter-explorer, codex-fixer-1, clippy-cleaner still in flight. |

docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:5485:docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:1042:+Search confirmed: no code in `core/` watches `session.followSet`, watches any "follows changed" event, or mutates `subscription.filters.authors` on the fly. Filters are immutable after `ndk.subscribe()` returns; the only post-creation mutation is `refreshRelayConnections` adding relays (see Mechanism 3 below).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:5486:docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:1117:+        if (isRelevant && typeof subscription.refreshRelayConnections === "function") {
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:5487:docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:1118:+            subscription.refreshRelayConnections();
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:5488:docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:1124:+`refreshRelayConnections` (`subscription/index.ts:787-812`):
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:5489:docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:1127:+public refreshRelayConnections(): void {
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:5490:docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:1146:+- **Bypassed when `relaySet` is explicit** — if your subscription was opened with a fixed `relaySet`, no auto-refresh.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:5491:docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:1157:+| Do open subscriptions auto-add relays when an author's NIP-65 list arrives? | Yes, via `refreshRelayConnections` | `core/src/ndk/index.ts:459-471`; `core/src/subscription/index.ts:787-812` |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:5492:docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:1176:+- Core NDK: filters are immutable post-`subscribe()`. There is no follow-list watcher. The closest mechanism (`outboxTracker.on("user:relay-list-updated", …) → refreshRelayConnections`) only adds *relays*, not *authors*.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:5493:docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:1244:+### No background-refresh API
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:5494:docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:1246:+There's no scheduled "tick" or "fetch since last seen" helper. NMP needs to build its own background-refresh loop using `since: lastSeen` filters.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:5495:docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:1428:+## Live subscription refresh
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:5496:docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:1437:+        if (isRelevant && typeof subscription.refreshRelayConnections === "function") {
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:5497:docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:1438:+            subscription.refreshRelayConnections();
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:5498:docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:1444:+`refreshRelayConnections()` (`subscription/index.ts:787-812`) recomputes the relay map via `calculateRelaySetsFromFilters` and **only adds** newly-discovered relays. It does not remove relays or change `filter.authors`. This is critical for understanding the limits of the auto-rewire (see kind3-auto-tracking.md).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:5499:docs/perf/codex-reviews/8d633e8.md:2302:docs/aim.md:129:Per-pubkey relay lists are fetched lazily via a gossip layer, cached in a swappable backend (in-memory or SQLite), and refreshed when a fresher kind-10002 arrives. When a user's outbox changes, dependent subscriptions automatically re-resolve their relay sets.

Bounded views | View registry, open/close/refresh actions, `ViewBatch`, claim GC, warmth TTL, raw/event-detail/timeline-like test views | Snapshots remain bounded by open views; shared materializations and empty payloads are deterministic |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:5507:docs/perf/codex-reviews/d660735.md:781:d660735:docs/aim.md:129:Per-pubkey relay lists are fetched lazily via a gossip layer, cached in a swappable backend (in-memory or SQLite), and refreshed when a fresher kind-10002 arrives. When a user's outbox changes, dependent subscriptions automatically re-resolve their relay sets.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:5508:docs/perf/codex-reviews/d660735.md:874:d660735:docs/design/ndk-applesauce-lessons.md:68:Relay capability should likewise be durable and observable. NDK's capability caching and Applesauce's explicit support checks both point to the same lesson: probing support on every operation wastes time, but assuming support forever is brittle. NMP should remember relay capabilities, refresh them deliberately, and expose degraded paths in diagnostics.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:5509:docs/perf/codex-reviews/d660735.md:1017:d660735:docs/product-spec/cli-toolchain-phasing.md:116:| 2. Bounded views | View registry, open/close/refresh actions, `ViewBatch`, claim GC, warmth TTL, raw/event-detail/timeline-like test views | Snapshots remain bounded by open views; shared materializations and empty payloads are deterministic |

docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:5511:docs/perf/codex-reviews/e9cbafa.md:453:-- Cached-data-never-withheld: any kind:0 in store is served immediately by profile view; background refresh does not gate.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:5512:docs/perf/codex-reviews/e9cbafa.md:530:+- `BackgroundWorkCapability`: kernel registers periodic background tasks (feed refresh, scheduled downloads); platform implements via BGTask scheduler (iOS) / WorkManager (Android).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:5513:docs/perf/codex-reviews/e9cbafa.md:672:-The `firehose-bench` harness (per `docs/design/firehose-bench.md`) is the load-bearing tool here. It runs in three modes: **live** (real relays, real network), **capture** (records live to a trace), **replay** (deterministic re-execution against `MockRelay`). Replay is what CI uses; live + capture are for soak testing and trace refresh.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:5525:docs/perf/codex-reviews/fb139ab.md:508:++- `Provenance → NIP-65 hint refresh.` If we observe many `ProvenanceRelayFact { relay_url: R, author: A }` records but no `Nip65RelayFact { pubkey: A, relay_url: R }`, the operator diagnostic can suggest "we are receiving A's events from R but A has not declared R; their kind:10002 may be stale." This is a future operator-UI affordance, not a behaviour.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:5552:docs/perf/codex-reviews/3f5a2b3.md:218:++- **Parallel-agent orchestration.** Multiple specialized subagents work in isolated git worktrees in parallel. Each commits + pushes via `fetch → rebase → push` (never force-push). The orchestrator dispatches, the heartbeat ticks every 15 minutes (`docs/perf/orchestration-log.md` is the durable trail).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:5553:docs/perf/codex-reviews/3f5a2b3.md:234:++| `docs/perf/` | Empirical measurements + heartbeats + codex reviews + debt inventories. |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:5554:docs/perf/codex-reviews/3f5a2b3.md:251:++*This file is regenerated on every heartbeat from the live state of the ladder + scope memo + last-commit summary. Manual edits between heartbeats are fine but will be folded back in by the next refresh.*
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:5555:docs/perf/codex-reviews/3f5a2b3.md:330:++The orchestrator's heartbeat triage rule includes a "framework-magic regression" gate: any milestone landing that *removes* a bullet without an ADR fails review.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:5556:docs/perf/codex-reviews/3f5a2b3.md:504:++The heartbeat triage cron (`docs/perf/orchestration-log.md`) treats a milestone landing without a framework-magic delta as a structural defect — the milestone must either touch the contract or explicitly affirm it did not.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:5557:docs/perf/codex-reviews/3f5a2b3.md:1323:+@@ -8,4 +8,5 @@ Durable trail of the parallel-agent orchestration. One line per heartbeat or sig
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:5558:docs/perf/codex-reviews/3f5a2b3.md:1329:++| 2026-05-18 02:55 | 4 | Heartbeat #4 (autonomous). MAJOR: T17 framework-magic-designer landed edc17b0 (13-bullet contract + 14 named tests in framework_magic_contract.rs across 10 sub-files + index). T15 applesauce-explorer + T16 ndk-explorer both completed but couldn't write/commit themselves; orchestrator transcribed 13 files (5 applesauce + 8 ndk) and pushed as 8d633e8. KEY FINDING from NDK research that propagated into framework-magic contract + scope memo: "auto-update of open subs when follow list changes — no app code involved" is empirically FALSE in core NDK; Svelte gets it via runes, React requires explicit deps, raw core has NO follow-list watcher. NMP must build this as framework code (~200 LOC per platform) AND the framework-magic contract guarantees app dispatches zero code. T8 codex-fixer-1 also wrapped up its iterative cycle (fb139ab + 80217cc final). User landed mid-tick directive: maintain top-level README.md with TL;DR + decisions + architecture map (committed 810d0f8); heartbeat cron rewritten (cc1fba11) to fold README refresh into every tick. ~~4 lingering m11-design followup commits~~ (59f8d1b/8302cc1/fd8f173/2477372) from codex-fixer-2 cleaned by its own internal codex loop. T15/T16/T17 marked completed. Pending T13 (M2 codex fixes), T14 (M3 codex fixes) still held. Plan-splitter, m1-hardener, highlighter-explorer, codex-fixer-1, clippy-cleaner still in flight. |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:5559:docs/perf/codex-reviews/3f5a2b3.md:1932:++- **No outbox cache TTL or staleness check.** Once `MailboxesModel` has emitted, only `update$`/`insert$` for that specific kind:10002 changes it. There's no periodic refresh.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:5560:docs/perf/codex-reviews/3f5a2b3.md:2326:++Search confirmed: no code in `core/` watches `session.followSet`, watches any "follows changed" event, or mutates `subscription.filters.authors` on the fly. Filters are immutable after `ndk.subscribe()` returns; the only post-creation mutation is `refreshRelayConnections` adding relays (see Mechanism 3 below).

docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:5564:docs/perf/codex-reviews/3f5a2b3.md:2411:++public refreshRelayConnections(): void {
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:5565:docs/perf/codex-reviews/3f5a2b3.md:2430:++- **Bypassed when `relaySet` is explicit** — if your subscription was opened with a fixed `relaySet`, no auto-refresh.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:5566:docs/perf/codex-reviews/3f5a2b3.md:2441:++| Do open subscriptions auto-add relays when an author's NIP-65 list arrives? | Yes, via `refreshRelayConnections` | `core/src/ndk/index.ts:459-471`; `core/src/subscription/index.ts:787-812` |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:5567:docs/perf/codex-reviews/3f5a2b3.md:2460:++- Core NDK: filters are immutable post-`subscribe()`. There is no follow-list watcher. The closest mechanism (`outboxTracker.on("user:relay-list-updated", …) → refreshRelayConnections`) only adds *relays*, not *authors*.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:5568:docs/perf/codex-reviews/3f5a2b3.md:2528:++### No background-refresh API
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:5569:docs/perf/codex-reviews/3f5a2b3.md:2530:++There's no scheduled "tick" or "fetch since last seen" helper. NMP needs to build its own background-refresh loop using `since: lastSeen` filters.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:5570:docs/perf/codex-reviews/3f5a2b3.md:2712:++## Live subscription refresh
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:5571:docs/perf/codex-reviews/3f5a2b3.md:2721:++        if (isRelevant && typeof subscription.refreshRelayConnections === "function") {
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:5572:docs/perf/codex-reviews/3f5a2b3.md:2722:++            subscription.refreshRelayConnections();
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:5573:docs/perf/codex-reviews/3f5a2b3.md:2728:++`refreshRelayConnections()` (`subscription/index.ts:787-812`) recomputes the relay map via `calculateRelaySetsFromFilters` and **only adds** newly-discovered relays. It does not remove relays or change `filter.authors`. This is critical for understanding the limits of the auto-rewire (see kind3-auto-tracking.md).

docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:5606:docs/perf/codex-reviews/3f5a2b3.md:4200:+   129	Per-pubkey relay lists are fetched lazily via a gossip layer, cached in a swappable backend (in-memory or SQLite), and refreshed when a fresher kind-10002 arrives. When a user's outbox changes, dependent subscriptions automatically re-resolve their relay sets.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:5651:docs/perf/codex-reviews/3f5a2b3.md:7235:README.md:25:- **Cardinal doctrine (D0–D5).** Canonical wording from [`docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md` §1.5](docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md). Every PR is reviewed against this rubric. A change that makes any doctrine harder to enforce is rewritten or rejected. Conflicts between doctrines resolve in the order listed.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:5652:docs/perf/codex-reviews/3f5a2b3.md:7236:README.md:26:  - **D0** Kernel + extension modules — no app nouns in `nmp-core` (proven by the M11 podcast rebuild and the existing fixture-todo-core).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:5653:docs/perf/codex-reviews/3f5a2b3.md:7237:README.md:27:  - **D1** Best-effort rendering — render now, refine in place. Placeholders are part of the type contract; the API has no "spinner gating cached content" pattern.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:5654:docs/perf/codex-reviews/3f5a2b3.md:7238:README.md:28:  - **D2** Negentropy first, REQ second. NIP-77 reconciliation with durable watermarks is the default backfill; live REQ is the tailing path. (See M4.)
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:5655:docs/perf/codex-reviews/3f5a2b3.md:7239:README.md:29:  - **D3** Outbox routing is automatic; manual relay selection is the opt-out. Reads route per author write relays; publishes route to author write relays + tagged recipients' inbox relays. (See M2.)
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:5656:docs/perf/codex-reviews/3f5a2b3.md:7240:README.md:30:  - **D4** Single writer per fact; caches derive. Five cache layers exist; one writer per fact; cache invalidation is not a concept in the public API.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:5657:docs/perf/codex-reviews/3f5a2b3.md:7241:README.md:31:  - **D5** Snapshots bounded by what's open. `AppState` carries the projection through currently-open views, not the underlying event store.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:5658:docs/perf/codex-reviews/3f5a2b3.md:7242:README.md:33:  Three additional load-bearing rules are not D-numbered but are equally binding (RMP-bible invariants from [`docs/aim.md` §2](docs/aim.md)): **errors never cross FFI** (operational failures become `toast: Option<String>` state fields), **capabilities report; never decide policy** (native bridges execute and report; Rust decides retry/recovery), and the **reactivity contract** (composite reverse index · ≤60 Hz/view · working-set bounded; ADR-0001..0004).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:5659:docs/perf/codex-reviews/3f5a2b3.md:7243:README.md:90:| `docs/product-spec.md` + `docs/product-spec/` | What we ship at v1. The cardinal doctrine D0–D5 lives in §1.5. |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:5660:docs/perf/codex-reviews/3f5a2b3.md:7244:README.md:103:2. `docs/product-spec.md` §1.5 — the doctrine D0–D5.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:5678:docs/perf/codex-reviews/0dfb975.md:1711:+Conditional GET (ETag / If-Modified-Since) for `RefreshFeed`: the kernel persists a `FeedFetchCache { feed_url, etag?, last_modified?, fetched_at_ms }` row per podcast. On refresh: send the headers; on 304 → emit `RefreshFeedOutput::NotModified`.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:5700:docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:464:+- `Provenance → NIP-65 hint refresh.` If we observe many `ProvenanceRelayFact { relay_url: R, author: A }` records but no `Nip65RelayFact { pubkey: A, relay_url: R }`, the operator diagnostic can suggest "we are receiving A's events from R but A has not declared R; their kind:10002 may be stale." This is a future operator-UI affordance, not a behaviour.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:5701:docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:595:+    /// account's mailbox refresh; ignored on account switch.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:5702:docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:632:+    /// mailbox refresh).

docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:5705:docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:1647:docs/design/subscription-compilation/diagnostics.md:126:- `Provenance → NIP-65 hint refresh.` If we observe many `ProvenanceRelayFact { relay_url: R, author: A }` records but no `Nip65RelayFact { pubkey: A, relay_url: R }`, the operator diagnostic can suggest "we are receiving A's events from R but A has not declared R; their kind:10002 may be stale." This is a future operator-UI affordance, not a behaviour.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:5706:docs/perf/codex-reviews/031fc07.md:1730:docs/aim.md:129:Per-pubkey relay lists are fetched lazily via a gossip layer, cached in a swappable backend (in-memory or SQLite), and refreshed when a fresher kind-10002 arrives. When a user's outbox changes, dependent subscriptions automatically re-resolve their relay sets.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:5727:docs/perf/codex-reviews/edc17b0.md:122:+The orchestrator's heartbeat triage rule includes a "framework-magic regression" gate: any milestone landing that *removes* a bullet without an ADR fails review.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:5728:docs/perf/codex-reviews/edc17b0.md:296:+The heartbeat triage cron (`docs/perf/orchestration-log.md`) treats a milestone landing without a framework-magic delta as a structural defect — the milestone must either touch the contract or explicitly affirm it did not.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:5729:docs/perf/codex-reviews/edc17b0.md:1216:docs/perf/orchestration-log.md:12:| 2026-05-18 02:55 | 4 | Heartbeat #4 (autonomous). MAJOR: T17 framework-magic-designer landed edc17b0 (13-bullet contract + 14 named tests in framework_magic_contract.rs across 10 sub-files + index). T15 applesauce-explorer + T16 ndk-explorer both completed but couldn't write/commit themselves; orchestrator transcribed 13 files (5 applesauce + 8 ndk) and pushed as 8d633e8. KEY FINDING from NDK research that propagated into framework-magic contract + scope memo: "auto-update of open subs when follow list changes — no app code involved" is empirically FALSE in core NDK; Svelte gets it via runes, React requires explicit deps, raw core has NO follow-list watcher. NMP must build this as framework code (~200 LOC per platform) AND the framework-magic contract guarantees app dispatches zero code. T8 codex-fixer-1 also wrapped up its iterative cycle (fb139ab + 80217cc final). User landed mid-tick directive: maintain top-level README.md with TL;DR + decisions + architecture map (committed 810d0f8); heartbeat cron rewritten (cc1fba11) to fold README refresh into every tick. ~~4 lingering m11-design followup commits~~ (59f8d1b/8302cc1/fd8f173/2477372) from codex-fixer-2 cleaned by its own internal codex loop. T15/T16/T17 marked completed. Pending T13 (M2 codex fixes), T14 (M3 codex fixes) still held. Plan-splitter, m1-hardener, highlighter-explorer, codex-fixer-1, clippy-cleaner still in flight. |

MAJOR: T17 framework-magic-designer landed edc17b0 (13-bullet contract + 14 named tests in framework_magic_contract.rs across 10 sub-files + index). T15 applesauce-explorer + T16 ndk-explorer both completed but couldn't write/commit themselves; orchestrator transcribed 13 files (5 applesauce + 8 ndk) and pushed as 8d633e8. KEY FINDING from NDK research that propagated into framework-magic contract + scope memo: "auto-update of open subs when follow list changes — no app code involved" is empirically FALSE in core NDK; Svelte gets it via runes, React requires explicit deps, raw core has NO follow-list watcher. NMP must build this as framework code (~200 LOC per platform) AND the framework-magic contract guarantees app dispatches zero code. T8 codex-fixer-1 also wrapped up its iterative cycle (fb139ab + 80217cc final). User landed mid-tick directive: maintain top-level README.md with TL;DR + decisions + architecture map (committed 810d0f8); heartbeat cron rewritten (cc1fba11) to fold README refresh into every tick. ~~4 lingering m11-design followup commits~~ (59f8d1b/8302cc1/fd8f173/2477372) from codex-fixer-2 cleaned by its own internal codex loop. docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:5737:docs/perf/codex-reviews/edc17b0.md:1486:    73	The orchestrator's heartbeat triage rule includes a "framework-magic regression" gate: any milestone landing that *removes* a bullet without an ADR fails review.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:5738:docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:58:+- **Kind:3 auto-tracking** for the active account's follow list. When a kind:3 lands for the active account, the planner automatically refreshes the author-set of every subscription that depends on "the current user's follows". The app never re-issues the REQ — the framework recompiles transparently.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:5739:docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:327:docs/plan/scope-adjustments-2026-05-18.md:19:- **Kind:3 auto-tracking** for the active account's follow list. The app never re-issues the REQ — the framework recompiles transparently.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:5741:docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:410:docs/plan/m2-subscription-compilation.md:16:- **Kind:3 auto-tracking**: when a kind:3 (follow list) lands for the active account, the planner automatically refreshes the author-set of every subscription that depends on "the current user's follows" — recompiling transparently without the app re-issuing the REQ. Reference: NDK auto-follow kind:3 changes + Applesauce event-store query builder auto-tracking.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:5744:docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:1050:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:3641:README.md:26:  - **D0** Kernel never grows app nouns (proven by the M11 podcast rebuild and the existing fixture-todo-core)
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:5745:docs/perf/codex-reviews/9eace68.md:1072:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:4048:README.md:35:- **Outbox routing is automatic by default** — `nmp-core::planner` resolves author write relays + recipient inbox relays on every publish; explicit override is an audited opt-out. (Stronger than NDK's caller-responsibility model and Applesauce's caller-responsibility model.)

docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:5784:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:15:Cumulative codex review on NMP master from edc17b0~..51120cb. Covers: framework-magic contract (edc17b0), m11 followup cleanups (8302cc1/59f8d1b/fd8f173/2477372), autonomous-mode log (fa7d144), applesauce+ndk research notes (8d633e8), top-level README.md (810d0f8), HB4 orchestration log (51120cb).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:5785:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:29: README.md                                          | 109 +++++++++++
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:5786:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:61:51120cb perf(orchestration): HB4 (autonomous) — framework-magic landed + research + README
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:5787:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:63:810d0f8 docs(readme): top-level README with TL;DR, decisions, architecture map
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:5788:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:69:diff --git a/README.md b/README.md
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:5789:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:73:+++ b/README.md
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:5790:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:79:+**Live status snapshot — see `docs/perf/orchestration-log.md` for per-heartbeat detail and `docs/perf/pending-user-decisions.md` for autonomous-mode decisions awaiting review.**
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:5791:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:150:+- **Parallel-agent orchestration.** Multiple specialized subagents work in isolated git worktrees in parallel. Each commits + pushes via `fetch → rebase → push` (never force-push). The orchestrator dispatches, the heartbeat ticks every 15 minutes (`docs/perf/orchestration-log.md` is the durable trail).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:5792:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:166:+| `docs/perf/` | Empirical measurements + heartbeats + codex reviews + debt inventories. |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:5793:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:183:+*This file is regenerated on every heartbeat from the live state of the ladder + scope memo + last-commit summary. Manual edits between heartbeats are fine but will be folded back in by the next refresh.*
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:5794:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:262:+The orchestrator's heartbeat triage rule includes a "framework-magic regression" gate: any milestone landing that *removes* a bullet without an ADR fails review.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:5795:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:436:+The heartbeat triage cron (`docs/perf/orchestration-log.md`) treats a milestone landing without a framework-magic delta as a structural defect — the milestone must either touch the contract or explicitly affirm it did not.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:5796:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:1255:@@ -8,4 +8,5 @@ Durable trail of the parallel-agent orchestration. One line per heartbeat or sig
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:5797:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:1261:+| 2026-05-18 02:55 | 4 | Heartbeat #4 (autonomous). MAJOR: T17 framework-magic-designer landed edc17b0 (13-bullet contract + 14 named tests in framework_magic_contract.rs across 10 sub-files + index). T15 applesauce-explorer + T16 ndk-explorer both completed but couldn't write/commit themselves; orchestrator transcribed 13 files (5 applesauce + 8 ndk) and pushed as 8d633e8. KEY FINDING from NDK research that propagated into framework-magic contract + scope memo: "auto-update of open subs when follow list changes — no app code involved" is empirically FALSE in core NDK; Svelte gets it via runes, React requires explicit deps, raw core has NO follow-list watcher. NMP must build this as framework code (~200 LOC per platform) AND the framework-magic contract guarantees app dispatches zero code. T8 codex-fixer-1 also wrapped up its iterative cycle (fb139ab + 80217cc final). User landed mid-tick directive: maintain top-level README.md with TL;DR + decisions + architecture map (committed 810d0f8); heartbeat cron rewritten (cc1fba11) to fold README refresh into every tick. ~~4 lingering m11-design followup commits~~ (59f8d1b/8302cc1/fd8f173/2477372) from codex-fixer-2 cleaned by its own internal codex loop. T15/T16/T17 marked completed. Pending T13 (M2 codex fixes), T14 (M3 codex fixes) still held. Plan-splitter, m1-hardener, highlighter-explorer, codex-fixer-1, clippy-cleaner still in flight. |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:5798:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:1864:+- **No outbox cache TTL or staleness check.** Once `MailboxesModel` has emitted, only `update$`/`insert$` for that specific kind:10002 changes it. There's no periodic refresh.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:5799:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:2258:+Search confirmed: no code in `core/` watches `session.followSet`, watches any "follows changed" event, or mutates `subscription.filters.authors` on the fly. Filters are immutable after `ndk.subscribe()` returns; the only post-creation mutation is `refreshRelayConnections` adding relays (see Mechanism 3 below).
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:5800:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:2333:+        if (isRelevant && typeof subscription.refreshRelayConnections === "function") {
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:5801:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:2334:+            subscription.refreshRelayConnections();
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:5802:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:2340:+`refreshRelayConnections` (`subscription/index.ts:787-812`):
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:5803:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:2343:+public refreshRelayConnections(): void {
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:5804:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:2362:+- **Bypassed when `relaySet` is explicit** — if your subscription was opened with a fixed `relaySet`, no auto-refresh.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:5805:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:2373:+| Do open subscriptions auto-add relays when an author's NIP-65 list arrives? | Yes, via `refreshRelayConnections` | `core/src/ndk/index.ts:459-471`; `core/src/subscription/index.ts:787-812` |
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:5806:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:2392:+- Core NDK: filters are immutable post-`subscribe()`. There is no follow-list watcher. The closest mechanism (`outboxTracker.on("user:relay-list-updated", …) → refreshRelayConnections`) only adds *relays*, not *authors*.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:5807:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:2460:+### No background-refresh API
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:5808:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:2462:+There's no scheduled "tick" or "fetch since last seen" helper. NMP needs to build its own background-refresh loop using `since: lastSeen` filters.
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:5809:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:2644:+## Live subscription refresh
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:5810:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:2653:+        if (isRelevant && typeof subscription.refreshRelayConnections === "function") {
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:5811:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:2654:+            subscription.refreshRelayConnections();
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:5812:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:2660:+`refreshRelayConnections()` (`subscription/index.ts:787-812`) recomputes the relay map via `calculateRelaySetsFromFilters` and **only adds** newly-discovered relays. It does not remove relays or change `filter.authors`. This is critical for understanding the limits of the auto-rewire (see kind3-auto-tracking.md).

docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:5827:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:3496:    76	- **Parallel-agent orchestration.** Multiple specialized subagents work in isolated git worktrees in parallel. docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:5839:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:3967:    19	- **Kind:3 auto-tracking** for the active account's follow list. When a kind:3 lands for the active account, the planner automatically refreshes the author-set of every subscription that depends on "the current user's follows". The app never re-issues the REQ — the framework recompiles transparently.

docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:5844:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:4048:README.md:35:- **Outbox routing is automatic by default** — `nmp-core::planner` resolves author write relays + recipient inbox relays on every publish; explicit override is an audited opt-out. (Stronger than NDK's caller-responsibility model and Applesauce's caller-responsibility model.)
docs/perf/codex-reviews/810d0f8.md:5845:docs/perf/codex-reviews/51120cb.md:4132:   129	Per-pubkey relay lists are fetched lazily via a gossip layer, cached in a swappable backend (in-memory or SQLite), and refreshed when a fresher kind-10002 arrives. When a user's outbox changes, dependent subscriptions automatically re-resolve their relay sets.

docs/design/subscription-compilation/diagnostics.md:121:    /// Kernel-configured indexer relay (e.g. purplepag.es). This is the sub-category that

docs/design/subscription-compilation/diagnostics.md:123:    /// set is a policy choice that lives inside lane 4 (User-configured). D3: the operator
docs/design/subscription-compilation/diagnostics.md:124:    /// configured the indexer set; the kernel applies it as policy for reads when NIP-65
docs/design/subscription-compilation/diagnostics.md:125:    /// mailboxes are unknown. Never for writes.

docs/design/subscription-compilation/intro.md:30:/// A logical interest is what a kernel-side consumer (view, action, monitor,
docs/design/subscription-compilation/intro.md:31:/// sync job, pointer loader) wants alive on the wire. The compiler turns N
docs/design/subscription-compilation/intro.md:32:/// logical interests into M ≤ N per-relay plans.
docs/design/subscription-compilation/intro.md:35:    /// Stable identity assigned by the registry on first insertion. Survives
docs/design/subscription-compilation/intro.md:36:    /// recompilation. Two interests with identical content collide on hash but
docs/design/subscription-compilation/intro.md:37:    /// keep distinct ids if they were registered by distinct claims.
docs/design/subscription-compilation/intro.md:40:    /// Scope decides how mailbox resolution and indexer fallback behave.
docs/design/subscription-compilation/intro.md:41:    /// Account-scoped interests resolve via the active account's mailbox view
docs/design/subscription-compilation/intro.md:42:    /// for ambiguous filters (e.g. interests with no `authors` and no `#p`).
docs/design/subscription-compilation/intro.md:45:    /// What the consumer wants. This is a normalised filter set, not a Nostr
docs/design/subscription-compilation/intro.md:46:    /// wire filter. Tags use sorted vec representation so equality is stable.
docs/design/subscription-compilation/intro.md:49:    /// Routing hints the consumer wants honoured. The compiler may ignore
docs/design/subscription-compilation/intro.md:50:    /// hints if they conflict with policy (e.g. private-publish privacy).
docs/design/subscription-compilation/intro.md:53:    /// Lifecycle: tailing means "stay open after EOSE"; one_shot closes on
docs/design/subscription-compilation/intro.md:54:    /// EOSE. Window is the planner's intent, not necessarily the relay
docs/design/subscription-compilation/intro.md:55:    /// `since`/`until` it ends up emitting.
docs/design/subscription-compilation/intro.md:60:    /// Bound to the active account in SessionState. Re-routes on account switch.
docs/design/subscription-compilation/intro.md:62:    /// Bound to a specific account (multi-account UIs, M8). Re-routes on that
docs/design/subscription-compilation/intro.md:63:    /// account's mailbox refresh; ignored on account switch.
docs/design/subscription-compilation/intro.md:65:    /// No account context. Used for global pointer loaders, NIP-19-driven
docs/design/subscription-compilation/intro.md:66:    /// fetches, and indexer-direct probes.
docs/design/subscription-compilation/intro.md:97:    /// Translate a view spec into the logical interests required to keep it
docs/design/subscription-compilation/intro.md:98:    /// live. Called by the planner when the view opens; re-called on
docs/design/subscription-compilation/intro.md:99:    /// recompilation triggers that the view module opts into (account-switch,
docs/design/subscription-compilation/intro.md:100:    /// mailbox refresh).
docs/design/subscription-compilation/outbox.md:24:    /// Compute the relay set for publishing a signed event. Pure (no side
docs/design/subscription-compilation/outbox.md:25:    /// effects); the caller (action ledger) feeds the result into the
docs/design/subscription-compilation/outbox.md:26:    /// per-relay publish state machine.
docs/design/subscription-compilation/outbox.md:38:    /// Ledger acceptance threshold: `usize` (not `u8`) so large recipient lists
docs/design/subscription-compilation/outbox.md:39:    /// cannot overflow. For public events this is `max(1, ceil(N/3))`; for private
docs/design/subscription-compilation/outbox.md:40:    /// this equals the per-recipient delivery requirements count.
docs/design/subscription-compilation/outbox.md:42:    /// For `PrivateToRecipients`: one entry per recipient with their specific
docs/design/subscription-compilation/outbox.md:43:    /// inbox relay set and success threshold. The ledger fails the publish if
docs/design/subscription-compilation/outbox.md:44:    /// any recipient has zero successful deliveries.
docs/design/subscription-compilation/outbox.md:76:    /// Public events (kind:1, kind:0, kind:3, kind:10002, kind:7, ...).
docs/design/subscription-compilation/outbox.md:77:    /// Falls back to indexer set if author has no write relays.
docs/design/subscription-compilation/outbox.md:79:    /// Private/gift-wrapped events (kind:1059 wrapping NIP-44). Fails closed
docs/design/subscription-compilation/outbox.md:80:    /// if any recipient has no inbox relays.
docs/design/subscription-compilation/outbox.md:82:    /// Notifications (kind:1 with `#p` tags, reactions, zaps, replies that
docs/design/subscription-compilation/outbox.md:83:    /// the author wants to surface to the tagged pubkey). Combines author
docs/design/subscription-compilation/outbox.md:84:    /// write + each `#p` inbox.

docs/design/subscription-compilation/nip65.md:154:    /// in the same second must not silently disagree on the stored version.

    77	    /// Falls back to indexer set if author has no write relays.
    78	    Public,
    79	    /// Private/gift-wrapped events (kind:1059 wrapping NIP-44). Fails closed
    80	    /// if any recipient has no inbox relays.
    81	    PrivateToRecipients { recipients: Vec<Pubkey> },
    82	    /// Notifications (kind:1 with `#p` tags, reactions, zaps, replies that
    83	    /// the author wants to surface to the tagged pubkey). Combines author
    84	    /// write + each `#p` inbox.
    85	    PublicWithNotifications { notify: Vec<Pubkey> },
    86	}
    87	
    88	#[derive(Clone, Debug)]
    89	pub enum PublishPlanError {
    90	    NoAuthorRelays { author: Pubkey, lane_facts: ByLaneCounts },
    91	    PrivateRecipientUnroutable { recipient: Pubkey },
    92	    OverrideRejected { reason: String },
    93	}
    94	```
    95	
    96	The trait is consumed by the action ledger (per `docs/design/kernel-substrate.md` §4 — the kernel owns "per-relay publish attempts" provenance). When an `ActionModule::reduce` reaches its publish step, it calls the planner, gets a `PublishPlan`, and the kernel fans out to relays with the standard ledger-correlated retry/cancel semantics.
    97

   104	    pub mailbox_cache:    &'a dyn MailboxCache,
   105	    pub user_configured:  &'a UserConfiguredRelays,

    90	@@ -0,0 +1,43 @@
    91	+# Design: Subscription Compilation + Outbox Routing (M2)
    92	+
    93	+> **Status:** Draft (M2 design + impl-prep).
    94	+> **Date:** 2026-05-18.
    95	+> **Companion docs:** `docs/plan.md` §M2; `docs/aim.md` §4.4–§4.5; `docs/design/ndk-applesauce-lessons.md` §7; ADR-0007 (diagnostic lanes); `docs/design/kernel-substrate.md` §3 (`ViewModule`) and §4 (`ActionModule`); `docs/product-spec/subsystems.md` §7.2–§7.3.
    96	+> **Scope:** Replace the "hardcoded two-role relay set" planner in `crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/{requests,ingest,mod}.rs` with a **subscription compilation stage** that turns logical interests into per-relay plans driven by NIP-65 mailboxes, and graduates outbox routing to a first-class planner subsystem. v1 is in-memory; M3 plugs it into LMDB. This is a design doc; no implementation lands in this PR.
    97	+
    98	+This document is split into focused sub-files to stay under the 500 LOC ceiling (`AGENTS.md`).
    99	+
   100	+- [Intro — problem statement + logical interest model](subscription-compilation/intro.md) (§1, §2)
   101	+- [Compiler — pipeline + plan-id contract + function migration table](subscription-compilation/compiler.md) (§3)
   102	+- [Recompilation — triggers and actor message shapes](subscription-compilation/recompilation.md) (§4)
   103	+- [Diagnostics — four-lane records + reverse-coverage view](subscription-compilation/diagnostics.md) (§5, §8)
   104	+- [nmp-nip65 — crate layout, traits, public surface](subscription-compilation/nip65.md) (§6)
   105	+- [Outbox — publish-planner seam + override action](subscription-compilation/outbox.md) (§7)
   106	+- [Tests — wire-frame audit gate](subscription-compilation/tests.md) (§9)
   107	+
   108	+## Section map
   109	+
   110	+| § | Topic | File |
   111	+|---|---|---|
   112	+| 1 | What is wrong with the current planner (cited line refs) | intro.md |
   113	+| 2 | Logical interest — formal definition + how views express them | intro.md |
   114	+| 3 | Compilation pipeline: authors → mailboxes → per-relay plans + plan-id | compiler.md |
   115	+| 4 | Recompilation triggers (kind:10002, view open/close, reconnect, account switch, manual, user-configured change) | recompilation.md |
   116	+| 5 | Four-lane diagnostic records (NIP-65 / hint / provenance / user-configured) | diagnostics.md |
   117	+| 6 | `nmp-nip65` file layout, `MailboxesViewModule`, public surface | nip65.md |
   118	+| 7 | `PublishPlanner` trait, write fan-out policy, override + debug warning | outbox.md |
   119	+| 8 | Reverse-relay-coverage diagnostic view ("this relay serves N authors of our timeline") | diagnostics.md |
   120	+| 9 | M2 exit-gate audit test path + assertions | tests.md |
   121	+| 10 | Open questions for follow-up ADRs | this file (below) |
   122	+
   123	+## 10. Open questions
   124	+
   125	+These remain to be resolved by ADRs after design review, not in this design pass.
   126	+
   127	+1. **Plan-id stability under perturbation.** The intro/compiler picks "logical-interest set + author-mailbox snapshot ⇒ plan-id." That ties plan-id to mailbox membership, so a single new kind:10002 arrival reshuffles plan-ids for every interest including that author. An alternative scopes plan-id to the *logical-interest set only* and tracks per-relay assignment as a separate stable identifier. Pick one in an ADR; the test contracts in §9 assume the former.
   128	+2. **Filter-merge lattice formal-isation.** §3 step 3 lists which filter fields are safely mergeable across logical interests (`authors`, `kinds`, `since`, `until`, `limit`, tag operators). It does not yet specify the merge algebra for the corner case where two interests differ only in `limit` but agree on authors and kinds. Worth an ADR-level note before the lattice is coded; `docs/product-spec/subsystems.md` §7.2 promises "a formal merge lattice for `limit`, `since`, `until`, multi-filter arrays, and tag operators."
   129	+3. **Per-author indexer-fallback ledger row?** Today the compiler treats indexer fallback as an inline relay assignment. If the kind:10002 fetch is its own durable action (M6 ledger), the fallback becomes a tracked action with retry/cancel semantics. Cleaner for diagnostics; heavier for M2. Defer.
   130	+4. **Read-relay vs write-relay use for subscriptions on the same author.** NIP-65 defines write relays (outbox) for the author's own events and read relays (inbox) for events directed *at* the author. For a `Timeline { authors: [...] }` we want write relays. For a `Notifications { p: [author] }` we want inbox relays. The compiler distinguishes them by filter shape (`authors` vs `#p`), matching the `docs/product-spec/subsystems.md` §7.3 routing table. Document a corner case: kind-1 filtered by both `authors` and `#p` is rare but real (replies to the author from the author). Pick a precedence in an ADR; current bias is `authors` wins (write relays).
   131	+5. **User-configured relay precedence vs NIP-65.** A user adds `wss://my-private.example` to local config. Does it *augment* (union) or *override* (replace) NIP-65 routing for the active account? `subsystems.md` §7.3 default-resolves by NIP-65; user-configured is "fallback" in the indexer sense. ADR needs to spell out the augment/override question for the active account specifically.
   132	+6. **Auth-paused relays in compiled plans.** If a relay is in `RelayAuthState::ChallengeReceived`, the compiler still produces a plan that assigns interests to it (so reconnect-after-auth resumes correctly), but emission must pause. Is the pause modeled inside the compiler (per-relay gate) or inside the wire-emitter (consumes plans, applies pause)? Bias: wire-emitter, but the compiler must surface the pause as a fact for `LogicalInterestStatus`. Resolve before M5.
   133	+7. **NSE crate compilation surface.** `nmp-nip17-nse` (M9) runs in iOS Notification Service Extension with bounded memory; it needs to compile a single-author single-relay plan without the full planner. Confirm in an ADR that the compiler exposes a `compile_one(spec, mailbox_cache_snapshot) -> Plan` pure function suitable for NSE use, and that the function does not require a live actor.
   134	diff --git a/docs/design/subscription-compilation/compiler.md b/docs/design/subscription-compilation/compiler.md
   135	new file mode 100644
   136	index 0000000..21a4aa7
   137	--- /dev/null
   138	+++ b/docs/design/subscription-compilation/compiler.md
   139	@@ -0,0 +1,193 @@
   140	+# Subscription Compilation §3 — The Compilation Pipeline
   141	+
   142	+> Parent: `docs/design/subscription-compilation.md`.
   143	+> Read first: [intro.md](intro.md) for the `LogicalInterest` shape this stage consumes.
   144	+
   145	+The compiler is a pure function plus a small amount of state (the mailbox cache and the active plan registry). It runs whenever a recompilation trigger fires (§4) and produces a `CompiledPlan` that the wire-emitter applies as a diff against the relay sockets.
   146	+
   147	+## 3.0 Pipeline overview
   148	+
   149	+```
   150	+   logical_interests:                 mailbox_cache + relay_config
   151	+   Vec<LogicalInterest>                       │
   152	+            │                                 │
   153	+            ▼                                 ▼
   154	+   ┌───────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
   155	+   │ Stage 1: Resolve authors → mailboxes              │  (§3.1)
   156	+   │   each author → { write, read, both, missing }    │
   157	+   └───────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
   158	+            │
   159	+            ▼
   160	+   ┌───────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
   161	+   │ Stage 2: Indexer fallback for missing mailboxes   │  (§3.2)
   162	+   │   missing → enqueue kind:10002 probe              │
   163	+   │   missing-author reads → indexer set (read only)  │
   164	+   └───────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
   165	+            │
   166	+            ▼
   167	+   ┌───────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
   168	+   │ Stage 3: Per-relay shape merge                    │  (§3.3)
   169	+   │   group interests by target relay URL             │
   170	+   │   merge compatible shapes inside each relay       │
   171	+   │   refuse merges that would change semantics       │
   172	+   └───────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
   173	+            │
   174	+            ▼
   175	+   ┌───────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
   176	+   │ Stage 4: Plan-id binding                          │  (§3.4)
   177	+   │   compute plan_id = hash(interest_set,            │
   178	+   │                          mailbox_snapshot,        │
   179	+   │                          merge_lattice_version)   │
   180	+   │   stable across no-op recompilations              │
   181	+   └───────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
   182	+            │
   183	+            ▼
   184	+   CompiledPlan { plan_id, per_relay: Vec<RelayPlan> }
   185	+```
   186	+
   187	+The wire-emitter (`crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/wire.rs`, to be added) diffs the new plan against the current wire-sub registry: opens new REQs, closes orphaned ones, leaves stable assignments untouched.
   188	+
   189	+## 3.1 Stage 1 — Resolve authors to mailboxes
   190	+
   191	+Inputs: every `LogicalInterest` with non-empty `shape.authors` or non-empty `shape.tags[#p]`; the mailbox cache populated by `ingest_relay_list` (`crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/ingest.rs:209-233`).
   192	+
   193	+Output: an `AuthorRouting` per author per direction:
   194	+
   195	+```rust
   196	+pub struct AuthorRouting {
   197	+    pub author: Pubkey,
   198	+    pub direction: RoutingDirection,        // Outbox or Inbox
   199	+    pub relays: BTreeSet<RelayUrl>,         // resolved write/read/both
   200	+    pub source: RoutingSource,              // Nip65 | UserConfigured | Indexer | Hint
   201	+    pub freshness_ms: Option<u64>,          // age of the kind:10002 record
   202	+}
   203	+
   204	+pub enum RoutingDirection {
   205	+    Outbox,    // for `authors:` filters — the author's *write* relays
   206	+    Inbox,     // for `#p:` filters    — the tagged author's *read* relays
   207	+}
   208	+```
   209	+
   210	+Direction is decided by the interest's filter shape per `docs/product-spec/subsystems.md` §7.3:
   211	+
   212	+| Interest shape | Direction | Source per author |
   213	+|---|---|---|
   214	+| Non-empty `authors`, no `#p` | Outbox | author's `write_relays ∪ both_relays` |
   215	+| Empty `authors`, non-empty `#p` | Inbox | tagged author's `read_relays ∪ both_relays` |
   216	+| Both populated | Outbox primarily; Inbox interests split (see §3.3) | both |
   217	+| Neither populated | (handled by stage 3 as "use active-account read relays") | — |
   218	+
   219	+`docs/product-spec/subsystems.md` §7.3 specifies one explicit override: DMs (NIP-17 gift-wraps, M9) fail closed if recipient inbox relays are missing. The compiler enforces this by refusing to produce a plan for an interest tagged `privacy = FailClosed` if any tagged-pubkey inbox lookup returns `Indexer` source. §7 details the publish-side enforcement.
   220	+
