     1	# Subscription Compilation §3 — The Compilation Pipeline
     2	
     3	> Parent: `docs/design/subscription-compilation.md`.
     4	> Read first: [intro.md](intro.md) for the `LogicalInterest` shape this stage consumes.
     5	
     6	The compiler is a pure function plus a small amount of state (the mailbox cache and the active plan registry). It runs whenever a recompilation trigger fires (§4) and produces a `CompiledPlan` that the wire-emitter applies as a diff against the relay sockets.
     7	
     8	## 3.0 Pipeline overview
     9	
    10	```
    11	   logical_interests:                 mailbox_cache + relay_config
    12	   Vec<LogicalInterest>                       │
    13	            │                                 │
    14	            ▼                                 ▼
    15	   ┌───────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
    16	   │ Stage 1: Resolve authors → mailboxes              │  (§3.1)
    17	   │   each author → { write, read, both, missing }    │
    18	   └───────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
    19	            │
    20	            ▼
    21	   ┌───────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
    22	   │ Stage 2: Indexer fallback for missing mailboxes   │  (§3.2)
    23	   │   missing → enqueue kind:10002 probe              │
    24	   │   missing-author reads → indexer set (read only)  │
    25	   └───────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
    26	            │
    27	            ▼
    28	   ┌───────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
    29	   │ Stage 3: Per-relay shape merge                    │  (§3.3)
    30	   │   group interests by target relay URL             │
    31	   │   merge compatible shapes inside each relay       │
    32	   │   refuse merges that would change semantics       │
    33	   └───────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
    34	            │
    35	            ▼
    36	   ┌───────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
    37	   │ Stage 4: Plan-id binding                          │  (§3.4)
    38	   │   compute plan_id = hash(interest_set,            │
    39	   │                          mailbox_snapshot,        │
    40	   │                          merge_lattice_version)   │
    41	   │   stable across no-op recompilations              │
    42	   └───────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
    43	            │
    44	            ▼
    45	   CompiledPlan { plan_id, per_relay: Vec<RelayPlan> }
    46	```
    47	
    48	The wire-emitter (`crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/wire.rs`, to be added) diffs the new plan against the current wire-sub registry: opens new REQs, closes orphaned ones, leaves stable assignments untouched.
    49	
    50	## 3.1 Stage 1 — Resolve authors to mailboxes
    51	
    52	Inputs: every `LogicalInterest` with non-empty `shape.authors` or non-empty `shape.tags[#p]`; the mailbox cache populated by `ingest_relay_list` (`crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/ingest.rs:209-233`).
    53	
    54	Output: an `AuthorRouting` per author per direction:
    55	
    56	```rust
    57	pub struct AuthorRouting {
    58	    pub author: Pubkey,
    59	    pub direction: RoutingDirection,        // Outbox or Inbox
    60	    pub relays: BTreeSet<RelayUrl>,         // resolved write/read/both
    61	    pub source: RoutingSource,              // Nip65 | UserConfigured | Hint
    62	    // Note: there is no RoutingSource::Indexer variant — indexer fallback is
    63	    // modeled as UserConfigured { category: Indexer } (see diagnostics.md §5.0).
    64	    // This keeps the four-lane discipline strict: indexers are a sub-category of
    65	    // user-configured, not a fifth lane.
    66	    pub freshness_ms: Option<u64>,          // age of the kind:10002 record
    67	}
    68	
    69	pub enum RoutingDirection {
    70	    Outbox,    // for `authors:` filters — the author's *write* relays
    71	    Inbox,     // for `#p:` filters    — the tagged author's *read* relays
    72	}
    73	```
    74	
    75	Direction is decided by the interest's filter shape per `docs/product-spec/subsystems.md` §7.3:
    76	
    77	| Interest shape | Direction | Source per author |
    78	|---|---|---|
    79	| Non-empty `authors`, no `#p` | Outbox | author's `write_relays ∪ both_relays` |
    80	| Empty `authors`, non-empty `#p` | Inbox | tagged author's `read_relays ∪ both_relays` |
    81	| Both populated | Outbox primarily; Inbox interests split (see §3.3) | both |
    82	| Neither populated | (handled by stage 3 as "use active-account read relays") | — |
    83	
    84	`docs/product-spec/subsystems.md` §7.3 specifies one explicit override: DMs (NIP-17 gift-wraps, M9) fail closed if recipient inbox relays are missing. The compiler enforces this by refusing to produce a plan for an interest tagged `privacy = FailClosed` if any tagged-pubkey inbox lookup returns `Indexer` source. §7 details the publish-side enforcement.
    85	
    86	## 3.2 Stage 2 — Indexer fallback for unknown mailboxes
    87	
    88	The indexer set is a kernel-configured `Vec<RelayUrl>` (default: a small curated list; user-configurable in `AppConfig`). Today's `crates/nmp-core/src/relay.rs:2` is the placeholder for one indexer relay (`purplepag.es`); the v1 indexer set lives in `AppConfig.indexer_relays`.
    89	
    90	Two distinct behaviours:
    91	
    92	1. **Mailbox probe.** For every author with `mailbox_cache.get(author) == None`, the compiler emits a `IndexerProbe { author }` side effect on the plan. The probe registers as its own short-lived `LogicalInterest { shape: { kinds: [10002], authors: [author], limit: 1 }, lifecycle: OneShot, scope: Global }`. Recompilation triggers (§4 trigger A1) re-route the original interest once the kind:10002 lands.
    93	2. **Read fallback.** For a `RoutingDirection::Outbox` interest whose author has no known mailboxes, the compiler routes the interest to the indexer set **as read-only fallback**. Per `docs/product-spec/subsystems.md` §7.3 line 99: "fall back to indexer set for reads only; do not publish to indexers." The resulting `AuthorRouting` carries `source: RoutingSource::UserConfigured` with a `UserConfiguredRelayFact { category: UserConfiguredCategory::Indexer }` record flowing to the diagnostic surface. The four-lane view (§5) renders "author X is being served by indexer Y because we have no mailbox for them" by examining the `category` subcategory — the Indexer is a sub-category of lane 4 (User-configured), not a fifth lane.
    94	
    95	Bounded: a single author's indexer probe is enqueued at most once per `compiler_probe_window_secs` (default 60s) to prevent thundering-herd probes if a screen of N rows all claim the same unknown pubkey.
    96	
    97	## 3.3 Stage 3 — Per-relay shape merge
    98	
    99	After Stage 1, every interest has one or more `(relay_url, sub_shape)` assignments. Stage 3 groups by relay URL and merges shapes where merging preserves semantics.
   100	
   101	### Merge rules (the lattice)
   102	
   103	Two `InterestShape`s `A` and `B` are **mergeable on relay R** iff:
   104	
   105	1. `A.kinds == B.kinds` **or** one is empty (wildcard absorbs).
   106	2. `A.tags.keys() == B.tags.keys()` (same tag dimensions) **and** the union of values per dimension stays under the relay's per-filter limit (default 1000).
   107	3. `A.since` and `B.since`: merged `since = min(A.since, B.since)` *only if* both are present or both absent. Mixing a bounded interest with an unbounded one is **not** merged (would broaden the bounded one's window).
   108	4. `A.until` and `B.until`: same rule, mirror of (3) with `max`.
   109	5. `A.limit` and `B.limit`: mergeable iff both are absent. If either has a `limit`, **do not merge** — broadening would mask the limit's intent.
   110	6. `A.lifecycle == B.lifecycle`. Tailing and one-shot do not merge (one-shot would never close).
   111	7. `A.event_ids` and `B.event_ids`: merge by union, capped at the relay's per-filter `ids` limit.
   112	
   113	When mergeable, the merged shape is `{ authors: A.authors ∪ B.authors, ... }`. The merged interest tracks both originating `InterestId`s so per-event dispatch back to consumers stays correct.
   114	
   115	When not mergeable, the two interests get distinct sub-shapes on the same relay, producing two distinct REQs. That is fine and expected.
   116	
   117	Open question 2 in the parent index (`subscription-compilation.md`) covers the `limit`-only corner case formally.
   118	
   119	The NMP merge lattice is simpler than Applesauce's `selectOptimalRelays` greedy set-cover
   120	(`docs/research/applesauce/outbox.md` §3, `relay-selection.ts:14-93`): Applesauce optimizes
   121	the number of relay connections by picking a minimum covering set across all contacts. NMP's
   122	Stage 3 merges shapes per relay but does not eliminate relays — the set-cover optimization
   123	(capping to `maxConnections`) is a future extension. For M2, every declared write relay gets
   124	a REQ; relay-count optimization is open question 8 (future ADR).
   125	
   126	### Per-relay output
   127	
   128	```rust
   129	pub struct RelayPlan {
   130	    pub relay_url: RelayUrl,
   131	    pub role_tags: BTreeSet<RoutingSource>,   // why this relay is in the plan
   132	    pub sub_shapes: Vec<SubShape>,            // each emits one REQ
   133	}
   134	
   135	pub struct SubShape {
   136	    pub shape: InterestShape,                  // canonical, post-merge
   137	    pub originating_interests: Vec<InterestId>,
   138	    pub canonical_filter_hash: String,         // for ADR-0007 WireSubscriptionStatus
   139	}
   140	```
   141	
   142	The wire-emitter renders each `SubShape` as exactly one `REQ` on `relay_url` with a sub-id of `c{plan_id}-r{relay_idx}-s{shape_idx}`. The sub-id is meaningful only to the kernel; diagnostics use `canonical_filter_hash` for stable identity across re-emission.
   143	
   144	## 3.4 Stage 4 — Plan-id binding
   145	
   146	`plan_id` is the **stable identity** the platform observes for diagnostic continuity. It answers: "did this recompilation actually change anything observable?"
   147	
   148	Definition: **hash only mailboxes referenced by the current interest set**, not the whole
   149	cache. This resolves open question 1 in the parent index — choosing this scope means that
   150	an unrelated author's kind:10002 arriving does not churn plan-ids for unrelated interests.
   151	
   152	```
   153	// referenced_pubkeys = union of all shape.authors and shape.tags[#p] across interest_set
   154	referenced_pubkeys = interest_set.iter()
   155	    .flat_map(|i| i.shape.authors.iter().chain(i.shape.tags.get("#p").unwrap_or(&[])))
   156	    .collect::<BTreeSet<Pubkey>>();
   157	
   158	plan_id = blake3(
   159	    sorted(interest_set.iter().map(|i| (i.id, i.shape, i.scope, i.lifecycle))),
   160	    sorted(referenced_pubkeys.iter().filter_map(|pk| mailbox_cache.get(pk))
   161	              .map(|ml| (ml.pubkey, ml.created_at, sorted(ml.write), sorted(ml.read)))),
   162	    INDEXER_SET_VERSION,
   163	    USER_CONFIGURED_RELAYS_VERSION,
   164	    MERGE_LATTICE_VERSION,
   165	)
   166	```
   167	
   168	Properties:
   169	
   170	- **Recompilation with no change ⇒ same plan-id.** If `ingest_relay_list` (`crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/ingest.rs:218-221`) deduplicates and decides not to replace a stale mailbox, no plan-id churn.
   171	- **Adding an interest changes plan-id even if no new wire REQ results.** Two interests can merge into the same SubShape; the plan-id changes because the *interest set* changed. The platform diagnostic correctly reports "logical-interest count went up; wire-sub count did not."
   172	- **A new kind:10002 for a referenced author changes plan-id.** The hash only covers mailboxes for authors the current interest set touches, so a kind:10002 for an unrelated pubkey (not in any active interest's `authors` or `#p`) does not churn plan-ids.
   173	- **A new kind:10002 for an unreferenced author does NOT change plan-id.** This is the key
   174	  difference from hashing the whole snapshot. The wire-emitter's diff is unaffected. This
   175	  property is critical for D8 (reactivity contract — §1.5): recompilation work must be bounded
   176	  by what's open, not by the size of the total mailbox cache. Hashing only referenced mailboxes
   177	  ensures recompile cost scales with `|interest_set| × |referenced_authors|`, not with the
   178	  entire cache.
   179	- **Indexer set change changes plan-id.** Operator config edits surface immediately.
   180	
   181	The `plan_id` is stored on `CompiledPlan` and rendered into `LogicalInterestStatus` (extending the record at `crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/mod.rs:147-154` with `plan_id: String, plan_generation: u64`). Tests in §9 assert plan-id stability across no-op recompilations.
   182	
   183	## 3.5 Migration of existing functions
   184	
   185	This is the binding contract: each function in `crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/requests.rs` and `crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/ingest.rs` either disappears, becomes thin glue over the compiler, or graduates into a typed module. The compiler does not coexist with the old planner; M2 replaces it.
   186	
   187	| Current function (file:line) | M2 replacement |
   188	|---|---|
   189	| `startup_requests` (requests.rs:50-106) | **Relocated outside `nmp-core` per D0.** The seed timeline / kind:0 / kind:10002 / kind:3 bootstrap is social graph knowledge — not kernel substrate. It moves to `nmp-nip01` / `nmp-nip02` / the demo app's startup sequence. `nmp-core` only executes interests registered by modules; it does not hard-code which interests those are. The TEST_PUBKEY-specific bootstrap (line 71-82) moves to the demo app (or a test fixture in `nmp-testing`). The kernel's `ActorStart` handler calls `compiler.recompile(Trigger::Startup)`, which is a no-op until a module registers interests. |
   190	| `open_author` (requests.rs:118-140) | Registers three `LogicalInterest`s scoped `ActiveAccount` (kind:10002, kind:0, kinds:1+6 for author); calls `compiler.recompile(Trigger::ViewOpen)`. Refcount stays — but it lives on `InterestId` now, not on `ViewInterest { key, refcount }`. The `can_send` gate disappears: the compiler always produces a plan; the wire-emitter is the only thing that may queue deferrals. |
   191	| `open_thread` (requests.rs:142-168) | Registers a `Thread { event_id }` view-module spec; the view module returns interests with `event_ids` and `#e`-tag shapes. Hydration cascade in `prepare_thread_requests` (requests.rs:441-466) becomes part of the view module's `reduce` returning new interests when new event ids surface. |
   192	| `open_firehose_tag` (requests.rs:170-200) | Registers one `LogicalInterest { shape: { kinds: [1], tags: { #t: [tag] } }, scope: ActiveAccount, lifecycle: Tailing }`. Routes to active-account read relays per §3.1 table. |
   193	| `claim_profile` / `release_profile` (requests.rs:202-263) | Registers/unregisters one `LogicalInterest { shape: { authors: [pk], kinds: [0], limit: 1 }, lifecycle: OneShot }` per claim. Refcount of distinct consumers becomes the `InterestId` claim set inside the registry. **Dedup of (pk, kinds=[0]) across N timeline rows yields one merged SubShape and one wire REQ** — this is what bug-extinction "1000 avatars do not produce 1000 REQs" verifies. The refcount must be an integer counter, not a boolean presence flag — Applesauce gotcha `75ef7d5f` (`docs/research/applesauce/gotchas.md` §G2) shows the boolean model causes leaks when two consumers share a claim and one unsubscribes. |
   194	| `close_author` / `close_thread` (requests.rs:265-311) | Drop interests by `InterestId`; recompile with `Trigger::ViewClose`. Wire-emitter closes orphaned REQs. The "warm-close" grace from the view-warmth doctrine (`docs/design/kernel-substrate.md` §3 "lifecycle") is the compiler's, not the view's — interests stay registered for the warmth window after their last claim. |
   195	| `close_subscriptions_with_prefixes` (requests.rs:313-331) | **Deleted.** The wire-emitter closes by `WireSubId`, which is the compiler's diff output. String-prefix matching of sub-ids is a 2026-05-period scaffold that the compiler removes. |
   196	| `pending_view_requests` (requests.rs:333-355) | Becomes `compiler.flush_deferred_for_relay(role, url)`: called when a relay reconnects (§4 trigger A3). The compiler resubmits its current plan against that relay's slot. |
   197	| `firehose_requests` (requests.rs:357-372) | Replaced as described above for `open_firehose_tag`. The `diag-firehose-N` sub-id scheme goes away — `canonical_filter_hash` plus `plan_id` give stable identity. |
   198	| `pending_profile_claim_requests` (requests.rs:374-388) | Disappears. Claims are interests; the compiler is the only thing that decides "this interest needs a REQ." |
   199	| `profile_claim_request` (requests.rs:390-402) | Disappears. The compiler routes claimed-profile interests through Stage 1; indexer fallback (Stage 2) handles the no-mailbox case. |
   200	| `author_requests` (requests.rs:404-439) | Disappears (replaced by `open_author`'s interest registration). |
   201	| `prepare_thread_requests` / `enqueue_thread_*` / `maybe_open_thread_hydration` (requests.rs:441-528) | Move to a `ThreadViewModule` in `nmp-nip10`. The hydration cascade is `view_module.reduce(...)` returning additional interests as new event ids surface in store. |
   202	| `req` (requests.rs:530-556) | **Deleted.** Replaced by the wire-emitter's `emit_req(relay_url, sub_id, filter)`. No call site outside the wire-emitter is permitted to construct a REQ. |
   203	| `defer_outbound` (requests.rs:558-568) | Moves to the wire-emitter; deferral is per-relay, keyed by URL, not by role. |
   204	| `ingest_relay_list` (ingest.rs:209-233) | Stays, but emits a `Trigger::Nip65Arrived { pubkey }` event (§4 trigger A1) on a material update. Becomes the producer side of the recompilation cycle. |
   205	| `ingest_profile` / `ingest_contacts` / `ingest_timeline_event` (ingest.rs:166-279) | Unchanged in storage shape. Their relevance to compilation is that they feed the view modules' projections (per `docs/design/reactivity/view-deltas-and-projections.md`). |
   206	| `should_store_event` (ingest.rs:268-279) | Unchanged. Per-sub-id string filtering goes away when sub-ids become `c{plan}-r{relay}-s{shape}`, but the predicate switches to "is this event id covered by an active interest?" — a `compiler.is_covered(event)` call. |
   207	| `maybe_open_timeline` (ingest.rs:329-365) | The "seed-contacts arrive → open union timeline" logic moves to `nmp-nip02` (follows/contacts module), not to an `nmp-core` helper. D0: `nmp-core` must not know the social graph. The contacts module watches the kind:3 projection and registers a `Timeline { authors: union }` interest on behalf of whichever view asked for it. |
   208	
   209	What this migration does **not** do (deferred per parent index open questions 3, 6, 7):
   210	
   211	- It does not move the action ledger into M2 — `SendNote` lands in M6.
   212	- It does not implement LMDB persistence for the mailbox cache — M3.
   213	- It does not implement NIP-77 watermarks — M4.
   214	- It does not add a per-author indexer-fallback ledger row — open question 3.
   215	- It does not move social bootstrap (follow-list timeline, account kind:0/3/10002 fetches)
   216	  into `nmp-core` — those are module concerns (D0). The impl PR for M2 must register those
   217	  interests from `nmp-nip01`/`nmp-nip02` or the demo app, not from the kernel actor directly.
   218	
   219	The compiler is **in-memory v1** by design. The mailbox cache is the existing `HashMap<String, AuthorRelayList>` (`crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/mod.rs:313`); it just gets a new consumer.

codex
I found one concrete leftover contradiction already: the publish privacy sketch still says public publishes fall back to indexers, even though the algorithm now removes the indexer argument. I’m checking for the same stale `RoutingSource::Indexer` shape elsewhere before finalizing the review.
exec
/bin/zsh -lc "nl -ba docs/design/subscription-compilation/outbox.md | sed -n '1,220p'" in /Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform
 succeeded in 0ms:
     1	# Subscription Compilation §7 — Outbox Routing as a Planner Subsystem
     2	
     3	> Parent: `docs/design/subscription-compilation.md`.
     4	> Read first: `docs/product-spec/subsystems.md` §7.3 (outbox routing algorithm); `docs/aim.md` §4.4 ("outbox / smart relay routing") and §6 doctrines 5 and 10.
     5	
     6	This section defines the **publish-side seam** the M2 milestone lands so the M6 write path has a ready surface. There is no publish code in the repo today (`crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/requests.rs` contains no `EVENT` outbound; the relay worker has no publish channel). M2 lands the trait and the override action; M6 writes the first concrete consumer (`SendNoteAction`).
     7	
     8	This is the framing the parent index calls out as "design seam now, first concrete consumer in M6." Without this seam, M6 risks reinventing outbox routing inline.
     9	
    10	Contrast with Applesauce: `ActionContext.publish(event, relays?)` passes outbox resolution
    11	responsibility to each action (`docs/research/applesauce/outbox.md` §7 lines 116-138). Any
    12	Applesauce action can pass any relay list. NMP's structural guarantee is stronger — D3
    13	(outbox routing automatic; `docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md` §1.5): no normal publish
    14	action carries a relay field, and `Nip65PublishPlanner` structurally excludes the indexer
    15	parameter from its publish resolution path.
    16	
    17	## 7.1 The `PublishPlanner` trait
    18	
    19	```rust
    20	// crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/planner/publish.rs (proposed)
    21	
    22	#[async_trait]
    23	pub trait PublishPlanner: Send + Sync {
    24	    /// Compute the relay set for publishing a signed event. Pure (no side
    25	    /// effects); the caller (action ledger) feeds the result into the
    26	    /// per-relay publish state machine.
    27	    fn plan_publish(
    28	        &self,
    29	        event: &Event,
    30	        privacy: PublishPrivacy,
    31	        override_: Option<PublishOverride>,
    32	    ) -> Result<PublishPlan, PublishPlanError>;
    33	}
    34	
    35	pub struct PublishPlan {
    36	    pub plan_id: PublishPlanId,                // hashes event coords + chosen relays
    37	    pub assignments: Vec<PublishAssignment>,
    38	    /// Ledger acceptance threshold: `usize` (not `u8`) so large recipient lists
    39	    /// cannot overflow. For public events this is `max(1, ceil(N/3))`; for private
    40	    /// this equals the per-recipient delivery requirements count.
    41	    pub required_success_count: usize,
    42	    /// For `PrivateToRecipients`: one entry per recipient with their specific
    43	    /// inbox relay set and success threshold. The ledger fails the publish if
    44	    /// any recipient has zero successful deliveries.
    45	    pub per_recipient: Vec<RecipientDelivery>,  // empty for Public/PublicWithNotifications
    46	    pub deadline_ms: u64,
    47	}
    48	
    49	pub struct RecipientDelivery {
    50	    pub recipient: Pubkey,
    51	    pub inbox_relays: BTreeSet<RelayUrl>,
    52	    pub required_success_count: usize,          // must be ≥ 1
    53	}
    54	
    55	pub struct PublishAssignment {
    56	    pub relay_url: RelayUrl,
    57	    pub reason: PublishRouteReason,            // which lane motivated this relay
    58	    pub privacy_role: PrivacyRole,             // for the audit log; not policy
    59	}
    60	
    61	pub enum PublishRouteReason {
    62	    AuthorWriteRelay  { lane: RelayFactLane },     // Nip65 or UserConfigured
    63	    RecipientInbox    { recipient: Pubkey, lane: RelayFactLane },
    64	    Hint              { source: HintSource },
    65	    Override          { audit: String },           // see §7.4
    66	}
    67	
    68	pub enum PrivacyRole {
    69	    Author,        // this relay is in the plan as the author's outbox
    70	    Recipient,     // this relay is in the plan as a recipient's inbox
    71	    Both,          // single relay is both
    72	}
    73	
    74	#[derive(Clone, Debug)]
    75	pub enum PublishPrivacy {
    76	    /// Public events (kind:1, kind:0, kind:3, kind:10002, kind:7, ...).
    77	    /// Falls back to indexer set if author has no write relays.
    78	    Public,
    79	    /// Private/gift-wrapped events (kind:1059 wrapping NIP-44). Fails closed
    80	    /// if any recipient has no inbox relays.
    81	    PrivateToRecipients { recipients: Vec<Pubkey> },
    82	    /// Notifications (kind:1 with `#p` tags, reactions, zaps, replies that
    83	    /// the author wants to surface to the tagged pubkey). Combines author
    84	    /// write + each `#p` inbox.
    85	    PublicWithNotifications { notify: Vec<Pubkey> },
    86	}
    87	
    88	#[derive(Clone, Debug)]
    89	pub enum PublishPlanError {
    90	    NoAuthorRelays { author: Pubkey, lane_facts: ByLaneCounts },
    91	    PrivateRecipientUnroutable { recipient: Pubkey },
    92	    OverrideRejected { reason: String },
    93	}
    94	```
    95	
    96	The trait is consumed by the action ledger (per `docs/design/kernel-substrate.md` §4 — the kernel owns "per-relay publish attempts" provenance). When an `ActionModule::reduce` reaches its publish step, it calls the planner, gets a `PublishPlan`, and the kernel fans out to relays with the standard ledger-correlated retry/cancel semantics.
    97	
    98	## 7.2 Default implementation: `Nip65PublishPlanner`
    99	
   100	```rust
   101	// crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/planner/publish_default.rs (proposed)
   102	
   103	pub struct Nip65PublishPlanner<'a> {
   104	    pub mailbox_cache:    &'a dyn MailboxCache,
   105	    pub user_configured:  &'a UserConfiguredRelays,
   106	    // intentionally no `indexer_set` field: publish NEVER falls back to indexers (D3).
   107	    // The indexer set is a read-only discovery mechanism (§3.2). Publishing to an
   108	    // indexer the author has not declared as a write relay violates D3 (outbox routing
   109	    // automatic — `docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md` §1.5) and the invariant at
   110	    // `docs/product-spec/subsystems.md` §7.3 line 99.
   111	    pub active_account:   Option<AccountId>,
   112	}
   113	
   114	impl PublishPlanner for Nip65PublishPlanner<'_> {
   115	    fn plan_publish(&self, event: &Event, privacy: PublishPrivacy,
   116	                    override_: Option<PublishOverride>) -> Result<PublishPlan, PublishPlanError>
   117	    { /* algorithm in §7.3 */ }
   118	}
   119	```
   120	
   121	This is the only `PublishPlanner` impl shipped in v1. The trait exists so a future `Wot​PublishPlanner` (M13 WoT subsystem) or a sandbox planner used in tests can replace it without touching action-ledger code.
   122	
   123	## 7.3 Write fan-out algorithm (per `docs/product-spec/subsystems.md` §7.3)
   124	
   125	Inputs: a signed `event`, a `PublishPrivacy` mode, an optional `PublishOverride`.
   126	
   127	The algorithm deliberately does **not** accept an indexer set. Indexers are read-only
   128	discovery infrastructure (compiler Stage 2, §3.2). A publish planner that accepted an
   129	indexer argument would make it too easy to accidentally route writes to indexers.
   130	Compare: Applesauce's `ActionContext.publish(event, relays?)` is caller-responsibility —
   131	any action can pass any relays (`docs/research/applesauce/outbox.md` §7 lines 116-138).
   132	NMP's planner is structural: publish resolution and read-fallback are separate code paths.
   133	
   134	```
   135	1. If `override_` is Some:
   136	     a. Derive the allowed base set first (steps 2-3 without override).
   137	     b. For PrivateToRecipients: validate that override_relays ⊆ declared inboxes.
   138	        If any override relay is not a declared inbox, return Err(OverrideRejected).
   139	     c. Apply the override (narrow the base set to override_relays).
   140	     d. Set every PublishRouteReason::Override; continue to step 4.
   141	2. Resolve author write relays (no indexer fallback):
   142	     author_outbox = resolve_author_outbox_no_indexer(cache, user_configured, event.pubkey)
   143	     If author_outbox.relays is empty:
   144	         return Err(NoAuthorRelays { ... })  // fail; do not fall back to indexer
   145	3. Match on privacy:
   146	   a. Public:
   147	        assignments = [each author_outbox.relays → AuthorWriteRelay { lane }]
   148	        required_success_count = max(1, ceil(N/3))   // configurable via AppConfig
   149	   b. PrivateToRecipients { recipients }:
   150	        For each recipient r:
   151	            inbox = resolve_author_inbox(cache, user_configured, r)
   152	            // no indexer arg — NDK gotcha a912a2c2: bootstrap timing race means
   153	            // we may not have inbox relays yet; fail-closed is correct for privacy
   154	            If inbox.source == RoutingSource::Indexer or inbox.relays is empty:
   155	                return Err(PrivateRecipientUnroutable { recipient: r })
   156	        assignments = union(each recipient's inbox.relays → RecipientInbox { recipient, lane })
   157	        // intentionally NO author-write inclusion: private events must not go to public outbox
   158	        // per-recipient delivery requirements: one requirement per recipient
   159	        per_recipient_required = recipients.iter().map(|r| {
   160	            RecipientDelivery {
   161	                recipient: r.clone(),
   162	                inbox_relays: recipient_inboxes[r].clone(),
   163	                required_success_count: 1usize,  // usize, not u8 — avoid overflow for large recipient lists
   164	            }
   165	        }).collect()
   166	        required_success_count = per_recipient_required.len()  // all recipients must receive
   167	   c. PublicWithNotifications { notify }:
   168	        assignments = author_outbox ∪ union(each notify pubkey's inbox)
   169	        required_success_count = max(1, ceil(author_outbox.len() / 3))
   170	4. plan_id = blake3(event.id, sorted assignments)
   171	5. deadline_ms = now + AppConfig.publish_deadline_ms (default 30_000)
   172	6. Return PublishPlan { plan_id, assignments, required_success_count, deadline_ms }
   173	```
   174	
   175	Notes on the algorithm:
   176	
   177	- **Step 2's `resolve_author_outbox_no_indexer`** is the structural enforcement of D3 and
   178	  `docs/product-spec/subsystems.md` §7.3 line 99: "fall back to indexer set for reads only;
   179	  do not publish to indexers." The function signature deliberately omits the indexer set so
   180	  the caller cannot accidentally pass it. A failed Step 2 surfaces as `Failed { reason:
   181	  NoAuthorRelays }` in the action ledger, rendered as a toast per ADR-0007's `SideEffect` lane.
   182	- **Step 1's override validation order** (derive base set first, validate override as subset)
   183	  ensures the privacy constraint is always checked — the override cannot bypass the
   184	  `PrivateToRecipients` fail-closed check by returning early before validation.
   185	- **Step 3(b)'s `Indexer` source check** is the structural enforcement of bug-extinction #4
   186	  ([`docs/plan/m9-messaging.md`](../../plan/m9-messaging.md) — "DM to public: no API path
   187	  can send a DM to a non-inbox relay"). Compare: NDK gotcha `a912a2c2`
   188	  (`docs/research/ndk/gotchas.md`) shows that outbox bootstrap timing can leave inbox relays
   189	  empty at first query — NMP's fail-closed here is intentional rather than "retry later."
   190	- **`required_success_count: usize`** prevents overflow when `recipients.len()` is large. The
   191	  `u8` type in the original draft was wrong (max 255 recipients before silent truncation). For
   192	  `PrivateToRecipients`, the per-recipient `RecipientDelivery` set is what the ledger actually
   193	  consults; `required_success_count` is the aggregate guard.
   194	
   195	## 7.4 The `PublishOverride` escape hatch
   196	
   197	The override exists for tests, migration tools, and operator power-user flows. Per `docs/aim.md` §6 doctrine 5 ("manual relay selection is the opt-out, not the default") and `docs/product-spec/subsystems.md` §7.3 line 90 ("explicit overrides are named, one-shot, and debug-flagged in logs"), the override must be:
   198	
   199	1. **Named** — its own typed `AppAction` variant, not a hidden parameter on `SendNote`.
   200	2. **One-shot** — does not persist as a default for future publishes.
   201	3. **Audited** — emits a `Diagnostic::PublishOverrideUsed { reason, action_id }` on the `SideEffect` lane and writes a debug-level log line on every dispatch.
   202	4. **Refused for privacy-sensitive modes** — `PublishPrivacy::PrivateToRecipients` rejects an override that adds non-inbox relays. The override may *narrow* a private fan-out to a subset of declared inboxes; it may not *widen* to public relays.
   203	
   204	### The override action
   205	
   206	```rust
   207	// crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/actions/publish_override.rs (proposed)
   208	
   209	#[derive(Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
   210	pub struct PublishWithOverride {
   211	    pub inner: AppAction,                    // the underlying publish action
   212	    pub override_relays: Vec<RelayUrl>,
   213	    pub override_audit: String,              // human-readable justification
   214	}
   215	
   216	pub struct PublishOverride {
   217	    pub relays: Vec<RelayUrl>,
   218	    pub audit:  String,
   219	}
   220

/bin/zsh -lc "nl -ba docs/design/subscription-compilation/diagnostics.md | sed -n '1,240p'" in /Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform
 succeeded in 0ms:
     1	# Subscription Compilation §5 + §8 — Four-Lane Diagnostics
     2	
     3	> Parent: `docs/design/subscription-compilation.md`.
     4	> Read first: ADR-0007 (`docs/decisions/0007-diagnostics-and-non-nostr-domain-data.md`) — every record here extends ADR-0007 types; it does not replace them.
     5	
     6	The compiler's routing decisions are the most subtle correctness surface in the M2 milestone. They are also the easiest to silently get wrong (`docs/design/ndk-applesauce-lessons.md` §3, "automatic behaviour also needs strong tests"). Diagnostics make the four sources of relay knowledge legible — separately, never collapsed.
     7	
     8	**Indexer fallback is lane 4 (User-configured), not a fifth lane.** The kernel-configured
     9	indexer set is an operator policy choice expressed as `UserConfiguredCategory::Indexer` (see
    10	§5.1 Lane 4). Keeping it inside lane 4 preserves the four-lane discipline and ensures that
    11	the diagnostic UI always sees exactly four columns, regardless of whether an author is
    12	being served via NIP-65, hints, provenance, or any subcategory of user-configured (including
    13	indexers). This resolves the ambiguity at prior diagnostics.md lines 15, 116, 157.
    14	
    15	## 5.0 The four lanes
    16	
    17	Per `docs/design/ndk-applesauce-lessons.md` §4 (lines 39–46) and `docs/aim.md` §6 doctrine 10 ("provenance preserved"), the four relay-fact lanes are:
    18	
    19	1. **NIP-65** — a pubkey's declared relay preferences (kind:10002).
    20	2. **Hint** — relay URLs embedded in events or NIP-19 pointers (`e`/`a` tag third slot, `nevent`'s relay vector, etc.).
    21	3. **Provenance** — relays we have actually observed an event arriving from.
    22	4. **User-configured** — local-policy relays added by the user/operator, plus the kernel-configured indexer fallback set.
    23	
    24	Each lane is its own record stream. They never merge into a single "relays" field — that collapse is exactly the bug `docs/design/ndk-applesauce-lessons.md` §4 line 46 forbids. They may be displayed side-by-side in a diagnostic view; the actor stores them apart.
    25	
    26	This is structurally enforced: there is no `Vec<RelayUrl>` field on any compiler output type. Every relay-bearing field carries a `lane: RelayFactLane` discriminator.
    27	
    28	```rust
    29	// crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/diagnostics/lanes.rs (proposed)
    30	
    31	#[derive(Clone, Copy, Debug, PartialEq, Eq, Hash, Serialize, Deserialize)]
    32	pub enum RelayFactLane {
    33	    Nip65,
    34	    Hint,
    35	    Provenance,
    36	    UserConfigured,
    37	}
    38	```
    39	
    40	## 5.1 Per-lane record schemas
    41	
    42	Each lane has one record type. All four are exposed to the platform via the existing ADR-0007 `ViewBatch` lane (low-cadence, coalesced to 1–4 Hz per ADR-0007 "How status crosses the bridge"). They feed into the diagnostics screen, not into normal product UI.
    43	
    44	### Lane 1 — `Nip65RelayFact`
    45	
    46	```rust
    47	pub struct Nip65RelayFact {
    48	    pub pubkey: Pubkey,
    49	    pub relay_url: RelayUrl,
    50	    pub roles: Nip65Roles,                    // read | write | both
    51	    pub kind10002_event_id: EventId,           // provenance of the kind:10002
    52	    pub kind10002_created_at: UnixSeconds,
    53	    pub kind10002_seen_from: Vec<RelayUrl>,    // which relays delivered it
    54	    pub freshness: FreshnessHint,              // recent / hours_old / days_old / never_verified
    55	}
    56	
    57	pub struct Nip65Roles {
    58	    pub read: bool,
    59	    pub write: bool,
    60	}
    61	```
    62	
    63	Emitted whenever `ingest_relay_list` (`crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/ingest.rs:209-233`) replaces a mailbox entry. One record per `(pubkey, relay_url)` pair; an author with 4 declared relays produces 4 records on each update.
    64	
    65	### Lane 2 — `HintRelayFact`
    66	
    67	```rust
    68	pub struct HintRelayFact {
    69	    /// The pubkey this hint is associated with — the event author for EventTag hints,
    70	    /// the pointer's subject for Nip19 hints. Required for the coverage reducer to
    71	    /// count how many authors are served via hints (see §8.2 by_lane.hint counter).
    72	    pub subject: Pubkey,
    73	    pub relay_url: RelayUrl,
    74	    pub source: HintSource,
    75	    pub freshness_ms: u64,                     // monotonic from observation
    76	    pub recently_succeeded: bool,              // last attempt produced ≥1 EVENT
    77	}
    78	
    79	pub enum HintSource {
    80	    EventTag    { event_id: EventId, tag: TagKey, position: u8 },
    81	    Nip19       { pointer: String /* nevent1.../naddr1... */ },
    82	    UserConfig  { config_path: String },        // for hints injected via config
    83	}
    84	```
    85	
    86	The `subject` field is the author identity key for the coverage reducer's `by_lane.hint`
    87	counter (§8.2). Without it, the reverse-relay-coverage view cannot answer "how many distinct
    88	authors are routed via hints to relay R?" — the assertion in `tests.md:202` that
    89	`coverage.by_lane.hint == 1` would be untestable.
    90	
    91	Emitted by the pointer loader (post-M2; for M2 the field exists but is rarely populated — only `e`/`a`-tag third-slot hints from thread-view hydration fill it). Per-event hints are de-duplicated; an event whose `e` tag contains a hint URL produces one `HintRelayFact` per (relay_url, source) pair.
    92	
    93	### Lane 3 — `ProvenanceRelayFact`
    94	
    95	```rust
    96	pub struct ProvenanceRelayFact {
    97	    pub relay_url: RelayUrl,
    98	    pub event_id: EventId,
    99	    pub seen_at_ms: u64,
   100	    pub wire_sub_id: String,                   // which REQ delivered it
   101	    pub kind: u32,
   102	    pub author: Pubkey,
   103	}
   104	```
   105	
   106	Emitted by `handle_event` (`crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/ingest.rs:134-164`) for every EVENT arrival. This is the highest-cardinality lane and the only one where coalescing matters at the ADR-0007 boundary: the platform diagnostic view consumes a summarised projection (`ProvenanceSummary` per author or per event), not the raw fact stream.
   107	
   108	### Lane 4 — `UserConfiguredRelayFact`
   109	
   110	```rust
   111	pub struct UserConfiguredRelayFact {
   112	    pub relay_url: RelayUrl,
   113	    pub category: UserConfiguredCategory,
   114	    pub generation: u64,                       // config version; matches Trigger::*Changed
   115	    pub added_at_ms: u64,
   116	}
   117	
   118	pub enum UserConfiguredCategory {
   119	    AccountRead,                                // user's own read relays (overrides NIP-65 read)
   120	    AccountWrite,                               // user's own write relays
   121	    /// Kernel-configured indexer relay (e.g. purplepag.es). This is the sub-category that
   122	    /// represents indexer fallback routing in diagnostics — NOT a fifth lane. The indexer
   123	    /// set is a policy choice that lives inside lane 4 (User-configured). D3: the operator
   124	    /// configured the indexer set; the kernel applies it as policy for reads when NIP-65
   125	    /// mailboxes are unknown. Never for writes.
   126	    Indexer,
   127	    Debug,                                      // operator-injected for testing
   128	}
   129	```
   130	
   131	Emitted on `Trigger::UserConfiguredRelaysChanged` / `Trigger::IndexerSetChanged`. Low-cardinality, low-cadence.
   132	
   133	## 5.2 What the compiler may *combine*; what stays distinct
   134	
   135	The compiler may *use* facts from multiple lanes to compute a routing decision; it may **not** present them as one. Concretely:
   136	
   137	- **Routing decision** (`AuthorRouting.source` from [compiler.md](compiler.md) §3.1): records *which lane* the relay set was derived from. Single-valued; one of `Nip65 | Hint | Indexer | UserConfigured`. The author may have facts in three lanes; the compiler picks one, says so, and the other lane records remain visible.
   138	- **`RelayPlan.role_tags`** is a `BTreeSet<RoutingSource>` because a single relay may be in the plan for multiple reasons (e.g. NIP-65 for author A + user-configured fallback for everyone). The set discriminates, it does not collapse.
   139	- **The platform diagnostic view** receives all four lanes as separate `ViewBatch` records. The UI may render them in one screen with four side-by-side columns, but the data path is four lanes.
   140	
   141	A test (§9) asserts that no compiler output type has a field of type `Vec<RelayUrl>` without an adjacent `RelayFactLane`. That is the structural enforcement.
   142	
   143	## 5.3 Lane interactions
   144	
   145	The lanes inform each other through these well-defined hooks:
   146	
   147	- `Provenance → NIP-65 hint refresh.` If we observe many `ProvenanceRelayFact { relay_url: R, author: A }` records but no `Nip65RelayFact { pubkey: A, relay_url: R }`, the operator diagnostic can suggest "we are receiving A's events from R but A has not declared R; their kind:10002 may be stale." This is a future operator-UI affordance, not a behaviour.
   148	- `Hint → planner suggestion.` `HintRelayFact` with `recently_succeeded: true` may surface in the diagnostic view as "you might want to add this to your indexer set." Again, not automatic.
   149	- `User-configured` is the **only** lane the compiler treats as authoritative-by-policy (the user said so). Open question 5 in the parent index resolves the augment-vs-override precedence between NIP-65 and user-configured for the active account.
   150	
   151	The lesson the four-lane discipline preserves: routing is **derivable but contested** evidence. Collapsing the lanes loses information; preserving them lets the diagnostic answer "why did we route this REQ to that relay?" months after the decision.
   152	
   153	---
   154	
   155	# §8 — Reverse-relay-coverage diagnostic view
   156	
   157	> The inverse question. For any relay we are talking to, *whose* timeline does it serve?
   158	
   159	This is one specific `ViewModule` that consumes the four-lane fact streams plus the compiler's `RelayPlan`s and produces a per-relay summary.
   160	
   161	## 8.1 Spec, payload, dependencies
   162	
   163	```rust
   164	pub struct RelayCoverageSpec {
   165	    pub relay_url: RelayUrl,
   166	}
   167	
   168	pub struct RelayCoveragePayload {
   169	    pub relay_url: RelayUrl,
   170	    pub serving_authors: u32,
   171	    pub author_examples: Vec<Pubkey>,      // first N (configurable, default 16)
   172	    pub by_lane: ByLaneCounts,
   173	    pub wire_sub_count: u32,
   174	    pub last_event_at_ms: Option<u64>,
   175	    pub provenance_count_last_minute: u32,
   176	}
   177	
   178	pub struct ByLaneCounts {
   179	    pub nip65: u32,             // authors for whom relay is in their NIP-65 set
   180	    pub hint: u32,              // authors for whom we routed here via hints (requires subject: Pubkey on HintRelayFact)
   181	    pub user_configured: u32,   // authors served via any UserConfigured sub-category
   182	    /// Sub-count of `user_configured` where `category == Indexer`. Not a fifth lane —
   183	    /// these are already counted in `user_configured`. Exposed separately so the diagnostic
   184	    /// UI can show "12 via user-config (including 8 indexer fallback)" without a lane split.
   185	    pub indexer_fallback: u32,
   186	}
   187	
   188	// `ViewModule::dependencies` returns:
   189	//   - Mailbox cache updates touching any author in our timeline
   190	//   - RelayPlan updates touching `relay_url`
   191	//   - Provenance facts on `relay_url` (rate-limited; only the count, not individual events)
   192	```
   193	
   194	## 8.2 Implementation outline
   195	
   196	The view's `reduce` consumes **all four** input streams — one per lane. All four must be
   197	wired so the test assertions at `tests.md:202` that check `by_lane.hint` and
   198	`by_lane.user_configured` are actually backed by live data:
   199	
   200	1. `Nip65RelayFact` records — increments/decrements `by_lane.nip65` per (relay_url, pubkey) membership.
   201	2. `HintRelayFact` records — increments/decrements `by_lane.hint` per (relay_url, subject) pair. The `subject: Pubkey` field (§5.1 Lane 2) is the author identity; without it this counter cannot be maintained.
   202	3. `UserConfiguredRelayFact` records — increments/decrements `by_lane.user_configured` per (relay_url) membership. Records with `category == Indexer` contribute to `by_lane.indexer_fallback` (a sub-count of `user_configured`); the UI can show both the aggregate and the Indexer-specific sub-total.
   203	4. `CompiledPlan` re-emissions — every plan recompile produces a `(plan_id, relay_url) → authors` projection this view subscribes to. The compiler exposes this as `RelayAuthorCoverage` in the projection cache.
   204	5. `ProvenanceRelayFact` records — feeds the rolling 60-second counter for `provenance_count_last_minute`.
   205	
   206	This is the M2 exit-gate diagnostic listed in [`docs/plan/m2-subscription-compilation.md`](../../plan/m2-subscription-compilation.md) ("Reverse-relay-coverage view for diagnostics: 'this relay is serving N authors of our timeline.'").
   207	
   208	## 8.3 Cardinality and emission cadence
   209	
   210	One `RelayCoverageSpec`/relay → ≤ N records, where N is the number of relays currently in the planner's union of `RelayPlan`s. For typical Nostr usage that is in the low tens; rendering all of them on one diagnostic screen is fine.
   211	
   212	Emission cadence follows ADR-0007's diagnostic-view rule: material-transition immediately, otherwise 1–4 Hz. The provenance counter ticks every second; the `by_lane` counts only emit on `CompiledPlan` recompiles or new mailbox arrivals.
   213	
   214	## 8.4 Why it lives in diagnostics, not in product UI
   215	
   216	Per `docs/aim.md` §4.4 ("the developer does not pick relays per operation; the framework does") and ADR-0007's domain-of-diagnostics separation, end-user product UIs do not show "relay X is serving 12 authors." That is operator/debug surface. Normal apps consume the `LogicalInterestStatus` summaries; `RelayCoveragePayload` is for the diagnostics screen (proof iOS app screenshot in `docs/perf/m2/outbox-routing.md` per [`docs/plan/m2-subscription-compilation.md`](../../plan/m2-subscription-compilation.md)).

Records with `category == Indexer` contribute to `by_lane.indexer_fallback` (a sub-count of `user_configured`); the UI can show both the aggregate and the Indexer-specific sub-total.
docs/design/subscription-compilation/tests.md:151:    assert!(alice_relay_v1.role_tags.contains(&RoutingSource::Indexer));

docs/design/subscription-compilation/recompilation.md:97:Outbox routing implication: if `pubkey` was previously routed to the indexer fallback set (Stage 2 read-fallback), the compiler now reassigns to the author's declared write relays. The wire-emitter closes the indexer REQ for that author's slice and opens a new REQ on the declared relays. ADR-0007 diagnostics reflect the route source flipping from `UserConfigured::Indexer` to `Nip65`.
docs/design/subscription-compilation/intro.md:11:- **Relay choice is a 2-variant enum, not a URL set.** `crates/nmp-core/src/relay.rs:15-39` defines `RelayRole::{Content, Indexer}` with a `.url() -> &'static str` that returns one of the two literals. This shape cannot express "this REQ should go to the union of these N authors' write relays."
docs/design/subscription-compilation/intro.md:13:- **Startup REQs ignore mailboxes by construction.** `crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/requests.rs:50-106` (`startup_requests`) issues six fixed REQs, each pinned to `Content` or `Indexer`. The seed-bootstrap timeline (line 65–70) fans seven hundred-author future timelines through one relay. The exit-gate test for M2 ([`docs/plan/m2-subscription-compilation.md`](../../plan/m2-subscription-compilation.md)) requires that this fan exactly equal the union of those authors' write relays.
docs/design/subscription-compilation/intro.md:14:- **View-open REQs ignore mailboxes too.** `crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/requests.rs:404-439` (`author_requests`) hardcodes a three-REQ shape — `author-relays-N` on Indexer, `author-profile-N` on Indexer, `author-notes-N` on Content. The author's notes are fetched from the global content relay even though by the time the view opens we may already have that author's kind:10002 in cache (see next bullet).
docs/design/subscription-compilation/intro.md:16:- **Profile claim path is single-relay.** `crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/requests.rs:390-402` (`profile_claim_request`) sends a kind:0 fetch to `RelayRole::Indexer` unconditionally. It cannot consult mailboxes for the claimed author.
docs/design/subscription-compilation/intro.md:111:- `AuthorView { pubkey }` returns three interests: kind:10002 (Indexer fallback policy, see §3), kind:0 (one-shot), and `{ authors: [pubkey], kinds: {1, 6}, limit: 100 }` (Tailing).
docs/design/subscription-compilation/outbox.md:77:    /// Falls back to indexer set if author has no write relays.
docs/design/subscription-compilation/outbox.md:106:    // intentionally no `indexer_set` field: publish NEVER falls back to indexers (D3).
docs/design/subscription-compilation/outbox.md:127:The algorithm deliberately does **not** accept an indexer set. Indexers are read-only
docs/design/subscription-compilation/outbox.md:154:            If inbox.source == RoutingSource::Indexer or inbox.relays is empty:
docs/design/subscription-compilation/outbox.md:179:  do not publish to indexers." The function signature deliberately omits the indexer set so
docs/design/subscription-compilation/outbox.md:185:- **Step 3(b)'s `Indexer` source check** is the structural enforcement of bug-extinction #4
docs/design/subscription-compilation/compiler.md:22:   │ Stage 2: Indexer fallback for missing mailboxes   │  (§3.2)
docs/design/subscription-compilation/compiler.md:62:    // Note: there is no RoutingSource::Indexer variant — indexer fallback is
docs/design/subscription-compilation/compiler.md:63:    // modeled as UserConfigured { category: Indexer } (see diagnostics.md §5.0).
docs/design/subscription-compilation/compiler.md:84:`docs/product-spec/subsystems.md` §7.3 specifies one explicit override: DMs (NIP-17 gift-wraps, M9) fail closed if recipient inbox relays are missing. The compiler enforces this by refusing to produce a plan for an interest tagged `privacy = FailClosed` if any tagged-pubkey inbox lookup returns `Indexer` source. §7 details the publish-side enforcement.
docs/design/subscription-compilation/compiler.md:86:## 3.2 Stage 2 — Indexer fallback for unknown mailboxes
docs/design/subscription-compilation/compiler.md:93:2. **Read fallback.** For a `RoutingDirection::Outbox` interest whose author has no known mailboxes, the compiler routes the interest to the indexer set **as read-only fallback**. Per `docs/product-spec/subsystems.md` §7.3 line 99: "fall back to indexer set for reads only; do not publish to indexers." The resulting `AuthorRouting` carries `source: RoutingSource::UserConfigured` with a `UserConfiguredRelayFact { category: UserConfiguredCategory::Indexer }` record flowing to the diagnostic surface. The four-lane view (§5) renders "author X is being served by indexer Y because we have no mailbox for them" by examining the `category` subcategory — the Indexer is a sub-category of lane 4 (User-configured), not a fifth lane.
docs/design/subscription-compilation/compiler.md:179:- **Indexer set change changes plan-id.** Operator config edits surface immediately.

 succeeded in 0ms:
     1	# Design: Subscription Compilation + Outbox Routing (M2)
     2	
     3	> **Status:** Draft (M2 design + impl-prep).
     4	> **Date:** 2026-05-18.
     5	> **Companion docs:** [`docs/plan/m2-subscription-compilation.md`](../plan/m2-subscription-compilation.md); `docs/aim.md` §4.4–§4.5; `docs/design/ndk-applesauce-lessons.md` §7; ADR-0007 (diagnostic lanes); `docs/design/kernel-substrate.md` §3 (`ViewModule`) and §4 (`ActionModule`); `docs/product-spec/subsystems.md` §7.2–§7.3.
     6	> **Scope:** Replace the "hardcoded two-role relay set" planner in `crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/{requests,ingest,mod}.rs` with a **subscription compilation stage** that turns logical interests into per-relay plans driven by NIP-65 mailboxes, and graduates outbox routing to a first-class planner subsystem. v1 is in-memory; M3 plugs it into LMDB. This is a design doc; no implementation lands in this PR.
     7	
     8	This document is split into focused sub-files to stay under the 500 LOC ceiling (`AGENTS.md`).
     9	
    10	- [Intro — problem statement + logical interest model](subscription-compilation/intro.md) (§1, §2)
    11	- [Compiler — pipeline + plan-id contract + function migration table](subscription-compilation/compiler.md) (§3)
    12	- [Recompilation — triggers and actor message shapes](subscription-compilation/recompilation.md) (§4)
    13	- [Diagnostics — four-lane records + reverse-coverage view](subscription-compilation/diagnostics.md) (§5, §8)
    14	- [nmp-nip65 — crate layout, traits, public surface](subscription-compilation/nip65.md) (§6)
    15	- [Outbox — publish-planner seam + override action](subscription-compilation/outbox.md) (§7)
    16	- [Tests — wire-frame audit gate](subscription-compilation/tests.md) (§9)
    17	
    18	## Section map
    19	
    20	| § | Topic | File |
    21	|---|---|---|
    22	| 1 | What is wrong with the current planner (cited line refs) | intro.md |
    23	| 2 | Logical interest — formal definition + how views express them | intro.md |
    24	| 3 | Compilation pipeline: authors → mailboxes → per-relay plans + plan-id | compiler.md |
    25	| 4 | Recompilation triggers (kind:10002, view open/close, reconnect, account switch, manual, user-configured change) | recompilation.md |
    26	| 5 | Four-lane diagnostic records (NIP-65 / hint / provenance / user-configured) | diagnostics.md |
    27	| 6 | `nmp-nip65` file layout, `MailboxesViewModule`, public surface | nip65.md |
    28	| 7 | `PublishPlanner` trait, write fan-out policy, override + debug warning | outbox.md |
    29	| 8 | Reverse-relay-coverage diagnostic view ("this relay serves N authors of our timeline") | diagnostics.md |
    30	| 9 | M2 exit-gate audit test path + assertions | tests.md |
    31	| 10 | Open questions for follow-up ADRs | this file (below) |
    32	
    33	## 10. Open questions
    34	
    35	These remain to be resolved by ADRs after design review, not in this design pass.
    36	
    37	1. **Plan-id stability under perturbation.** ~~RESOLVED~~ — `compiler.md` §3.4 now hashes
    38	   only mailboxes for pubkeys referenced by the current interest set, not the whole snapshot.
    39	   This scopes plan-id churn to authors actually in active interests. An arriving kind:10002
    40	   for an unrelated pubkey does NOT churn plan-ids (D8: recompile cost must be bounded by
    41	   what's open). The test contracts in §9 are written against this formulation.
    42	2. **Filter-merge lattice formal-isation.** §3 step 3 lists which filter fields are safely mergeable across logical interests (`authors`, `kinds`, `since`, `until`, `limit`, tag operators). It does not yet specify the merge algebra for the corner case where two interests differ only in `limit` but agree on authors and kinds. Worth an ADR-level note before the lattice is coded; `docs/product-spec/subsystems.md` §7.2 promises "a formal merge lattice for `limit`, `since`, `until`, multi-filter arrays, and tag operators."
    43	3. **Per-author indexer-fallback ledger row?** Today the compiler treats indexer fallback as an inline relay assignment. If the kind:10002 fetch is its own durable action (M6 ledger), the fallback becomes a tracked action with retry/cancel semantics. Cleaner for diagnostics; heavier for M2. Defer.
    44	4. **Read-relay vs write-relay use for subscriptions on the same author.** NIP-65 defines write relays (outbox) for the author's own events and read relays (inbox) for events directed *at* the author. For a `Timeline { authors: [...] }` we want write relays. For a `Notifications { p: [author] }` we want inbox relays. The compiler distinguishes them by filter shape (`authors` vs `#p`), matching the `docs/product-spec/subsystems.md` §7.3 routing table. Document a corner case: kind-1 filtered by both `authors` and `#p` is rare but real (replies to the author from the author). Pick a precedence in an ADR; current bias is `authors` wins (write relays).
    45	5. **User-configured relay precedence vs NIP-65.** A user adds `wss://my-private.example` to local config. Does it *augment* (union) or *override* (replace) NIP-65 routing for the active account? `subsystems.md` §7.3 default-resolves by NIP-65; user-configured is "fallback" in the indexer sense. ADR needs to spell out the augment/override question for the active account specifically.
    46	6. **Auth-paused relays in compiled plans.** If a relay is in `RelayAuthState::ChallengeReceived`, the compiler still produces a plan that assigns interests to it (so reconnect-after-auth resumes correctly), but emission must pause. Is the pause modeled inside the compiler (per-relay gate) or inside the wire-emitter (consumes plans, applies pause)? Bias: wire-emitter, but the compiler must surface the pause as a fact for `LogicalInterestStatus`. Resolve before M5.
    47	7. **NSE crate compilation surface.** `nmp-nip17-nse` (M9) runs in iOS Notification Service Extension with bounded memory; it needs to compile a single-author single-relay plan without the full planner. Confirm in an ADR that the compiler exposes a `compile_one(spec, mailbox_cache_snapshot) -> Plan` pure function suitable for NSE use, and that the function does not require a live actor.

     1	# Subscription Compilation §9 — Wire-Frame Audit Gate
     2	
     3	> Parent: `docs/design/subscription-compilation.md`.
     4	> Read first: [`docs/plan/m2-subscription-compilation.md`](../../plan/m2-subscription-compilation.md) (M2 exit gates); `docs/design/firehose-bench.md` (the modeled bench harness this test does *not* duplicate).
     5	
     6	The M2 exit gate is a single integration test that asserts on the *shape and identity* of the compiler's wire output, not on perf budgets. It is the structural-correctness counterpart to firehose-bench's perf-correctness suite.
     7	
     8	## 9.1 Test file location
     9	
    10	```
    11	crates/nmp-testing/tests/m2_subscription_compilation_audit.rs
    12	```
    13	
    14	The `crates/nmp-testing/tests/` directory does not exist yet; M2 creates it. This is intentional — it establishes the convention that planner-correctness gates live as Cargo integration tests under `nmp-testing/tests/`, distinct from the modeled benches under `nmp-testing/bin/`.
    15	
    16	`Cargo.toml` for `nmp-testing` needs the standard `[[test]]` block:
    17	
    18	```toml
    19	[[test]]
    20	name = "m2_subscription_compilation_audit"
    21	path = "tests/m2_subscription_compilation_audit.rs"
    22	```
    23	
    24	`cargo test -p nmp-testing --test m2_subscription_compilation_audit` is the M2 exit-gate invocation. CI adds this to the `cargo test --workspace` pre-merge gate per [`docs/plan/ci-hygiene.md`](../../plan/ci-hygiene.md).
    25	
    26	## 9.2 What the test asserts
    27	
    28	Four assertions corresponding to the four M2 exit-gate bullets in [`docs/plan/m2-subscription-compilation.md`](../../plan/m2-subscription-compilation.md):
    29	
    30	### Assertion 1 — Bug-extinction #3 surface check
    31	
    32	> "No public API path lets the developer specify relays for a publish; explicit override action exists and produces a debug warning."
    33	
    34	> **Codegen dependency (M2 impl-PR gate).** The test below introspects an
    35	> `AppActionMeta` reflection helper that lives in the per-app generated crate
    36	> (ADR-0010). `AppActionMeta` does not exist yet — it is a M2 implementation-PR
    37	> deliverable. ADR-0010's codegen must emit it before this assertion can compile.
    38	> The M2 impl PR must either (a) add `AppActionMeta` emission to the codegen tool
    39	> (`nmp-gen modules`), or (b) provide a `proc-macro`-driven enum walker as a
    40	> fallback. This test is intentionally marked `#[ignore]` in CI until the codegen
    41	> dependency lands; the ignore comment must name the tracking ADR and impl-PR.
    42	
    43	```rust
    44	#[test]
    45	fn no_public_publish_action_carries_relay_list() {
    46	    // Compile-time-ish check: introspect the AppAction enum's generated variants
    47	    // (per ADR-0010, the per-app generated enum is a closed enum we can match
    48	    // exhaustively in tests). For every variant whose namespace starts with
    49	    // "kernel.publish" or "nip01.send" or "nip17.send", assert that no field
    50	    // is of type Vec<RelayUrl> EXCEPT the one variant `PublishWithOverride`.
    51	    let variants = AppActionMeta::all_variants();
    52	    for v in variants {
    53	        if v.is_publish_action() {
    54	            let has_relay_field = v.fields().any(|f| f.ty == "Vec<RelayUrl>");
    55	            if v.namespace == "kernel.publish_override" {
    56	                assert!(has_relay_field, "override must carry override_relays");
    57	            } else {
    58	                assert!(!has_relay_field,
    59	                    "{} must not expose a relays parameter", v.namespace);
    60	            }
    61	        }
    62	    }
    63	}
    64	```
    65	
    66	This is a "shape of the API" assertion, not a behaviour assertion. If a future PR adds a relay field to `SendNote`, the test fails.
    67	
    68	### Assertion 2 — Per-author wire fan-out for a 1000-author timeline
    69	
    70	> "For a timeline of 1000 authors, the compiled plan opens REQs only against the union of those authors' write relays (de-duplicated). Test asserts on the wire frame count."
    71	
    72	```rust
    73	#[test]
    74	fn timeline_compiles_to_per_relay_union() {
    75	    let mut harness = PlannerHarness::new();
    76	
    77	    // Seed mailbox cache with 1000 authors, deliberately overlapping relay sets:
    78	    //   - 600 authors use { wss://relay.damus.io, wss://nos.lol }
    79	    //   - 300 authors use { wss://nostr.wine, wss://nos.lol }
    80	    //   - 100 authors use { wss://operator-niche.example }
    81	    let authors = make_authors_with_overlapping_mailboxes(1000);
    82	    for (pk, mb) in &authors { harness.mailbox_cache_mut().put(pk.clone(), mb.clone()); }
    83	
    84	    // Register one Timeline interest containing all 1000 authors.
    85	    let interest_id = harness.register_interest(LogicalInterest {
    86	        scope: InterestScope::ActiveAccount,
    87	        shape: InterestShape::timeline_for(authors.iter().map(|(pk, _)| pk.clone()).collect()),
    88	        lifecycle: InterestLifecycle::Tailing,
    89	        ..LogicalInterest::default()
    90	    });
    91	
    92	    let plan = harness.compile().expect("compile");
    93	
    94	    // Assert: exactly one REQ per relay in the union of write relays.
    95	    let expected_relays: BTreeSet<RelayUrl> = authors.iter()
    96	        .flat_map(|(_, mb)| mb.write.iter().cloned())
    97	        .collect();
    98	    let actual_relays: BTreeSet<RelayUrl> = plan.per_relay.iter()
    99	        .map(|rp| rp.relay_url.clone()).collect();
   100	    assert_eq!(actual_relays, expected_relays);
   101	
   102	    // Assert: each relay carries exactly one SubShape (merge happened).
   103	    for rp in &plan.per_relay {
   104	        assert_eq!(rp.sub_shapes.len(), 1,
   105	            "relay {} should have one merged sub-shape, has {}",
   106	            rp.relay_url, rp.sub_shapes.len());
   107	    }
   108	
   109	    // Assert: each relay's authors are exactly the subset that declared it.
   110	    for rp in &plan.per_relay {
   111	        let expected_authors: BTreeSet<Pubkey> = authors.iter()
   112	            .filter(|(_, mb)| mb.write.contains(&rp.relay_url))
   113	            .map(|(pk, _)| pk.clone())
   114	            .collect();
   115	        let actual_authors = &rp.sub_shapes[0].shape.authors;
   116	        assert_eq!(actual_authors, &expected_authors,
   117	            "relay {} should serve only its declared authors", rp.relay_url);
   118	    }
   119	
   120	    // Assert: plan-id is deterministic — running compile twice yields the same id.
   121	    let plan2 = harness.compile().expect("compile #2");
   122	    assert_eq!(plan.plan_id, plan2.plan_id, "recompile with no input changes ≠ same plan_id");
   123	}
   124	```
   125	
   126	This is the single most load-bearing test in M2. It assert on:
   127	
   128	- **Relay count** = size of union of declared write relays (no extras, no misses).
   129	- **Per-relay author partition** = exact subset semantics.
   130	- **Sub-shape merge** = one REQ per relay (merge lattice worked).
   131	- **Plan-id stability** = re-compile is idempotent.
   132	
   133	### Assertion 3 — Late-arriving kind:10002 triggers recompilation
   134	
   135	> "An author whose mailbox was unknown gets re-routed once their kind:10002 lands, without the platform observing protocol churn."
   136	
   137	```rust
   138	#[test]
   139	fn late_nip65_arrival_reroutes_without_churn() {
   140	    let mut harness = PlannerHarness::new();
   141	    let target = pubkey("alice");
   142	
   143	    // Seed: no mailbox for alice. Register an interest that needs her.
   144	    harness.register_interest(LogicalInterest::timeline_for(vec![target.clone()]));
   145	    let plan_v1 = harness.compile().unwrap();
   146	
   147	    // The author should be routed via indexer fallback.
   148	    let alice_relay_v1 = plan_v1.per_relay.iter()
   149	        .find(|rp| rp.sub_shapes[0].shape.authors.contains(&target))
   150	        .expect("alice routed somewhere");
   151	    assert!(alice_relay_v1.role_tags.contains(&RoutingSource::Indexer));
   152	
   153	    // Now alice's kind:10002 arrives.
   154	    harness.ingest_nip65(&target, ["wss://alice-relay.example"]);
   155	
   156	    // The ingest emits Trigger::Nip65Arrived → recompile happens internally.
   157	    harness.flush_pending_triggers();
   158	    let plan_v2 = harness.last_compiled_plan();
   159	
   160	    // Assert: plan-id changed.
   161	    assert_ne!(plan_v1.plan_id, plan_v2.plan_id);
   162	
   163	    // Assert: alice now routes to her declared relay, not the indexer.
   164	    let alice_relay_v2 = plan_v2.per_relay.iter()
   165	        .find(|rp| rp.sub_shapes[0].shape.authors.contains(&target))
   166	        .expect("alice still routed");
   167	    assert_eq!(alice_relay_v2.relay_url, "wss://alice-relay.example".into());
   168	    assert!(alice_relay_v2.role_tags.contains(&RoutingSource::Nip65));
   169	
   170	    // Assert: the audit stream contains exactly ONE planner re-emission for alice;
   171	    // the platform sees one transition, not a thrash of N intermediate states.
   172	    let audit = harness.compile_audit_log();
   173	    let alice_transitions = audit.iter()
   174	        .filter(|a| a.affected_authors.contains(&target))
   175	        .count();
   176	    assert_eq!(alice_transitions, 1, "expected exactly one recompile for late NIP-65");
   177	}
   178	```
   179	
   180	This assertion is what `docs/design/ndk-applesauce-lessons.md` §2 line 19 calls out as NDK's important operational truth: "metadata can arrive late… the system should be able to refresh or expand active work without the app tearing down and recreating views."
   181	
   182	### Assertion 4 — Four-lane diagnostic distinctness
   183	
   184	> "The diagnostics screen shows the four relay-fact lanes (NIP-65 / hint / provenance / user-configured) separately."
   185	
   186	```rust
   187	#[test]
   188	fn four_lanes_stay_distinct_in_diagnostic_payload() {
   189	    let mut harness = PlannerHarness::new();
   190	    let author = pubkey("alice");
   191	
   192	    // Set up evidence in all four lanes for the same relay url.
   193	    let url: RelayUrl = "wss://r.example".into();
   194	    harness.ingest_nip65(&author, [url.clone()]);              // Nip65 fact
   195	    harness.observe_hint(&author, url.clone(),                 // Hint fact (with subject: Pubkey)
   196	        HintSource::EventTag { event_id: eid("e1"), tag: TagKey::E, position: 2 });
   197	    harness.observe_provenance(&author, url.clone(), eid("e2")); // Provenance fact
   198	    harness.user_configured_relay(url.clone(),                 // UserConfigured fact
   199	        UserConfiguredCategory::Indexer);
   200	
   201	    let coverage = harness.open_view::<RelayCoverageView>(
   202	        RelayCoverageSpec { relay_url: url.clone() });
   203	
   204	    // Assert all four lane counts are non-zero — this is the structural guarantee.
   205	    // If any count is zero, the reducer is not consuming that lane's fact stream.
   206	    assert!(coverage.by_lane.nip65 >= 1,
   207	        "NIP-65 lane must be non-zero: reducer is not consuming Nip65RelayFact stream");
   208	    assert!(coverage.by_lane.hint >= 1,
   209	        "Hint lane must be non-zero: reducer is not consuming HintRelayFact stream \
   210	         (check that HintRelayFact.subject is set and the reducer reads it)");
   211	    assert!(coverage.by_lane.user_configured >= 1,
   212	        "UserConfigured lane must be non-zero: reducer is not consuming UserConfiguredRelayFact stream");
   213	    // Provenance count is the rolling 60s counter; alice's event landed once.

   215	        "Provenance lane must be non-zero: reducer is not consuming ProvenanceRelayFact stream");

   223	    // Structural: no compiler output collapses lanes.

   229	        "role_tags should record at least one routing reason for this relay");
