     1	# Subscription Compilation §6 — `nmp-nip65` Crate Layout
     2	
     3	> Parent: `docs/design/subscription-compilation.md`.
     4	> Read first: `docs/design/kernel-substrate.md` §3 (`ViewModule`) for the trait this crate implements; `docs/design/app-extension-kernel.md` §3 layering table — `nmp-nip65` is a **protocol module**, not an app module.
     5	
     6	`nmp-nip65` is the first NMP protocol module (per the v1 reference-modules list in `docs/design/kernel-substrate.md` §11) whose job is *not* to expose product views. It contributes one `ViewModule` (for app-side rendering of "this user's relay list") and implements the NIP-65 parsing logic. It is **not** the canonical source of the mailbox cache — that trait lives in `nmp-core` to avoid a dependency cycle.
     7	
     8	**Crate dependency resolution (D0):** `nmp-core::kernel::planner` must consume the mailbox
     9	cache, but it cannot import from `nmp-nip65` (that would create a cycle: `nmp-nip65` →
    10	`nmp-core` → `nmp-nip65`). Resolution: the `MailboxCache` trait and associated types
    11	(`MailboxSnapshot`, `CachePutResult`) live in `nmp-core::substrate::mailbox` (or a tiny
    12	zero-dependency `nmp-mailbox-types` crate). `nmp-nip65` implements the trait via
    13	`InMemoryMailboxCache` and registers the kind:10002 ingest handler; `nmp-core` declares the
    14	trait and consumes it — no cycle. This is consistent with D0: the kernel defines the seam,
    15	the module fills it.
    16	
    17	## 6.1 File structure
    18	
    19	```
    20	crates/nmp-nip65/
    21	├── Cargo.toml
    22	├── README.md
    23	├── src/
    24	│   ├── lib.rs              # re-exports + crate-level documentation
    25	│   ├── module.rs           # impl ViewModule for MailboxesView
    26	│   ├── parse.rs            # kind:10002 tag parsing (extracted from kernel)
    27	│   ├── cache.rs            # MailboxCache trait + InMemory impl
    28	│   ├── routing.rs          # AuthorRouting, RoutingSource, mailbox lookup API
    29	│   └── tests/
    30	│       ├── parse.rs        # round-trip + edge-case tag parsing
    31	│       ├── routing.rs      # mailbox → relay-set resolution scenarios
    32	│       └── snapshot.rs     # cache snapshot/restore for compiler-input tests
    33	└── tests/                  # integration tests against the in-memory cache
    34	```
    35	
    36	Soft target per file: ≤ 300 LOC (AGENTS.md). The crate stays small; everything heavier (filter compilation, indexer probes) lives in `nmp-core::kernel::planner`, not here.
    37	
    38	## 6.2 Traits implemented
    39	
    40	`nmp-nip65` implements exactly one extension trait family: `ViewModule`. It does *not* implement `ActionModule` (kind:10002 publish is the user's own "update my relay list" action, deferred to M6's action ledger; in this milestone it has no write surface). It does *not* implement `DomainModule` (mailbox records live in the kernel-owned mailbox cache; they are queryable Nostr events, not durable app-defined records).
    41	
    42	### `MailboxesView` (`impl ViewModule`)
    43	
    44	```rust
    45	// crates/nmp-nip65/src/module.rs
    46	
    47	pub struct MailboxesView;
    48	
    49	#[derive(Clone, Serialize, Deserialize, Hash, Eq, PartialEq)]
    50	pub struct MailboxesSpec {
    51	    pub pubkey: Pubkey,
    52	}
    53	
    54	#[derive(Clone, Serialize)]
    55	pub struct MailboxesPayload {
    56	    pub pubkey: Pubkey,
    57	    pub read:  Vec<RelayUrl>,
    58	    pub write: Vec<RelayUrl>,
    59	    pub both:  Vec<RelayUrl>,
    60	    pub created_at: UnixSeconds,           // 0 if unknown
    61	    pub source: MailboxSource,
    62	}
    63	
    64	pub enum MailboxSource {
    65	    Cached    { freshness: FreshnessHint },
    66	    Fetching,
    67	    Unknown,
    68	}
    69	
    70	impl ViewModule for MailboxesView {
    71	    const NAMESPACE: &'static str = "nip65.mailboxes";
    72	    type Spec    = MailboxesSpec;
    73	    type Payload = MailboxesPayload;
    74	    type Delta   = MailboxesPayload;       // payloads are small; emit whole snapshots
    75	    type Key     = Pubkey;
    76	    type State   = MailboxesPayload;
    77	
    78	    fn key(spec: &MailboxesSpec) -> Pubkey {
    79	        spec.pubkey.clone()
    80	    }
    81	
    82	    fn dependencies(spec: &MailboxesSpec) -> ViewDependencies {
    83	        ViewDependencies::author_kind(&spec.pubkey, 10002)
    84	    }
    85	
    86	    fn interests(spec: &MailboxesSpec, ctx: &InterestContext) -> Vec<LogicalInterest> {
    87	        vec![LogicalInterest {
    88	            id: ctx.fresh_id(),
    89	            scope: InterestScope::Global,
    90	            shape: InterestShape {
    91	                authors: btreeset![spec.pubkey.clone()],
    92	                kinds:   btreeset![10002],
    93	                limit:   Some(1),
    94	                ..Default::default()
    95	            },
    96	            hints: vec![],
    97	            lifecycle: InterestLifecycle::OneShot,
    98	        }]
    99	    }
   100	
   101	    fn open(ctx: &ViewContext, spec: MailboxesSpec) -> (Self::State, Self::Payload) {
   102	        let snapshot = ctx.mailbox_cache().get(&spec.pubkey);
   103	        let payload = MailboxesPayload::from_snapshot(spec.pubkey, snapshot);
   104	        (payload.clone(), payload)
   105	    }
   106	
   107	    fn on_event_inserted(ctx: &ViewContext, st: &mut Self::State, ev: &Event)
   108	        -> Option<Self::Delta>
   109	    {
   110	        // D4: single writer per fact. The canonical mailbox write path is
   111	        // `ingest_relay_list` → `MailboxCache::put`. This view MUST NOT re-parse
   112	        // kind:10002 tags independently — doing so would make two writers for the
   113	        // same fact (the cache writer and the view's parse). Instead, read the
   114	        // canonical snapshot from the cache that was already updated by ingest.
   115	        if ev.kind != 10002 || ev.pubkey != st.pubkey { return None; }
   116	        let snapshot = ctx.mailbox_cache().get(&st.pubkey)?;
   117	        if snapshot.created_at <= st.created_at { return None; }
   118	        *st = MailboxesPayload::from_snapshot(st.pubkey.clone(), Some(snapshot));
   119	        Some(st.clone())
   120	    }
   121	
   122	    // on_event_removed / replaced / projection_changed / on_tick: defaults
   123	    fn snapshot(_ctx: &ViewContext, st: &Self::State) -> Self::Payload {
   124	        st.clone()
   125	    }
   126	}
   127	```
   128	
   129	The view exists so platform code can render "alice@example uses these relays" using the same path as any other view (`useMailboxes(pubkey)`); it is *not* the compiler's source of truth. The compiler reads `MailboxCache` directly.
   130	
   131	## 6.3 Public surface (compiler-facing, not FFI-facing)
   132	
   133	The `MailboxCache` trait and its associated types live in **`nmp-core::substrate::mailbox`**
   134	to break the dependency cycle (see §6.1 intro). `nmp-nip65` provides the `InMemoryMailboxCache`
   135	implementation; `nmp-core::kernel::planner` consumes only the trait, never the concrete type.
   136	
   137	```rust
   138	// crates/nmp-core/src/substrate/mailbox.rs  ← trait lives here, not in nmp-nip65
   139	
   140	pub trait MailboxCache: Send + Sync {
   141	    fn get(&self, pubkey: &Pubkey) -> Option<MailboxSnapshot>;
   142	    fn put(&mut self, pubkey: Pubkey, snapshot: MailboxSnapshot)
   143	        -> CachePutResult;
   144	    fn snapshot_all(&self) -> Vec<(Pubkey, MailboxSnapshot)>;
   145	    fn generation(&self) -> u64;           // monotonic; advances on every accepted put
   146	}
   147	
   148	pub enum CachePutResult {
   149	    Inserted,
   150	    ReplacedNewer { prior_created_at: UnixSeconds },
   151	    /// NIP-01 tie-break: equal `created_at` keeps the event with the lexicographically
   152	    /// lower event id, per Applesauce gotcha `90d525af`
   153	    /// (`docs/research/applesauce/gotchas.md` §G1). Two clients producing kind:10002
   154	    /// in the same second must not silently disagree on the stored version.
   155	    RejectedStale { current_created_at: UnixSeconds },
   156	}
   157	
   158	#[derive(Clone, Debug)]
   159	pub struct MailboxSnapshot {
   160	    pub kind10002_event_id: EventId,
   161	    pub created_at: UnixSeconds,
   162	    pub read:  Vec<RelayUrl>,
   163	    pub write: Vec<RelayUrl>,
   164	    pub both:  Vec<RelayUrl>,
   165	    pub seen_from: Vec<RelayUrl>,          // ProvenanceRelayFact seed
   166	}
   167	```
   168	
   169	```rust
   170	// crates/nmp-nip65/src/cache.rs  ← concrete impl lives here
   171	
   172	pub struct InMemoryMailboxCache { /* HashMap<Pubkey, MailboxSnapshot> */ }
   173	impl MailboxCache for InMemoryMailboxCache { /* ... */ }
   174	```
   175	
   176	The `MailboxCache` trait is the seam M3 (LMDB persistence) replaces with a backing-store-aware implementation. The compiler does not know which backend it is reading.
   177	
   178	```rust
   179	// crates/nmp-nip65/src/routing.rs
   180	
   181	pub fn resolve_author_outbox(
   182	    cache: &dyn MailboxCache,
   183	    user_configured: &UserConfiguredRelays,
   184	    indexer_set: &[RelayUrl],
   185	    author: &Pubkey,
   186	) -> AuthorRouting { /* ... */ }
   187	
   188	pub fn resolve_author_inbox(
   189	    cache: &dyn MailboxCache,
   190	    user_configured: &UserConfiguredRelays,
   191	    indexer_set: &[RelayUrl],
   192	    author: &Pubkey,
   193	) -> AuthorRouting { /* ... */ }
   194	```
   195	
   196	These are the two pure functions [compiler.md](compiler.md) Stage 1 calls per author. They return `AuthorRouting` with the `RoutingSource` tag set per the four-lane discipline ([diagnostics.md](diagnostics.md) §5.2). Test fixtures live in `crates/nmp-nip65/src/tests/routing.rs`; the same fixtures plug into the audit gate (§9).
   197	
   198	The `resolve_author_outbox_no_indexer` variant used by the publish planner (§7) intentionally
   199	omits the indexer set parameter — compare NDK's `chooseRelayCombinationForPubkeys`
   200	(`docs/research/ndk/outbox.md` §"Big picture" → `core/src/outbox/index.ts:45`) which is
   201	the nearest equivalent for reads, and Applesauce's `OutboxModel` which delegates relay
   202	resolution to every caller rather than enforcing publish-vs-read separation
   203	(`docs/research/applesauce/outbox.md` §7 lines 116-138).
   204	
   205	```rust
   206	// crates/nmp-nip65/src/parse.rs
   207	
   208	pub fn parse_relay_list(created_at: UnixSeconds, tags: &[Vec<String>])
   209	    -> ParsedRelayList;
   210	```
   211	
   212	This is the function currently inlined as a free fn in `crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/nostr.rs` (referenced by `kernel/ingest.rs:210` and tested in `kernel/tests.rs:150`). M2 moves it here and re-exports from `nmp-core` for compatibility during the migration.
   213	
   214	## 6.4 What `nmp-nip65` does *not* contain
   215	
   216	By design, to keep the kernel boundary clean (per `docs/design/app-extension-kernel.md` §3):
   217	
   218	- **No publish action.** Updating a user's own kind:10002 is `nmp-nip01::UpdateRelayList` (M6); that action depends on `nmp-nip65::parse` to validate the local copy before publishing.
   219	- **No outbox routing policy.** The decision "publish goes to author write relays + recipient inbox relays" is the publish planner ([outbox.md](outbox.md) §7), not this crate. This crate provides the lookups; the policy lives in `nmp-core::kernel::planner::publish`.
   220	- **No indexer-probe scheduling.** Probes are kernel-side; this crate is unaware of probe lifecycle.
   221	- **No FFI types.** `MailboxesPayload` is exposed at FFI via the per-app generated enum (per ADR-0010 codegen); the crate itself ships pure Rust.
   222	
   223	## 6.5 Module composition (per `docs/design/kernel-substrate.md` §8)
   224	
   225	`nmp-nip65` consumes:
   226	
   227	- `nmp-core::substrate::{ViewModule, ViewContext, InterestContext, LogicalInterest,
   228	  MailboxCache, MailboxSnapshot, CachePutResult, ...}` — kernel trait surface including the
   229	  mailbox types (trait lives in core, not in this crate).
   230	- `nmp-core::kernel::projections` — for reading kind:10002 events out of the event store.
   231	
   232	`nmp-nip65` is consumed by:
   233	
   234	- The per-app generated enum — `MailboxesView` becomes one variant of `ViewSpec` in
   235	  `nmp-app-<name>` per ADR-0010.
   236	- Future `nmp-nip01::UpdateRelayList` (M6) — imports `parse::parse_relay_list` for validation.
   237	- Future `nmp-nip17` (M9) — DM publish path imports `routing::resolve_author_inbox`.
   238	
   239	`nmp-nip65` is **not** consumed by `nmp-core::kernel::planner`. The planner imports
   240	`nmp-core::substrate::mailbox::{MailboxCache, ...}` directly (no nmp-nip65 dep). The
   241	concrete `InMemoryMailboxCache` is injected at app startup via `AppConfig`; the planner
   242	receives a `&dyn MailboxCache` reference.
   243	
   244	## 6.6 Cargo manifest sketch
   245	
   246	```toml
   247	[package]
   248	name = "nmp-nip65"
   249	version = "0.0.1"
   250	edition = "2021"
   251	
   252	[dependencies]
   253	nmp-core   = { path = "../nmp-core" }
   254	serde      = { workspace = true, features = ["derive"] }
   255	serde_json = { workspace = true }
   256	
   257	[dev-dependencies]
   258	nmp-testing = { path = "../nmp-testing" }
   259	```
   260	
   261	No `nostr-sdk` dependency: this crate operates on parsed `Event` structs from `nmp-core`'s already-vetted ingest path. Avoiding a duplicate parse dependency keeps the surface auditable.

/bin/zsh -lc "nl -ba docs/design/subscription-compilation/tests.md | sed -n '230,330p'" in /Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform
 succeeded in 0ms:
   230	    assert!(matches!(alice_assignment.role_tags.iter().next().unwrap(),
   231	        RoutingSource::Nip65 | RoutingSource::UserConfigured),
   232	        "routing source must be a known lane discriminant, not a collapsed 'relays' field");
   233	}
   234	```
   235	
   236	This assertion encodes the doctrine: a single relay may be in the plan for multiple reasons; the plan must say which reasons, not collapse them. The explicit non-zero checks (before the exact-count checks) catch the failure mode where a reducer is wired but receives no facts — a silent count-stays-at-zero bug that the original `assert_eq!(count, 1)` alone would catch, but which is clearer with an explicit "lane not consumed" message.
   237	
   238	## 9.3 The `PlannerHarness`
   239	
   240	The test harness is itself part of `nmp-testing`:
   241	
   242	```rust
   243	// crates/nmp-testing/src/planner_harness.rs (proposed)
   244	
   245	pub struct PlannerHarness {
   246	    cache: InMemoryMailboxCache,
   247	    user_config: UserConfiguredRelays,
   248	    indexer_set: Vec<RelayUrl>,
   249	    interests: InterestRegistry,
   250	    compiler: SubscriptionCompiler,
   251	    audit_log: Vec<CompileAuditEntry>,
   252	}
   253	
   254	impl PlannerHarness {
   255	    pub fn new() -> Self;
   256	    pub fn mailbox_cache_mut(&mut self) -> &mut dyn MailboxCache;
   257	    pub fn register_interest(&mut self, i: LogicalInterest) -> InterestId;
   258	    pub fn drop_interest(&mut self, id: InterestId);
   259	    pub fn ingest_nip65(&mut self, author: &Pubkey, relays: impl IntoIterator<Item = RelayUrl>);
   260	    pub fn observe_hint(&mut self, author: &Pubkey, url: RelayUrl, source: HintSource);
   261	    pub fn observe_provenance(&mut self, author: &Pubkey, url: RelayUrl, event: EventId);
   262	    pub fn user_configured_relay(&mut self, url: RelayUrl, cat: UserConfiguredCategory);
   263	    pub fn force_recompile(&mut self, reason: InvalidateReason);
   264	    pub fn flush_pending_triggers(&mut self);
   265	    pub fn compile(&mut self) -> Result<CompiledPlan, CompileError>;
   266	    pub fn last_compiled_plan(&self) -> &CompiledPlan;
   267	    pub fn compile_audit_log(&self) -> &[CompileAuditEntry];
   268	    pub fn open_view<V: ViewModule>(&mut self, spec: V::Spec) -> V::Payload;
   269	}
   270	```
   271	
   272	The harness is the *minimum* surface required for the four assertions above. It is deliberately small so it does not become its own moving target.
   273	
   274	## 9.4 What this test does *not* cover
   275	
   276	By design (these belong to other M2 gates or later milestones):
   277	
   278	- **Real wire frames against a relay.** This is `firehose-bench live` per [`docs/plan/m1-twitter-slice.md`](../../plan/m1-twitter-slice.md) (M1 exit gate "Firehose-bench live cold_start"); the audit test is offline and synthetic.
   279	- **Wire-emitter diff correctness across two plans.** That is a separate unit test inside `nmp-core::kernel::wire`, not the milestone-exit gate.
   280	- **NIP-77 watermarks.** M4.
   281	- **Per-account auth state.** M5.
   282	- **The publish path running end-to-end.** M6.
   283	
   284	The audit gate's job is exactly the four assertions: API shape, fan-out structure, recompilation on late NIP-65, and four-lane diagnostic distinctness. Those are the four exit-gate bullets the milestone document lists; this test is the verification surface for all four.
   285	
   286	## 9.5 CI integration
   287	
   288	The test runs in the default `cargo test --workspace` job and takes < 1 second on standard hardware (no networking, no LMDB, in-memory cache only). It is the canonical regression test for "did someone re-introduce the hardcoded two-role planner?" and as such must never be skipped or `#[ignore]`d.
   289	
   290	If the M3 (LMDB) milestone graduates the mailbox cache to a real backend, this test continues to exercise the trait surface via the `InMemoryMailboxCache` impl — no changes required. That is the seam `nmp-nip65::cache::MailboxCache` exists for.

docs/design/subscription-compilation/diagnostics.md:42:Each lane has one record type. docs/design/subscription-compilation/diagnostics.md:106:Emitted by `handle_event` (`crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/ingest.rs:134-164`) for every EVENT arrival. This is the highest-cardinality lane and the only one where coalescing matters at the ADR-0007 boundary: the platform diagnostic view consumes a summarised projection (`ProvenanceSummary` per author or per event), not the raw fact stream.
docs/design/subscription-compilation/diagnostics.md:212:Emission cadence follows ADR-0007's diagnostic-view rule: material-transition immediately, otherwise 1–4 Hz. The provenance counter ticks every second; the `by_lane` counts only emit on `CompiledPlan` recompiles or new mailbox arrivals.
docs/design/subscription-compilation/diagnostics.md:216:Per `docs/aim.md` §4.4 ("the developer does not pick relays per operation; the framework does") and ADR-0007's domain-of-diagnostics separation, end-user product UIs do not show "relay X is serving 12 authors." That is operator/debug surface. Normal apps consume the `LogicalInterestStatus` summaries; `RelayCoveragePayload` is for the diagnostics screen (proof iOS app screenshot in `docs/perf/m2/outbox-routing.md` per [`docs/plan/m2-subscription-compilation.md`](../../plan/m2-subscription-compilation.md)).
docs/design/subscription-compilation/recompilation.md:71:        state: RelayAuthState,              // re-exported from ADR-0007
docs/design/subscription-compilation/recompilation.md:97:Outbox routing implication: if `pubkey` was previously routed to the indexer fallback set (Stage 2 read-fallback), the compiler now reassigns to the author's declared write relays. The wire-emitter closes the indexer REQ for that author's slice and opens a new REQ on the declared relays. ADR-0007 diagnostics reflect the route source flipping from `UserConfigured::Indexer` to `Nip65`.
docs/design/subscription-compilation/recompilation.md:105:Batching contract: the actor's planner inbox coalesces consecutive `ViewOpened` triggers within one actor tick into a single recompile pass. This is the existing reactivity batching (`docs/design/reactivity/scheduling-and-data-model.md`) extended to the planner; the M2 implementation respects the same `≤60Hz/view` budget from ADR-0002 by capping recompiles at one per tick regardless of trigger fan-in.
docs/design/subscription-compilation/recompilation.md:115:Compiler effect: every `InterestScope::ActiveAccount` interest is re-resolved as if newly opened. `InterestScope::Account(specific)` interests are untouched. `InterestScope::Global` interests are untouched.

docs/design/subscription-compilation/compiler.md:209:What this migration does **not** do (deferred per parent index open questions 3, 6, 7):

docs/design/subscription-compilation/outbox.md:181:  NoAuthorRelays }` in the action ledger, rendered as a toast per ADR-0007's `SideEffect` lane.

docs/design/subscription-compilation/outbox.md:280:- **Concurrent publish coalescing** — if two actions publish the same event (a republish), the planner can dedupe to one wire EVENT per relay. Defer to M6 / M7 stress test.
docs/design/subscription-compilation/intro.md:19:The summary diagnosis: **the planner is a string formatter, not a compiler.** Every REQ is a per-call-site decision; routing is one of two literals; recompilation is impossible because nothing is compiled. The diagnostics in `crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/mod.rs:117-154` already type `RelayStatus` / `WireSubscriptionStatus` / `LogicalInterestStatus` per ADR-0007 — but the planner currently emits at most one `LogicalInterestStatus` per view kind because there is no logical-interest object to scope it against.
docs/design/subscription-compilation/intro.md:127:- A **wire REQ**. Wire REQs are produced by the compiler; they live in `WireSubscriptionStatus` per ADR-0007.
docs/design/subscription-compilation/intro.md:129:- A **diagnostic record**. ADR-0007 lanes are facts derived from the planner's state, not inputs.
docs/design/subscription-compilation.md:5:> **Companion docs:** [`docs/plan/m2-subscription-compilation.md`](../plan/m2-subscription-compilation.md); `docs/aim.md` §4.4–§4.5; `docs/design/ndk-applesauce-lessons.md` §7; ADR-0007 (diagnostic lanes); `docs/design/kernel-substrate.md` §3 (`ViewModule`) and §4 (`ActionModule`); `docs/product-spec/subsystems.md` §7.2–§7.3.
docs/design/subscription-compilation.md:31:| 10 | Open questions for follow-up ADRs | this file (below) |
docs/design/subscription-compilation.md:33:## 10. Open questions
docs/design/subscription-compilation.md:35:These remain to be resolved by ADRs after design review, not in this design pass.
docs/design/subscription-compilation.md:37:1. **Plan-id stability under perturbation.** ~~RESOLVED~~ — `compiler.md` §3.4 now hashes
docs/design/subscription-compilation.md:42:2. **Filter-merge lattice formal-isation.** §3 step 3 lists which filter fields are safely mergeable across logical interests (`authors`, `kinds`, `since`, `until`, `limit`, tag operators). It does not yet specify the merge algebra for the corner case where two interests differ only in `limit` but agree on authors and kinds. Worth an ADR-level note before the lattice is coded; `docs/product-spec/subsystems.md` §7.2 promises "a formal merge lattice for `limit`, `since`, `until`, multi-filter arrays, and tag operators."
docs/design/subscription-compilation.md:43:3. **Per-author indexer-fallback ledger row?** Today the compiler treats indexer fallback as an inline relay assignment. If the kind:10002 fetch is its own durable action (M6 ledger), the fallback becomes a tracked action with retry/cancel semantics. Cleaner for diagnostics; heavier for M2. Defer.
docs/design/subscription-compilation.md:44:4. **Read-relay vs write-relay use for subscriptions on the same author.** NIP-65 defines write relays (outbox) for the author's own events and read relays (inbox) for events directed *at* the author. For a `Timeline { authors: [...] }` we want write relays. For a `Notifications { p: [author] }` we want inbox relays. The compiler distinguishes them by filter shape (`authors` vs `#p`), matching the `docs/product-spec/subsystems.md` §7.3 routing table. Document a corner case: kind-1 filtered by both `authors` and `#p` is rare but real (replies to the author from the author). Pick a precedence in an ADR; current bias is `authors` wins (write relays).
docs/design/subscription-compilation.md:45:5. **User-configured relay precedence vs NIP-65.** A user adds `wss://my-private.example` to local config. Does it *augment* (union) or *override* (replace) NIP-65 routing for the active account? `subsystems.md` §7.3 default-resolves by NIP-65; user-configured is "fallback" in the indexer sense. ADR needs to spell out the augment/override question for the active account specifically.
docs/design/subscription-compilation.md:47:7. **NSE crate compilation surface.** `nmp-nip17-nse` (M9) runs in iOS Notification Service Extension with bounded memory; it needs to compile a single-author single-relay plan without the full planner. Confirm in an ADR that the compiler exposes a `compile_one(spec, mailbox_cache_snapshot) -> Plan` pure function suitable for NSE use, and that the function does not require a live actor.

/bin/zsh -lc "nl -ba docs/product-spec/overview-and-dx.md | sed -n '45,95p'" in /Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform
 succeeded in 0ms:
    45	Apps built with this framework **never withhold cached data and never block on fetches**. Every view payload field carries a value, not a "loading" status. Missing display names default to a shortened npub; missing pictures default to a deterministic identicon URI; missing timestamps default to "now". When a more authoritative value (e.g., the author's kind:0) arrives later, the view payload updates in place and the affected cell re-renders. The UI never sees a spinner gating already-renderable content.
    46	
    47	The doctrine is enforced by the view payload **types**: display fields are non-`Option`, placeholders are part of the type contract, and freshness is exposed (when relevant) as an optional badge hint, not a render gate. There is no `if has_profile { render } else { spinner }` pattern available in the API — the framework does not provide one.
    48	
    49	This rules out, by construction, the most common Nostr-client failure modes:
    50	
    51	- Hiding a post because the author's profile hasn't loaded yet.
    52	- Replacing cached profile metadata with a spinner because "we might have something newer."
    53	- Refusing to render threads because the root event isn't in cache.
    54	- Profile-picture flicker between cached and placeholder.
    55	
    56	### D2. Negentropy first, REQ second
    57	
    58	NIP-77 negentropy reconciliation is the default backfill mechanism. Every `(filter, relay)` pair the app touches is treated as a tracked sync target with a watermark. Live REQ remains the tailing path, but historical gaps consult coverage first and prefer sync over REQ scans when relays support it.
    59	
    60	This is not a product feature you opt into later; it is a subscription policy built on explicit coverage metadata. See §7.8.
    61	
    62	### D3. Outbox routing is automatic; manual relay selection is the opt-out
    63	
    64	Per NIP-65, reads and writes are routed to the relevant relays by framework policy without normal app code specifying them. Subscriptions with `authors` filters route to those authors' write relays; publishes go to the author's write relays plus tagged recipients' inbox relays; discovery falls back to a configurable indexer set.
    65	
    66	The safe public path does not ask the developer to pick relays per operation. Explicit override and diagnostic/test paths exist, but they are named, observable, and excluded from the default app-building flow.
    67	
    68	This rules out, by construction:
    69	
    70	- Posts to relays the author hasn't declared as write relays.
    71	- DMs leaked to public relays.
    72	- Silent reads against a default relay set that miss an author's actual relays; unknown relay lists surface as coverage/diagnostic state and use a bounded fallback policy.
    73	- Hand-rolled fan-out logic in app code.
    74	
    75	### D4. Single writer per fact; caches derive
    76	
    77	The "single source of truth" doctrine does not mean one cache — there are five layers (durable event store, in-memory working set, view payloads, gossip cache, platform reactive shadow). It means **one writer per fact**, and every downstream cache derives from the writer mechanically. Cache invalidation is not a concept in the public API. Recomputation happens in the actor; the platform receives new derived state.
    78	
    79	### D5. Snapshots bounded by what's open
    80	
    81	What crosses FFI is the projection through currently-open views, not the underlying event store. `AppState` carries small screen-shaped data plus a map of `ViewId → ViewPayload` for views currently in use. Closing a view evicts its payload from the snapshot. The event store itself never crosses FFI. See §6.2 and the FFI architecture appendix (§A1).
    82	
    83	### D6. Errors never cross FFI as exceptions
    84	
    85	Operational failures surface as `toast: Option<String>` state fields, never as exceptions or `Result<T, E>` across the FFI boundary. Long-running operations expose `busy` flags that clear on completion regardless of outcome. Native `dispatch` calls never need `try` / `catch`; native reconciler callbacks never receive a Rust error type.
    86	
    87	This rules out, by construction:
    88	
    89	- Swift / Kotlin code wrapping framework calls in `do { try }` or `try { } catch`.
    90	- Per-operation error-type proliferation across UniFFI.
    91	- Silent failure: every error has at least one observable state field carrying its consequences (a toast, a `busy` flag clearing, a diagnostic record per ADR-0007).
    92	
    93	Per RMP bible invariant #2 (`docs/aim.md` §2).
    94	
    95	### D7. Capabilities report; never decide policy

    70	// AUTO-GENERATED — do not edit by hand. Regenerate with `nmp gen modules`.
    71	
    72	#[derive(Clone, uniffi::Enum)]
    73	pub enum AppAction {
    74	    // Kernel variants (from nmp-core)
    75	    Kernel(nmp_core::KernelAction),
    76	
    77	    // Protocol module variants
    78	    Nip01(nmp_nip01::Action),
    79	    Nip02(nmp_nip02::Action),
    80	    Nip25(nmp_nip25::Action),
    81	    Nip10(nmp_nip10::Action),

    83	    // App module variants
    84	    Twitter(twitter_core::Action),

    86	
    87	impl AppAction {
    88	    pub fn dispatch(self, app: &Arc<FfiApp>) {
    89	        match self {
    90	            AppAction::Kernel(a) => app.core_tx.send(CoreMsg::Kernel(a)).ok(),
    91	            AppAction::Nip01(a) => app.core_tx.send(CoreMsg::Module("nip01", a.into_bytes())).ok(),
    92	            AppAction::Nip02(a) => app.core_tx.send(CoreMsg::Module("nip02", a.into_bytes())).ok(),
    93	            // ...
    94	        };
    95	    }
    96	}
    97	```
    98	
    99	`AppUpdate`, `ViewSpec`, and the capability traits are generated analogously.
   100	
   101	### How the actor dispatches
   102	
   103	Inside `nmp-core`'s actor, message handling is generic over module identifier:
   104	
   105	```rust
   106	enum CoreMsg {
   107	    Kernel(KernelAction),
   108	    Module(&'static str, Vec<u8>),    // module namespace + serialized action
   109	    Internal(InternalEvent),
   110	}
   111	
   112	fn handle_message(&mut self, msg: CoreMsg) {
   113	    match msg {
   114	        CoreMsg::Kernel(a) => self.handle_kernel_action(a),
   115	        CoreMsg::Module(ns, bytes) => {
   116	            let handler = self.module_registry.get(ns).expect("module not registered");
   117	            handler.dispatch(self, &bytes);
   118	        }
   119	        CoreMsg::Internal(e) => self.handle_internal(e),
   120	    }

/bin/zsh -lc "nl -ba docs/design/kernel-substrate.md | sed -n '180,240p'" in /Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform
 succeeded in 0ms:
   180	## 4. `ActionModule` — durable workflows on the action ledger
   181	
   182	Replaces the closed `AppAction` enum. Every user intent — `SendNote`, `React`, `Repost`, `CreateHighlight`, `UploadBlob`, `RunSync` — is an `ActionModule`.
   183	
   184	```rust
   185	pub trait ActionModule: Send + Sync + 'static {
   186	    const NAMESPACE: &'static str;
   187	
   188	    type Action: Clone + Serialize + DeserializeOwned + Send + 'static;
   189	    type Step: Clone + Serialize + DeserializeOwned + Send + 'static;
   190	    type Output: Clone + Serialize + Send + 'static;
   191	
   192	    /// Validate + plan the action. Returns initial step state or rejection.
   193	    fn start(ctx: &mut ActionContext, action: Self::Action)
   194	        -> Result<ActionPlan<Self::Step>, ActionRejection>;
   195	
   196	    /// Drive the state machine. Input could be a capability result, a relay OK,
   197	    /// a timeout, a user approval, etc.
   198	    fn reduce(ctx: &mut ActionContext, id: ActionId, input: ActionInput<Self::Step>)
   199	        -> ActionTransition<Self::Step, Self::Output>;
   200	}
   201	
   202	pub struct ActionPlan<Step> {
   203	    pub initial_step: Step,
   204	    pub initial_status: ActionStatus,
   205	    pub deadline_ms: Option<u64>,
   206	}
   207	
   208	pub enum ActionTransition<Step, Output> {
   209	    Continue { step: Step, status: ActionStatus },
   210	    Complete { output: Output },
   211	    Fail { reason: String, transient: bool },
   212	    AwaitCapability { request: CapabilityRequest, next_step: Step },
   213	    AwaitUserApproval { prompt: ApprovalPrompt, next_step: Step },
   214	}
   215	```
   216	
   217	**What the kernel owns:**
   218	
   219	- Durable ledger rows (`actions` table in the storage backend).
   220	- Action IDs (ULID).
   221	- Status transitions: `Pending → Running → Completed | Failed | Cancelled`.
   222	- Retries with exponential backoff (for transient failures).
   223	- Cancellation correlation.
   224	- Provenance (which relays it published to, when, with what response).
   225	- Capability request/response correlation (the action awaits a `CapabilityResult`).
   226	- Restart recovery (actor restart re-loads in-flight actions; modules' `reduce` is called with `ActionInput::ResumedAfterRestart`).
   227	- Diagnostic rendering for ADR-0007.
   228	
   229	**What the module owns:**
   230	
   231	- Action types (e.g. `SendNote { content, reply_to }`, `React { target, emoji }`).
   232	- Step machine (validate → sign → publish → confirm).
   233	- Validation rules.
   234	- Final domain writes (insert the signed event into the store, append to a domain table, etc.).
   235	- Whether user approval is required (for sensitive actions).
   236	
   237	**Atomicity** (doctrine guarantee): the kernel ensures `module.reduce(...)`, ledger transitions, and local store writes happen as one actor message. External effects such as relay publishes cannot be rolled back after a relay accepts them, so publish steps must be ledger-correlated and restart-recoverable: a "publish accepted but local insert failed" path becomes an explicit failed/recovery ledger state, not silent divergence.
   238	
   239	**Example:**
   240	

codex
I would not merge this as-is. Main blockers:

- [outbox.md:54](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/docs/design/subscription-compilation/outbox.md:54) and [outbox.md:106](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/docs/design/subscription-compilation/outbox.md:106): public publish says it can fall back to the indexer path, and the algorithm calls `resolve_author_outbox(..., indexer, ...)`. That violates “do not publish to indexers.” Fix by splitting read fallback from publish resolution, or reject `RoutingSource::Indexer` for all publish modes.

- [outbox.md:103](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/docs/design/subscription-compilation/outbox.md:103): override returns before privacy validation, conflicting with [outbox.md:138](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/docs/design/subscription-compilation/outbox.md:138). Fix by deriving the allowed base set first, then validate override as a subset for private routes.

- [nip65.md:192](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/docs/design/subscription-compilation/nip65.md:192) plus [nip65.md:197](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/docs/design/subscription-compilation/nip65.md:197): `nmp-nip65` depends on `nmp-core`, while `nmp-core::kernel::planner` consumes `nmp-nip65`. That is a Rust crate cycle and blurs D0. Fix by moving mailbox traits/types into `nmp-core` or a tiny no-core crate, with `nmp-nip65` only registering module behavior.

- [nip65.md:6](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/docs/design/subscription-compilation/nip65.md:6), [nip65.md:98](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/docs/design/subscription-compilation/nip65.md:98), [compiler.md:52](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/docs/design/subscription-compilation/compiler.md:52): mailbox facts have too many apparent writers/read models: `ingest_relay_list`, `MailboxCache`, and `MailboxesView::on_event_inserted`. D4 wants one writer per fact. Fix by making one canonical mailbox projection/cache writer; views only read that projection.

- [compiler.md:140](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/docs/design/subscription-compilation/compiler.md:140) conflicts with [recompilation.md:95](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/docs/design/subscription-compilation/recompilation.md:95): `plan_id` hashes the whole mailbox snapshot, so unrelated kind:10002 arrivals churn global plan ids. Fix by hashing only mailboxes referenced by the current interest set, or separate logical plan id from relay-assignment id.

- [diagnostics.md:61](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/docs/design/subscription-compilation/diagnostics.md:61) and [diagnostics.md:172](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/docs/design/subscription-compilation/diagnostics.md:172): `HintRelayFact` has no author/subject key, and the coverage reducer does not consume hint or user-configured fact streams, yet [tests.md:202](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/docs/design/subscription-compilation/tests.md:202) asserts those counts. Add subject identity to hint facts and make the view consume all four lanes.

- [diagnostics.md:15](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/docs/design/subscription-compilation/diagnostics.md:15), [diagnostics.md:116](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/docs/design/subscription-compilation/diagnostics.md:116), [diagnostics.md:157](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/docs/design/subscription-compilation/diagnostics.md:157): indexer fallback is sometimes a fifth source and sometimes user-configured. Fix by modeling it as `UserConfigured { category: Indexer }`, with any indexer count as a subcategory, not a new lane.

- [compiler.md:166](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/docs/design/subscription-compilation/compiler.md:166) and [compiler.md:184](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/docs/design/subscription-compilation/compiler.md:184): startup timeline/profile/contact behavior remains framed as `nmp-core` migration work. D0 fix: move social bootstrap modules to `nmp-nip01` / `nmp-nip02` / demo app code; core should only execute registered module interests.

- [outbox.md:119](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/docs/design/subscription-compilation/outbox.md:119): private publish uses a union of recipient inbox relays plus `required_success_count = recipients.len() as u8`; this neither proves one success per recipient nor avoids overflow. Fix with per-recipient delivery requirements and `usize`.

Other checks: no literal `TODO`, `FIXME`, or `unimplemented!` in the added docs. File sizes are compliant: all new files are under 300 LOC, largest is `tests.md` at 273. The planned audit test still has weak spots: [tests.md:34](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/docs/design/subscription-compilation/tests.md:34) depends on non-existent `AppActionMeta`, and [tests.md:213](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/docs/design/subscription-compilation/tests.md:213) does not actually assert lane distinctness.

commit 5ff0ac5d1fc72420efc01b86475937cd477bca10
Author: pablof7z <pfer@me.com>
Date:   Mon May 18 02:29:57 2026 +0300

    docs(m2): fix codex feedback (no publish-to-indexer, D4 single-writer, D0 module ownership, plan-id stability, lane modeling, etc.)
    
    Addresses all 10 findings from the codex review of 031fc07:
    
    1. outbox.md: split read-fallback from publish resolution; Nip65PublishPlanner
       has no indexer_set field — publish never falls back to indexers (D3).
    
    2. outbox.md: derive allowed base set before applying override; validate override
       as ⊆ declared inboxes for PrivateToRecipients before returning plan.
    
    3. nip65.md: MailboxCache trait moves to nmp-core::substrate::mailbox to break the
       nmp-nip65 → nmp-core → nmp-nip65 cycle (D0); nmp-nip65 provides InMemoryMailboxCache
       impl; nmp-core::kernel::planner imports only the trait.
    
    4. nip65.md: on_event_inserted no longer re-parses kind:10002 tags (two writers
       for the same fact violates D4); reads canonical snapshot from mailbox_cache instead.
    
    5. compiler.md + index: plan_id hashes only mailboxes for pubkeys referenced by the
       current interest set, not the whole snapshot; unrelated kind:10002 arrivals do not
       churn plan-ids (D8 reactivity contract); open question 1 resolved.
    
    6. diagnostics.md: add subject: Pubkey to HintRelayFact so the coverage reducer can
       count hint-lane authors; §8.2 now explicitly wires all four fact streams.
    
    7. diagnostics.md: model indexer fallback as UserConfigured { category: Indexer } —
       a sub-category of lane 4, not a fifth lane; ByLaneCounts.indexer_fallback is a
       sub-count of user_configured, not a separate lane.
    
    8. compiler.md: startup bootstrap (seed timeline, kind:0/3/10002) relocated to
       nmp-nip01/nmp-nip02/demo-app per D0; nmp-core actor only executes registered
       module interests; maybe_open_timeline moves to nmp-nip02.
    
    9. outbox.md: PrivateToRecipients uses per-recipient RecipientDelivery requirements;
       required_success_count changed to usize (u8 overflow for large recipient lists).
    
    10. tests.md: AppActionMeta codegen dependency clearly documented as M2 impl-PR gate
        (marked #[ignore] until codegen lands); four-lane assertion now asserts all four
        lane counts >= 1 with per-lane failure messages.
    
    Research citations added:
    - NDK a14c7a78 (signer-before-pubkey) → recompilation.md A10
    - NDK a912a2c2 (outbox bootstrap timing race) → outbox.md §7.3, recompilation.md A1
    - Applesauce 90d525af (NIP-01 tie-break) → nip65.md MailboxCache CachePutResult
    - Applesauce 75ef7d5f (counter-vs-boolean) → compiler.md claim_profile migration
    - NDK core/src/subscription/index.ts:787-812 (refreshRelayConnections only-adds) → recompilation.md A1
    - Applesauce relay-selection.ts:14-93 (greedy set-cover) → compiler.md Stage 3
    - Applesauce outbox.md §7 lines 116-138 (caller-responsibility publish) → outbox.md §7.1
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 ```rust
 #[test]
@@ -190,7 +192,7 @@ fn four_lanes_stay_distinct_in_diagnostic_payload() {

     let url: RelayUrl = "wss://r.example".into();
     harness.ingest_nip65(&author, [url.clone()]);              // Nip65 fact
-    harness.observe_hint(&author, url.clone(),                 // Hint fact

     harness.user_configured_relay(url.clone(),                 // UserConfigured fact
@@ -199,24 +201,39 @@ fn four_lanes_stay_distinct_in_diagnostic_payload() {
     let coverage = harness.open_view::<RelayCoverageView>(
         RelayCoverageSpec { relay_url: url.clone() });
 
-    assert_eq!(coverage.by_lane.nip65, 1);
-    assert_eq!(coverage.by_lane.hint,  1);
-    assert_eq!(coverage.by_lane.user_configured, 1);
+    // Assert all four lane counts are non-zero — this is the structural guarantee.
+    // If any count is zero, the reducer is not consuming that lane's fact stream.
+    assert!(coverage.by_lane.nip65 >= 1,
+        "NIP-65 lane must be non-zero: reducer is not consuming Nip65RelayFact stream");
+    assert!(coverage.by_lane.hint >= 1,
+        "Hint lane must be non-zero: reducer is not consuming HintRelayFact stream \
+         (check that HintRelayFact.subject is set and the reducer reads it)");
+    assert!(coverage.by_lane.user_configured >= 1,
+        "UserConfigured lane must be non-zero: reducer is not consuming UserConfiguredRelayFact stream");
     // Provenance count is the rolling 60s counter; alice's event landed once.
-    assert_eq!(coverage.provenance_count_last_minute, 1);
+    assert!(coverage.provenance_count_last_minute >= 1,
+        "Provenance lane must be non-zero: reducer is not consuming ProvenanceRelayFact stream");
+
+    // Exact counts (lanes must be distinct, not collapsed):
+    assert_eq!(coverage.by_lane.nip65, 1, "NIP-65 count wrong");
+    assert_eq!(coverage.by_lane.hint,  1, "Hint count wrong");
+    assert_eq!(coverage.by_lane.user_configured, 1, "UserConfigured count wrong");
+    assert_eq!(coverage.provenance_count_last_minute, 1, "Provenance count wrong");
 
     // Structural: no compiler output collapses lanes.
     let plan = harness.compile().unwrap();
     let alice_assignment = plan.per_relay.iter()
         .find(|rp| rp.relay_url == url).unwrap();
     // role_tags is a SET, not a single value — lanes are preserved.
-    assert!(alice_assignment.role_tags.len() >= 1);

+        "role_tags should record at least one routing reason for this relay");
     assert!(matches!(alice_assignment.role_tags.iter().next().unwrap(),
-        RoutingSource::Nip65 | RoutingSource::UserConfigured));
+        RoutingSource::Nip65 | RoutingSource::UserConfigured),
+        "routing source must be a known lane discriminant, not a collapsed 'relays' field");
 }
 ```
 
-This assertion encodes the doctrine: a single relay may be in the plan for multiple reasons; the plan must say which reasons, not collapse them.
+This assertion encodes the doctrine: a single relay may be in the plan for multiple reasons; the plan must say which reasons, not collapse them. The explicit non-zero checks (before the exact-count checks) catch the failure mode where a reducer is wired but receives no facts — a silent count-stays-at-zero bug that the original `assert_eq!(count, 1)` alone would catch, but which is clearer with an explicit "lane not consumed" message.
 
 ## 9.3 The `PlannerHarness`

/bin/zsh -lc "nl -ba docs/design/subscription-compilation/intro.md | sed -n '90,130p'" in /Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform
 succeeded in 0ms:
    90	
    91	Every `ViewModule` (per `docs/design/kernel-substrate.md` §3) declares its dependencies via the existing `ViewDependencies` mechanism. M2 adds one new method:
    92	
    93	```rust
    94	pub trait ViewModule {
    95	    // ... existing methods ...
    96	
    97	    /// Translate a view spec into the logical interests required to keep it
    98	    /// live. Called by the planner when the view opens; re-called on
    99	    /// recompilation triggers that the view module opts into (account-switch,
   100	    /// mailbox refresh).
   101	    fn interests(spec: &Self::Spec, ctx: &InterestContext)
   102	        -> Vec<LogicalInterest>;
   103	}
   104	```
   105	
   106	`InterestContext` exposes read access to the mailbox cache and to the active account but **not** to the relay set; the view module never names relays. That keeps `nmp-aim.md` doctrine 5 ("outbox routing automatic; manual relay selection is the opt-out, not the default") structurally enforced — there is no place for a view module to write a URL.
   107	
   108	Concrete examples for the existing seed-timeline path:
   109	
   110	- `TimelineView { authors: [pablof7z, fiatjaf, jb55, ...follows] }` returns one `LogicalInterest { shape: { authors, kinds: {1, 6}, limit: 200 }, lifecycle: Tailing }`.
   111	- `AuthorView { pubkey }` returns three interests: kind:10002 (Indexer fallback policy, see §3), kind:0 (one-shot), and `{ authors: [pubkey], kinds: {1, 6}, limit: 100 }` (Tailing).
   112	- `ProfileClaim { pubkey }` (the refcounted UI path from `crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/requests.rs:202-237`) returns one interest: `{ authors: [pubkey], kinds: {0}, limit: 1, lifecycle: OneShot }`.
   113	- `ThreadView { event_id }` returns up to two interests: `{ ids: [...] }` for context, `{ kinds: {1, 6}, tags: { #e: [...] } }` for replies.
   114	
   115	The seed-bootstrap path (`crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/requests.rs:50-106`) becomes one `LogicalInterest` per concern, registered at actor `Start` rather than emitted as raw REQs. The compiler produces the wire artifacts.
   116	
   117	### 2.3 Account scope binding
   118	
   119	The kernel `SessionState` (`docs/product-spec/subsystems.md` §7.4; M8 graduates this to multi-account) carries an active account id. `InterestScope::ActiveAccount` resolves at compile time, not at registration time. On account switch (§4 trigger A4), the compiler re-evaluates every `ActiveAccount`-scoped interest against the new active account's mailbox view. This is the structural enforcement of bug-extinction #5 (account-context overlap) referenced in [`docs/plan/m8-multi-account.md`](../../plan/m8-multi-account.md) — the kernel cannot "forget" to re-route because every plan re-derives from the active scope.
   120	
   121	Account-scoped interests with empty `authors` and empty `#p` (e.g. a free-form hashtag firehose) resolve against the active account's *read relays* (NIP-65 read side) — the user's own subscription preferences, not a globally hardcoded relay. Today's `firehose_requests()` at `crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/requests.rs:357-372` hardcodes `RelayRole::Content`; under the compiler this becomes "active-account read relays, falling back to indexer set if the active account has no kind:10002."
   122	
   123	### 2.4 What is *not* a logical interest
   124	
   125	To keep the surface small, the following are explicitly **not** logical interests:
   126	
   127	- A **wire REQ**. Wire REQs are produced by the compiler; they live in `WireSubscriptionStatus` per ADR-0007.
   128	- A **publish**. Publishes are durable actions on the action ledger (`docs/design/kernel-substrate.md` §4); they consult the `PublishPlanner` (§7) but they are not interests because they do not stay alive.
   129	- A **diagnostic record**. ADR-0007 lanes are facts derived from the planner's state, not inputs.
   130	- An **HTTP fetch** (Blossom upload, indexer JSON probe). Those are `CapabilityModule` requests.

 succeeded in 0ms:
docs/design/subscription-compilation/intro.md:10:- **Two hardcoded relays.** `crates/nmp-core/src/relay.rs:1-2` declares `CONTENT_RELAY_URL = "wss://relay.primal.net"` and `INDEXER_RELAY_URL = "wss://purplepag.es"` as module-level constants. There is no per-author routing.
docs/design/subscription-compilation/intro.md:11:- **Relay choice is a 2-variant enum, not a URL set.** `crates/nmp-core/src/relay.rs:15-39` defines `RelayRole::{Content, Indexer}` with a `.url() -> &'static str` that returns one of the two literals. This shape cannot express "this REQ should go to the union of these N authors' write relays."
docs/design/subscription-compilation/intro.md:12:- **The seam that emits REQs is parameterized by `RelayRole`.** `crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/requests.rs:530-556` (`req()`) inserts a `WireSub { role, .. }` keyed by a string sub-id and emits `OutboundMessage { role, text }`. The role *is* the routing decision; there is no relay-URL field on `WireSub` or `OutboundMessage`. Any compiler that fans an interest out across N URLs has to replace this helper.
docs/design/subscription-compilation/intro.md:13:- **Startup REQs ignore mailboxes by construction.** `crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/requests.rs:50-106` (`startup_requests`) issues six fixed REQs, each pinned to `Content` or `Indexer`. The seed-bootstrap timeline (line 65–70) fans seven hundred-author future timelines through one relay. The exit-gate test for M2 ([`docs/plan/m2-subscription-compilation.md`](../../plan/m2-subscription-compilation.md)) requires that this fan exactly equal the union of those authors' write relays.
docs/design/subscription-compilation/intro.md:14:- **View-open REQs ignore mailboxes too.** `crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/requests.rs:404-439` (`author_requests`) hardcodes a three-REQ shape — `author-relays-N` on Indexer, `author-profile-N` on Indexer, `author-notes-N` on Content. The author's notes are fetched from the global content relay even though by the time the view opens we may already have that author's kind:10002 in cache (see next bullet).
docs/design/subscription-compilation/intro.md:15:- **Mailbox cache exists but no consumer.** `crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/ingest.rs:209-233` (`ingest_relay_list`) already parses kind:10002 into `self.author_relay_lists: HashMap<String, AuthorRelayList>` (declared at `crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/mod.rs:269-275` and reserved at `mod.rs:313`). The cache is written; **nothing reads it for routing**. This is the bug doctrine D5 ("capabilities report, never decide") inverted: we have the data, we ignore it.
docs/design/subscription-compilation/intro.md:16:- **Profile claim path is single-relay.** `crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/requests.rs:390-402` (`profile_claim_request`) sends a kind:0 fetch to `RelayRole::Indexer` unconditionally. It cannot consult mailboxes for the claimed author.
docs/design/subscription-compilation/intro.md:17:- **No publish path exists yet.** `crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/requests.rs:30` (no occurrences of `EVENT` outbound) and `crates/nmp-core/src/relay.rs:42-45` (`OutboundMessage` carries only role + text). The first publish action (M6 `SendNote`) will hit this same `req()`-style seam. M2 must establish the planner shape before M6 builds the first user of it; the doctrine "no developer-supplied relays for a publish" (`docs/aim.md` §6 doctrine 5; `docs/product-spec/subsystems.md` §7.3 row "Publish leaked to wrong relays") needs a structural enforcement point.
docs/design/subscription-compilation/intro.md:19:The summary diagnosis: **the planner is a string formatter, not a compiler.** Every REQ is a per-call-site decision; routing is one of two literals; recompilation is impossible because nothing is compiled. The diagnostics in `crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/mod.rs:117-154` already type `RelayStatus` / `WireSubscriptionStatus` / `LogicalInterestStatus` per ADR-0007 — but the planner currently emits at most one `LogicalInterestStatus` per view kind because there is no logical-interest object to scope it against.
docs/design/subscription-compilation/intro.md:28:// crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/planner/interest.rs (proposed)
docs/design/subscription-compilation/intro.md:110:- `TimelineView { authors: [pablof7z, fiatjaf, jb55, ...follows] }` returns one `LogicalInterest { shape: { authors, kinds: {1, 6}, limit: 200 }, lifecycle: Tailing }`.
docs/design/subscription-compilation/intro.md:111:- `AuthorView { pubkey }` returns three interests: kind:10002 (Indexer fallback policy, see §3), kind:0 (one-shot), and `{ authors: [pubkey], kinds: {1, 6}, limit: 100 }` (Tailing).
docs/design/subscription-compilation/intro.md:112:- `ProfileClaim { pubkey }` (the refcounted UI path from `crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/requests.rs:202-237`) returns one interest: `{ authors: [pubkey], kinds: {0}, limit: 1, lifecycle: OneShot }`.
docs/design/subscription-compilation/intro.md:115:The seed-bootstrap path (`crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/requests.rs:50-106`) becomes one `LogicalInterest` per concern, registered at actor `Start` rather than emitted as raw REQs. The compiler produces the wire artifacts.
docs/design/subscription-compilation/intro.md:121:Account-scoped interests with empty `authors` and empty `#p` (e.g. a free-form hashtag firehose) resolve against the active account's *read relays* (NIP-65 read side) — the user's own subscription preferences, not a globally hardcoded relay. Today's `firehose_requests()` at `crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/requests.rs:357-372` hardcodes `RelayRole::Content`; under the compiler this becomes "active-account read relays, falling back to indexer set if the active account has no kind:10002."
docs/design/subscription-compilation/compiler.md:48:The wire-emitter (`crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/wire.rs`, to be added) diffs the new plan against the current wire-sub registry: opens new REQs, closes orphaned ones, leaves stable assignments untouched.
docs/design/subscription-compilation/compiler.md:52:Inputs: every `LogicalInterest` with non-empty `shape.authors` or non-empty `shape.tags[#p]`; the mailbox cache populated by `ingest_relay_list` (`crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/ingest.rs:209-233`).
docs/design/subscription-compilation/compiler.md:88:The indexer set is a kernel-configured `Vec<RelayUrl>` (default: a small curated list; user-configurable in `AppConfig`). Today's `crates/nmp-core/src/relay.rs:2` is the placeholder for one indexer relay (`purplepag.es`); the v1 indexer set lives in `AppConfig.indexer_relays`.
docs/design/subscription-compilation/compiler.md:170:- **Recompilation with no change ⇒ same plan-id.** If `ingest_relay_list` (`crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/ingest.rs:218-221`) deduplicates and decides not to replace a stale mailbox, no plan-id churn.
docs/design/subscription-compilation/compiler.md:181:The `plan_id` is stored on `CompiledPlan` and rendered into `LogicalInterestStatus` (extending the record at `crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/mod.rs:147-154` with `plan_id: String, plan_generation: u64`). Tests in §9 assert plan-id stability across no-op recompilations.
docs/design/subscription-compilation/compiler.md:185:This is the binding contract: each function in `crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/requests.rs` and `crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/ingest.rs` either disappears, becomes thin glue over the compiler, or graduates into a typed module. The compiler does not coexist with the old planner; M2 replaces it.
docs/design/subscription-compilation/compiler.md:189:| `startup_requests` (requests.rs:50-106) | **Relocated outside `nmp-core` per D0.** The seed timeline / kind:0 / kind:10002 / kind:3 bootstrap is social graph knowledge — not kernel substrate. It moves to `nmp-nip01` / `nmp-nip02` / the demo app's startup sequence. `nmp-core` only executes interests registered by modules; it does not hard-code which interests those are. The TEST_PUBKEY-specific bootstrap (line 71-82) moves to the demo app (or a test fixture in `nmp-testing`). The kernel's `ActorStart` handler calls `compiler.recompile(Trigger::Startup)`, which is a no-op until a module registers interests. |
docs/design/subscription-compilation/compiler.md:190:| `open_author` (requests.rs:118-140) | Registers three `LogicalInterest`s scoped `ActiveAccount` (kind:10002, kind:0, kinds:1+6 for author); calls `compiler.recompile(Trigger::ViewOpen)`. Refcount stays — but it lives on `InterestId` now, not on `ViewInterest { key, refcount }`. The `can_send` gate disappears: the compiler always produces a plan; the wire-emitter is the only thing that may queue deferrals. |
docs/design/subscription-compilation/compiler.md:193:| `claim_profile` / `release_profile` (requests.rs:202-263) | Registers/unregisters one `LogicalInterest { shape: { authors: [pk], kinds: [0], limit: 1 }, lifecycle: OneShot }` per claim. Refcount of distinct consumers becomes the `InterestId` claim set inside the registry. **Dedup of (pk, kinds=[0]) across N timeline rows yields one merged SubShape and one wire REQ** — this is what bug-extinction "1000 avatars do not produce 1000 REQs" verifies. The refcount must be an integer counter, not a boolean presence flag — Applesauce gotcha `75ef7d5f` (`docs/research/applesauce/gotchas.md` §G2) shows the boolean model causes leaks when two consumers share a claim and one unsubscribes. |
docs/design/subscription-compilation/compiler.md:200:| `author_requests` (requests.rs:404-439) | Disappears (replaced by `open_author`'s interest registration). |
docs/design/subscription-compilation/compiler.md:207:| `maybe_open_timeline` (ingest.rs:329-365) | The "seed-contacts arrive → open union timeline" logic moves to `nmp-nip02` (follows/contacts module), not to an `nmp-core` helper. D0: `nmp-core` must not know the social graph. The contacts module watches the kind:3 projection and registers a `Timeline { authors: union }` interest on behalf of whichever view asked for it. |
docs/design/subscription-compilation/compiler.md:216:  into `nmp-core` — those are module concerns (D0). The impl PR for M2 must register those
docs/design/subscription-compilation/compiler.md:217:  interests from `nmp-nip01`/`nmp-nip02` or the demo app, not from the kernel actor directly.
docs/design/subscription-compilation/compiler.md:219:The compiler is **in-memory v1** by design. The mailbox cache is the existing `HashMap<String, AuthorRelayList>` (`crates/nmp-core/src/kernel/mod.rs:313`); it just gets a new consumer.
docs/design/kernel-substrate.md:13:`nmp-core` defines five trait families. Each extension crate implements one or more of them. The kernel runtime knows nothing about a module's specific types — only that the module conforms to these traits and contributes variants to the generated per-app enums (per ADR-0010).
docs/design/kernel-substrate.md:23:Modules typically implement several. A protocol module (`nmp-nip01`) implements `ViewModule` (Profile, raw events) + `ActionModule` (publish a kind-1). An app module (`twitter-core`) implements `DomainModule` (drafts, settings) + `ViewModule` (compose state) + `ActionModule` (compose-and-publish).
docs/design/kernel-substrate.md:115:The successor to the closed `ViewSpec` enum. Every view kind — kernel-Nostr (Profile, Timeline, Thread, ...), protocol-NIP (NIP-29 group state, NIP-17 conversation), or app-specific (Twitter compose, Highlighter capture queue) — is a `ViewModule`.
docs/design/kernel-substrate.md:242:// crates/nmp-nip01/src/actions/send_note.rs
docs/design/kernel-substrate.md:364:  kernel: nmp-core
docs/design/kernel-substrate.md:365:  protocol: nmp-nip01, nmp-nip02, nmp-nip10, nmp-nip25, nmp-nip65
docs/design/kernel-substrate.md:370:  src/view_spec.rs ✓ (8 view kinds: 4 from nmp-nip01, 1 each from nmp-nip02 / nmp-nip25 / nmp-nip65 / twitter-core)
docs/design/kernel-substrate.md:390:- **Views can read projections from other modules.** Projection caches are kernel-owned (per `reactivity.md` §6); any module can read them. This is how a Timeline view (in `nmp-nip01`) reads author-display projections that come from kind:0 inserts (also in `nmp-nip01`).
docs/design/kernel-substrate.md:448:- `nmp-nip01`: Event types, Filter, Profile / Contacts / Timeline view modules, SendNote / DeleteEvent actions.
docs/design/kernel-substrate.md:449:- `nmp-nip02`: Contacts module (re-exported for convenience; structure overlaps with nip01).
docs/design/kernel-substrate.md:484:2. Phase 1a.2 onward (Twitter clone) implements the demo entirely as extension modules with no `nmp-core` patches needed.
docs/design/kernel-substrate.md:485:3. A future Highlighter-lite, TENEX-lite, or podcast-lite module can be added without changes to `nmp-core` traits. Demonstrated on paper for v1; demonstrated in code post-v1.
docs/design/app-extension-kernel.md:21:The lesson is not that these nouns belong in `nmp-core`. The lesson is that NMP must provide a kernel where app crates can define those nouns safely, durably, observably, and with generated platform bindings while still obeying the "no native business logic" rule.
docs/design/app-extension-kernel.md:52:> If implementing Highlighter, TENEX, Win the Day, Cut Tracker, or Podcast requires adding app nouns to `nmp-core`, the extension boundary is wrong.
docs/design/app-extension-kernel.md:60:| `nmp-core` kernel | actor, event store, planner, ledger, domain-store traits, view/action/capability registries, diagnostics | No |
docs/design/app-extension-kernel.md:69:- `nmp-core` should know neither.
docs/design/app-extension-kernel.md:213:This supports app-local agents, feedback identities, human accounts, external signers, and provider credential owners without adding "agent" or "feedback" semantics to `nmp-core`.
docs/design/app-extension-kernel.md:244:Generated code is allowed to know app nouns because it is generated for that app. `nmp-core` is not.
docs/design/app-extension-kernel.md:248:These concepts should not be added to `nmp-core`:
docs/design/app-extension-kernel.md:284:Pass condition: no `Room`, `Highlight`, `Artifact`, or `CaptureDraft` nouns are added to `nmp-core`. Only reusable protocol modules may contain NIP-specific nouns.
docs/design/app-extension-kernel.md:348:5. A small fixture app module that proves the platform shell has no business logic and `nmp-core` has no app nouns.

- [outbox.md](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/docs/design/subscription-compilation/outbox.md:76): `PublishPrivacy::Public` still says it “falls back to indexer set.” That directly contradicts D3 and the new no-`indexer_set` planner shape. Fix the comment to fail with `NoAuthorRelays`.

- [diagnostics.md](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/docs/design/subscription-compilation/diagnostics.md:137), [tests.md](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/docs/design/subscription-compilation/tests.md:151), [outbox.md](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/docs/design/subscription-compilation/outbox.md:154), [compiler.md](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/docs/design/subscription-compilation/compiler.md:84): stale `RoutingSource::Indexer` / `Indexer` source remains, despite [compiler.md](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/docs/design/subscription-compilation/compiler.md:61) saying there is no Indexer variant. Fix to `RoutingSource::UserConfigured` + `UserConfiguredCategory::Indexer`, or check empty/unroutable inboxes.

- [intro.md](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/docs/design/subscription-compilation/intro.md:115): still says seed-bootstrap interests are registered at actor `Start`; [compiler.md](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/docs/design/subscription-compilation/compiler.md:189) says they moved out of `nmp-core`. D0 wants the compiler version. Align intro, and make module ownership explicit for `open_author` / profile claims.

- [tests.md](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/docs/design/subscription-compilation/tests.md:40) vs [tests.md](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/docs/design/subscription-compilation/tests.md:288): one says the M2 audit test is `#[ignore]` until codegen lands; the other says it must never be skipped or ignored. Pick one. For an exit gate, `AppActionMeta` should be a hard M2 deliverable.

- [nip65.md](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/docs/design/subscription-compilation/nip65.md:27): file tree still says `cache.rs` owns the `MailboxCache` trait, while [nip65.md](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/docs/design/subscription-compilation/nip65.md:133) says the trait lives in `nmp-core`. Fix the tree to avoid reintroducing the crate-cycle issue.

- [compiler.md](/Users/pablofernandez/Work/nostr-multi-platform/docs/design/subscription-compilation/compiler.md:124): references “open question 8,” but the parent list has only 1–7. Either add the ADR item deliberately or remove the dangling new open question.

Other checks: no `TODO`, `FIXME`, or `unimplemented` in the touched docs. File sizes are fine: largest is `tests.md` at 290 LOC, under the 300 soft limit and 500 hard ceiling.
